english:
[pagebreak]

The Old Ways by Celarus the Loremaster

[pagebreak]

e who know the Old Ways are well aware of the existence of a spiritual world invisible to the unenlightened. Just as one living in a kingdom but unaware of the political machinations underneath may see a new tax or battle preparation as the caprices of fortune, many observe floods, famines, and madness with helpless incomprehension. This is deplorable. As the great Cuilean Darnizhaan moaned, "The power of ignorance can shatter ebony like glass." What, after all, is the origin of these spiritual forces that move the invisible strings of Mundus? Any neophyte of Artaeum knows that these spirits are our ancestors -- and that, while living, they too were bewildered by the spirits of their ancestors, and so on back to the original Acharyai. The Daedra and gods to whom the common people turn are no more than the spirits of superior men and women whose power and passion granted them great influence in the afterworld. Certainly this is our truth and our religion. But how does it help us in our sacred duty of seliffrnsae, or providing "grave and faithful counsel" to lesser men? Primarily, it is easy to grasp the necessity both of endowing good men with great power and making powerful men good. We recognize the multiple threats that a strong tyrant represents -- breeds cruelty which feeds the Daedra Boethiah and hatred which feeds the Daedra Vaernima; if he should die having performed a particularly malevolent act, he may go to rule in Oblivion; and worst of all, he inspires other villains to thirst after power and other rulers to embrace villainy. Knowing this, we have developed patience in our dealings with such despots. They should be crippled, humiliated, impoverished, imprisoned. Other counsellors may advocate assassination or warfare -- which, aside from its spiritual insignificance, is expensive and likely to inflict at least as much pain on the innocents as the brutish dictator. No, we are intelligence gatherers, dignified diplomats -- not revolutionaries. How, then, are our counsellors "faithful"? We are faithful only to the Old Ways -- it is essential always to remember the spiritual world while keeping our eyes open in the physical one. Performing the Rites of Moawita on the 2nd of Hearth Fire and the Vigyld on the 1st of Second Seed are essential means of empowering salutary spirits and debilitating unclean ones. How, then, are we at once faithful to those we counsel and to the Isle of Artaeum? Perhaps the sage Taheritae said it best: "In Mundus, conflict and disparity are what bring change, and change is the most sacred of the Eleven Forces. Change is the force without focus or origin. It is the duty of the disciplined Psijic ["Enlightened One"] to dilute change where it brings greed, gluttony, sloth, ignorance, prejudice, cruelty... [here Taheritae lists the rest of the 111 Prodigalities], and to encourage change where it brings excellence, beauty, happiness, and enlightenment. As such, the faithful counsel has but one master: His mind. If the man the Psijic counsels acts wickedly and brings oegnithr ["bad change"] and will otherwise not be counselled, it is the Psijic's duty to counterbalance the oegnithr by any means necessary [emphasis mine]." A student of the Old Ways may indeed ally himself to a lord -- but it is a risky relationship. It cannot be stressed enough that the choice be wisely made. Should the lord refuse wise counsel and order the Psijic (to use Taheritae's outmoded word) to perform an act contrary to the teachings of the Old Ways, there are few available options. The Psijic may obey, albeit unwillingly, and fall prey to the dark forces against which he has devoted his life. The Psijic may abandon his lord, which will bring shame on him and the Isle of Artaeum, and so may never be allowed home again. Or the Psijic may simply kill himself.
chinese:
[pagebreak]

古道 博学大师凯勒瑞斯 著

[pagebreak]

我等通晓古道之人，皆深知未蒙启迪者目不得见的精神世界确实存在。正如身居王国却不觉其背后政治阴谋之人，可能将征税或备战视作命运变幻无常，而泛泛之众面临洪涝、饥荒和疯狂之时，亦觉茫然无助。此诚可悲可叹。正似伟大的奎连·达尔尼扎安所怨诉那般，“无知之力，足可如粉碎玻璃般粉碎黑檀。” 归根结底，拨动梦达思无形之弦的精魂力量，其起源究竟为何？任何阿塔姆的新人皆知，这些精魂为我等先祖——且先祖在日，彼等亦为其先祖灵魂所惑，而如此一直上溯至源初的阿查赖。普罗大众所求助的迪德拉与诸神，不外乎是拜力量与激情所赐而在来世取得巨大影响力的卓越者的精魂罢了。 毋庸置疑，此即我等所奉真理，我等所抱信仰。但其又如何相助于我等塞黎夫尔尼的神圣职责，为平凡者提供“严肃而忠实的劝告”呢？ 首先，赋予称义者以权势和令拥权者称义的必要性不必多言。我们深知强大的暴君意味着什么威胁--彼所滋生残暴邪行，温养迪德拉波耶西亚；彼所激发憎恶仇恨，助长迪德拉瓦尔迷纳；若他于崩殂前曾作惨绝人寰之举，则他或将握权于湮灭。最为可怖的，乃是他将鼓动其他恶徒渴求权柄，并令其他治人者欣然拥抱罪恶。正因如此，我等便在与暴君打交道之时自有耐心之法。彼等应受致残瘸瘫、辱骂唾弃、一贫如洗、陷身牢狱之苦。其他顾问乐得倡导暗杀或战争——然此不仅于精神上无足轻重，且代价高昂，对无辜大众造成痛苦并未少于暴虐独夫。非也，我等为集智之人、高贵的外交官——而非革命者。 如此，我等顾问又如何能“忠诚”呢？我等仅忠于古道--身在物质界，心念精神界，这至关重要。于炉火月2日所行莫瓦塔仪式，和于次种月首日所行的维吉尔德仪式，是裨益灵魂、涤除邪祟的重要手段。如此，我等又如何既忠于我们谏言之人，又忠于阿塔姆呢？贤者塔赫黎特所言最是贴切：“于梦达思，冲突与差异带来变化，而变化正是十一力中至圣者。变化乃无焦无源之力。戒律严明的赛伊克 （“通晓者”） 之职责，即稀释诸般招致贪婪、暴食、懒惰、无知、偏见、残忍……（于此，塔赫黎特列举111种罪业）的变化，而鼓励倡导引致卓越、美丽、幸福及启迪的变化。因而，忠实的顾问只有一主：自己的思想。如若赛伊克所建言者品行不端、招致奥格尼瑟（“坏变化”），则不可继续进言，赛伊克有责以任何必要之手段来制衡奥格尼瑟（此为我等关键职责）。” 古道门生自可与君王缔盟——但这是危险之举。身为赛伊克应当明智抉择，这一信条再怎么强调也不为过。但如若君王拒绝明谏，又命赛伊克（以塔赫黎特所用陈词旧句而言之）行背逆古道教义之举，则只能让赛伊克陷入两难之境。赛伊克虽觉违心，或将从之，从此堕入黑暗并放弃坚持一生的信仰。或背主而去，使其与阿塔姆岛蒙羞，因而被永远驱逐。或干脆自刎了却。

我等通晓古道之人，皆深知未蒙启迪者目不得见的精神世界确实存在。正如身居王国却不觉其背后政治阴谋之人，可能将征税或备战视作命运变幻无常，而泛泛之众面临洪涝、饥荒和疯狂之时，亦觉茫然无助。此诚可悲可叹。正似伟大的奎连·达尔尼扎安所怨诉那般，“无知之力，足可如粉碎玻璃般粉碎黑檀。” 归根结底，拨动梦达思无形之弦的精魂力量，其起源究竟为何？任何阿塔姆的新人皆知，这些精魂为我等先祖——且先祖在日，彼等亦为其先祖灵魂所惑，而如此一直上溯至源初的阿查赖。普罗大众所求助的迪德拉与诸神，不外乎是拜力量与激情所赐而在来世取得巨大影响力的卓越者的精魂罢了。 毋庸置疑，此即我等所奉真理，我等所抱信仰。但其又如何相助于我等塞黎夫尔尼的神圣职责，为平凡者提供“严肃而忠实的劝告”呢？ 首先，赋予称义者以权势和令拥权者称义的必要性不必多言。我们深知强大的暴君意味着什么威胁--彼所滋生残暴邪行，温养迪德拉波耶西亚；彼所激发憎恶仇恨，助长迪德拉瓦尔迷纳；若他于崩殂前曾作惨绝人寰之举，则他或将握权于湮灭。最为可怖的，乃是他将鼓动其他恶徒渴求权柄，并令其他治人者欣然拥抱罪恶。正因如此，我等便在与暴君打交道之时自有耐心之法。彼等应受致残瘸瘫、辱骂唾弃、一贫如洗、陷身牢狱之苦。其他顾问乐得倡导暗杀或战争——然此不仅于精神上无足轻重，且代价高昂，对无辜大众造成痛苦并未少于暴虐独夫。非也，我等为集智之人、高贵的外交官——而非革命者。 如此，我等顾问又如何能“忠诚”呢？我等仅忠于古道--身在物质界，心念精神界，这至关重要。于炉火月2日所行莫瓦塔仪式，和于次种月首日所行的维吉尔德仪式，是裨益灵魂、涤除邪祟的重要手段。如此，我等又如何既忠于我们谏言之人，又忠于阿塔姆呢？贤者塔赫黎特所言最是贴切：“于梦达思，冲突与差异带来变化，而变化正是十一力中至圣者。变化乃无焦无源之力。戒律严明的赛伊克 （“通晓者”） 之职责，即稀释诸般招致贪婪、暴食、懒惰、无知、偏见、残忍……（于此，塔赫黎特列举111种罪业）的变化，而鼓励倡导引致卓越、美丽、幸福及启迪的变化。因而，忠实的顾问只有一主：自己的思想。如若赛伊克所建言者品行不端、招致奥格尼瑟（“坏变化”），则不可继续进言，赛伊克有责以任何必要之手段来制衡奥格尼瑟（此为我等关键职责）。” 古道门生自可与君王缔盟——但这是危险之举。身为赛伊克应当明智抉择，这一信条再怎么强调也不为过。但如若君王拒绝明谏，又命赛伊克（以塔赫黎特所用陈词旧句而言之）行背逆古道教义之举，则只能让赛伊克陷入两难之境。赛伊克虽觉违心，或将从之，从此堕入黑暗并放弃坚持一生的信仰。或背主而去，使其与阿塔姆岛蒙羞，因而被永远驱逐。或干脆自刎了却。
agentText:
Addon: Skyrim EDID: Book4RareOldWays Record: BOOK:DESC SID: 0042D4 English: [pagebreak]

The Old Ways by Celarus the Loremaster

[pagebreak]

e who know the Old Ways are well aware of the existence of a spiritual world invisible to the unenlightened. Just as one living in a kingdom but unaware of the political machinations underneath may see a new tax or battle preparation as the caprices of fortune, many observe floods, famines, and madness with helpless incomprehension. This is deplorable. As the great Cuilean Darnizhaan moaned, "The power of ignorance can shatter ebony like glass." What, after all, is the origin of these spiritual forces that move the invisible strings of Mundus? Any neophyte of Artaeum knows that these spirits are our ancestors -- and that, while living, they too were bewildered by the spirits of their ancestors, and so on back to the original Acharyai. The Daedra and gods to whom the common people turn are no more than the spirits of superior men and women whose power and passion granted them great influence in the afterworld. Certainly this is our truth and our religion. But how does it help us in our sacred duty of seliffrnsae, or providing "grave and faithful counsel" to lesser men? Primarily, it is easy to grasp the necessity both of endowing good men with great power and making powerful men good. We recognize the multiple threats that a strong tyrant represents -- breeds cruelty which feeds the Daedra Boethiah and hatred which feeds the Daedra Vaernima; if he should die having performed a particularly malevolent act, he may go to rule in Oblivion; and worst of all, he inspires other villains to thirst after power and other rulers to embrace villainy. Knowing this, we have developed patience in our dealings with such despots. They should be crippled, humiliated, impoverished, imprisoned. Other counsellors may advocate assassination or warfare -- which, aside from its spiritual insignificance, is expensive and likely to inflict at least as much pain on the innocents as the brutish dictator. No, we are intelligence gatherers, dignified diplomats -- not revolutionaries. How, then, are our counsellors "faithful"? We are faithful only to the Old Ways -- it is essential always to remember the spiritual world while keeping our eyes open in the physical one. Performing the Rites of Moawita on the 2nd of Hearth Fire and the Vigyld on the 1st of Second Seed are essential means of empowering salutary spirits and debilitating unclean ones. How, then, are we at once faithful to those we counsel and to the Isle of Artaeum? Perhaps the sage Taheritae said it best: "In Mundus, conflict and disparity are what bring change, and change is the most sacred of the Eleven Forces. Change is the force without focus or origin. It is the duty of the disciplined Psijic ["Enlightened One"] to dilute change where it brings greed, gluttony, sloth, ignorance, prejudice, cruelty... [here Taheritae lists the rest of the 111 Prodigalities], and to encourage change where it brings excellence, beauty, happiness, and enlightenment. As such, the faithful counsel has but one master: His mind. If the man the Psijic counsels acts wickedly and brings oegnithr ["bad change"] and will otherwise not be counselled, it is the Psijic's duty to counterbalance the oegnithr by any means necessary [emphasis mine]." A student of the Old Ways may indeed ally himself to a lord -- but it is a risky relationship. It cannot be stressed enough that the choice be wisely made. Should the lord refuse wise counsel and order the Psijic (to use Taheritae's outmoded word) to perform an act contrary to the teachings of the Old Ways, there are few available options. The Psijic may obey, albeit unwillingly, and fall prey to the dark forces against which he has devoted his life. The Psijic may abandon his lord, which will bring shame on him and the Isle of Artaeum, and so may never be allowed home again. Or the Psijic may simply kill himself. Chinese: [pagebreak]

[pagebreak]

english: 18th of Evening Star, 4E 186 As I begin this new phase of my life, I have decided to finally keep a journal. So much has happened to me thus far, both within the Brotherhood and without - when I think there is no record of what has transpired, it almost seems an affront to Sithis himself. So I am determined to make amends. Yes, the Dark Brotherhood has its own scribes and chroniclers, but it is their solemn task to record those events deemed important to the organization as a whole. Let this volume serve as the personal record of one man, a lowly assassin who has pledged his blade and his life for the Dark Brotherhood. 23rd of Evening Star, 4E 186 I have arrived safely in the Cheydinhal Sanctuary, and have been greeted warmly by Rasha and the others. Indeed, the level of support and acceptance shown by my new family is rather overwhelming. For this Sanctuary knows suffering, knows sorrow, for the ghosts of Purification still haunt its halls. So, who better to understand the plight of a brother who has lost home and heart? Who better to comfort one whose Sanctuary is no more? The Bruma Sanctuary may be gone, but my dearest brothers and sisters will live forever in my dreams, just as their souls live forever by the Dread Father's side. 1st of Rain's Hand, 4E 187 Completed the baroness contract. She died well. Her handmaiden, less so. 12th of Rain's Hand, 4E 187 Cheydinhal suits me. With the destruction or abandonment of the other Sanctuaries, our contracts are plentiful, as are our bonuses. Still, we seem to be losing our footholds throughout Tamriel at an alarming rate. There are rumors that the Black Hand is split on our continued direction. Some favor expansion, the others consolidation. My personal feeling is that the Dark Brotherhood needs to, at the very least, maintain the illusion of being everywhere at once. It has become exceedingly difficult to fulfill (or even establish) contracts in provinces where we no longer have a physical presence, like Hammerfell. The more we ignore Tamriel, the more people lose faith in the Dark Brotherhood - our power, our services, our dedication to the Void. 27th of Rain's Hand, 4E 187 The Listener, Alisanne Dupre, has been visiting with us for several days, down from her private residence in Bravil. She and Rasha had been discussing the possibility of re-opening the Shadowscale training facility of Archon, in Black Marsh, but ultimately decided we lacked the resources to follow through with the plan. 27th of Rain's Hand, 4E 187 Completed the Arena contract. I ultimately decided to pose as a starstruck fan, and immediately got into the Grand Champion's good graces. While escorting the arrogant fool through the Great Forest, I slashed his throat and left the corpse for the bears.
chinese: 4E 186年，夜星月18日 随着我的生活进入了新阶段，我终于下定决心写日记了。到目前为止，我经历过了很多兄弟会里里外外的事情--如果我觉得无事可记的话，那就几乎是对西帝斯本尊的冒犯了。因此我决心做出弥补。 黑暗兄弟会是有自己的抄写员和记录人，不过他们的庄严任务是记录那些对整个组织而言意义重大的事件。就让这卷日记成为一个卑微的刺客，一个为黑暗兄弟会献出他的刀剑以及生命的人的私人记录吧。 4E 186年，夜星月23日 我已经安全抵达了夏丁赫尔圣所，受到了拉沙以及其他人的热烈欢迎。确实，我的新家庭对我的支持和接受程度相当高。因为这座圣所仍然记得痛苦，记得哀伤，被净化的幽灵们仍旧徘徊在这间大厅。那怎能不理解一位失去家和心灵的兄弟的困境呢？又怎能不给自己圣所已然不复存在的人以安慰呢？ 布鲁玛圣所也许消失了，然而我最亲爱的兄弟姐妹们会永远活在我的梦中，就如同他们的灵魂永远与恐惧之父同在一样。 4E 187年，雨手月1日 刺杀男爵夫人的契约完成了。她死得很干脆。但她的女仆就没这么舒服了。 4E 187年 雨手月12日 夏丁赫尔很适合我。随着其他圣所被摧毁和被抛弃，我们的契约和酬金越来越多。 然而，我们似乎正在以惊人的速度失去在整个泰姆瑞尔的据点。有传言说黑手就未来发展方向上出现了分歧。有人支持扩张，而其余人支持巩固整合。 我个人的想法是黑暗兄弟会至少需要维持一种无所不在的错觉。在我们不具有实际组织存在的省份，比如落锤，履行（甚至是签订）契约都变得很困难。我们越是忽视泰姆瑞尔，就会有越多人对黑暗兄弟会，对我们的力量、我们的服务、我们对于虚空的决心，失去信任。 4E 187年，雨手月27日 聆听者阿蕾萨妮·杜普雷从她在布拉维尔的私人居所来拜访了我们几天。她跟拉沙讨论要重启位于黑沼泽亚坎城的影鳞训练课程，但最终认定我们缺乏足够的资源来执行这一计划。 4E 187年，雨手月27日 竞技场的契约完成了。我最终决定扮演一位追星迷，并立即得到了总冠军的青睐。在护送这个目中无人的蠢蛋经过大森林时，我割断了他的喉咙，然后把他的尸体留下喂熊了。

HALLGERD'S TALE by Tavi Dromio

[pagebreak] [pagebreak]

think the greatest warrior who ever lived had to be Vilus Nommenus," offered Xiomara. "Name one other warrior who conquered more territory." "Tiber Septim, obviously," said Hallgerd. "He wasn't a warrior. He was an administrator... a politician," said Garaz. "And besides, acreage conquered can't be final means of determining the best warrior. How about skill with a blade?" "There are other weapons than blades," objected Xiomara. "Why not skill with an axe or a bow? Who was the greatest master of all weaponry?" "I can't think of one greatest master of all weaponry," said Hallgerd. "Balaxes of Agia Nero in Black Marsh was the greatest wielder of a lance. Ernse Llervu of the Ashlands is the greatest master of the club I've ever seen. The greatest master of the katana is probably an Akaviri warlord we've never heard of. As far as archery goes --" "Pelinal Whitestrake supposedly conquered all of Tamriel by himself," interrupted Xiomara. "That was before the First Era," said Garaz. "It's probably mostly myth. But there are all sorts of great warriors of the modern eras. The Camoran Usurper? The unknown hero who brought together the Staff of Chaos and defeated Jagar Tharn?" "We can't declare an unknown champion as the greatest warrior. What about Nandor Beraid, the Empress Katariah's champion?" suggested Xiomara. "They said he could use any weapon ever invented." "But what happened to him?" smiled Garaz. "He was drowned in the Sea of Ghosts because he couldn't get his armor off. Call me overly particular, but I think the greatest warrior in the world should know how to take armor off." "It's kinda hard to judge ability to wear armor as a skill," said Xiomara. "Either you have basic functionality in a suit of armor or you don't." "That's not true," said Hallgerd. "There are masters in that as well, people who can do things while wearing armor better than we can out of armor. Have you ever heard of Hlaalu Pasoroth, the King's great grandfather?" Xiomara and Garaz admitted that they had not. "This was hundreds and hundreds of years ago, and Pasoroth was the ruler of a great estate which he had won by right of being the greatest warrior in the land. It's been said, and truly, that much of the House's current power is based on Pasoroth's earnings as a warrior. Every week he held games at his castle, pitting his skill against the champions of the neighboring estates, and every week, he won something. His great skill wasn't in the use of weaponry, though he was decent enough with an axe and a long sword, but in his ability to move quickly and with great agility wearing a full suit of heavy mail. There were some who said that he moved faster while wearing armor than he did out of it. "Some months before this story begins, he had won the daughter of one of his neighbors, a beautiful creature named Mena who he had made his wife. He loved her very much, but he was intensely jealous, and with good reason. She wasn't very pleased with his husbandly skills, and the only reason Mena never strayed was because Pasoroth kept a close eye on her. She was, to put it kindly, naturally amorous and resentful of her position as a prize. Wherever he went, he always brought her with him. At the games, she was placed in a special box so that he could see her even while he competed. "But his real competition, though he didn't know it, was from a handsome young armorer he also had won at one of his competitions. Mena had noticed him, and the armorer, whose name was Taren, had certainly noticed her." "This has all the makings of a dirty joke, Hallgerd," said Xiomara, with a smile. "I swear that it's entirely true," said Hallgerd. "The problem facing the lovers was, of course, that they could never be alone. Perhaps because of this, it became a burning obsession to both of them. Taren decided that the best time for them to consummate their love was during the games. Mena feigned illness, so she didn't have to stay in the box, but Pasoroth visited the sickroom every few minutes between fights, so Taren and Mena could never get together. The sound of Pasoroth's armor clunking up the stairs to visit his sick wife gave Taren the idea. "He crafted his lord a new suit of armor, strong, and bright, and beautifully decorated. For his purposes, Taren rubbed the leg joints with luca dust so the more he sweated and the more he moved them, the more they'd stick together. After a little while, Taren figured, Pasoroth wouldn't be able to walk very quickly, and wouldn't have enough time in between fights to visit his wife. But just in case, Taren also added bells to the legs which rung loudly when they moved, so the couple would be able to hear him coming in plenty of time. "When the games commenced the following week, Mena feigned illness again and Taren presented his lord with the new armor. Pasoroth was delighted with it, as Taren hoped he would be, and donned it for his first fight. Taren then stole upstairs to Mena's bedchamber. "All was silent outside as the two began to make love. Suddenly, Mena noticed a peculiar expression on Taren's face and before she had a chance to ask him about it, his head fell off at the neck. Pasoroth was standing behind him with his axe in hand." "How did he get upstairs so quickly, with his leg joints gummed up? And didn't they hear the bells ringing?" asked Garaz. "Well, you see, when Pasoroth realized he couldn't walk on his legs very quickly, he walked on his hands." "I don't believe it," laughed Xiomara. "What happened next?" asked Garaz. "Did Pasoroth kill Mena also?" "No one knows exactly what happened next," said Hallgerd. "Pasoroth didn't return for the next game, nor for the next. Finally, at the fourth game, he returned to fight, and Mena appeared in the box to watch. She didn't appear to be sick anymore. In fact, she was smiling and had a light flush to her face." "They did it?" cried Xiomara. "I don't have all the salacious details, except that after the battle, it took ten squires thirteen hours to get Pasoroth's armor off because of all the luca dust mixed with sweat." "I don't understand, you mean, he didn't take his armor off when they -- but how?" "Like I said," replied Hallgerd. "This is a story about someone who was more agile and accomplished in his armor than out of it." "Now, that's skill," said Garaz.

哈尔杰德讲故事 塔维·德罗米奥 著

[pagebreak] [pagebreak]

“我觉得有史以来最伟大的战士一定是维鲁斯·诺梅努斯啦，”肖玛拉提名一人，“还有哪个战士夺取的疆域比他更多呢，提上名来吧。” “显然是泰伯·塞普汀么。”哈尔杰德说。 “他不是战士，他是个行政官……政治家啊。”加拉兹说，“再说，征服土地大小可不能作为选定最伟大战士的终极标准。你们看剑技如何呀？” “除了剑还有别的那么多武器啊，”肖玛拉反驳，“那为什么不说战斧或箭术咧？谁又是精通所有武器的大宗师呢？” “我可想不到谁是所有武器全通的大宗师，”哈尔杰德说，“黑沼泽埃吉亚·尼禄的巴拉克斯堪称枪中凶徒。灰地的厄恩斯·乐乌是我见过最强的棍棒手。若论打刀，那就该是某位咱们闻所未闻的阿卡维尔将军。至于箭术嘛——” “传说‘白轮’佩林纳尔只是单枪匹马就征服了泰姆瑞尔全境噢。”肖玛拉插嘴打断他。 “那都是第一纪元之前了，”加拉兹说，“有可能只是段神话而已。现代的伟大战士数不胜数。卡莫然篡位者又怎样呢？还有那个重组混沌之杖还击破了贾伽·萨恩的无名英雄嘛？” “咱把一位无名勇士称作至高战士明显有失偏颇吧。你们怎么看卡塔瑞亚女王的勇士南多尔·贝莱德？”肖玛拉再度提名，“据说他能驾驭任何已经被发明出来的武器啊。” “而他的下场呢？”加拉兹笑了，“他因为无法卸甲而溺死在幽灵海。你们大可说我吹毛求疵，但我觉得，世界上最伟大的战士最起码该知道如何脱下盔甲吧。” “要是把着甲视为一种技能来判定一位战士的高低，那可太难了吧，”肖玛拉说，“盔甲只有有用没用之分么。” “话别说死哦，”哈尔杰德说，“也有精于此道的大师，同样是穿着盔甲，人家做事情就比咱们有效率。你们有没有听说过国王的祖先，哈拉鲁·帕索罗斯啊？” 肖玛拉和加拉兹都坦言并不认识。 “那都是几百年前的事了，帕索罗斯是一大块封邑的统治者，这所庄园就是他作为这片土地最强战士赢来的。据说啊，而且也是事实吧，整个家族当今的权势呀，都是建立在帕索罗斯的战士生涯所积累资源的基础上喔。帕索罗斯每周都在他城堡里头搞比赛，跟周围领地的勇士们比试，不过他每回都能赢走些什么。 他最厉害的地方可不是耍武器，虽然他剑法斧法也还说得过去。但他全身给重甲包个严严实实的时侯，一举一动照样灵动敏捷得很，甚至有些传言说他着甲的时候比不穿盔甲行动更快。 “这故事开始前的几个月呢，他把附近某个邻邦的女儿给赢到了手。那叫米娜的女孩长得标致得很，帕索罗斯顺势娶她当了老婆。他非常疼爱妻子，却又十分猜忌她。米娜对丈夫的'技能'不怎么满意，而她还没出轨的唯一原因就是帕索罗斯老把她盯个死死的。说好听点，她身处这种被宠爱的地位，难免恃宠而骄，变得多情而不满。帕索罗斯不管上哪去都带着米娜。比武时，他就把她放在一间特制的箱子里头，这样即使时比赛时也能看到他。 “不过帕索罗斯可不知道，他最大的竞争威胁来自一个帅气军械师，这位小白脸也是他在比武里赢到的。米娜早留心他了，而那位叫泰伦的军械师自然也留心着她咯。” “哈尔杰德啊，这不就是个荤段子嘛。”肖玛拉笑道。 “我发誓确有其事，”哈尔杰德说，“情人们面临的问题嘛，毋庸置疑了，是他们永远没法私会。也许正因如此，这俩人反而有了一种强烈的痴迷。泰伦觉着他们交流爱情的良机呀，那就是在比武期间。米娜诈病就用不着给关在那箱房里头，但帕索罗斯在比武的时候隔个几分钟就要去探探病，叫这泰伦和米娜一直没法二人世界。不过，帕索罗斯每次探病上楼时甲叶叮当的声音让泰伦心生一计。 “他好好地给主子新打了一身强韧、光洁而且饰纹瑰丽的甲胄。出于他那目的，泰伦用卢卡灰擦抹护腿甲关节榫位，所以帕索罗斯运动越多冒汗越多，这地方就黏得越紧。过了一阵子，泰伦估计帕索罗斯肯定没法快走了，在比武两次战斗之间的空当也不够他去找老婆去了。但为了以防万一嘛，泰伦还在护腿甲上加了些铃铛，只要一动铃铛就铛铛作响。有了这个警报，奸夫淫妇就能在帕索罗斯过来之前有足够的时间提前听到他的到来。 “比武开始的第二周，米娜又装起病来。泰伦则给他主子献上新盔甲。如他所愿，帕索罗斯一见就爱不释手，还在第一战就穿上它了。泰伦随即摸上楼去了米娜的房间。 “外头静悄悄的，两人马上开始翻云覆雨。突然，米娜注意到泰伦表情异样，不等她开口问，奸夫就已经身首异处了。帕索罗斯拎着手斧正站在他后头呢。” “他怎么这么快就上楼了，护腿甲关节不是已经粘住了吗？难不成他们没听到铃声？”加拉兹问道。 “嗯，因为，当帕索罗斯发现他没法靠腿快走的时候，他就改用手走路了。” “我可不信。”肖玛拉大笑起来。 “然后呢？”加拉兹问，“帕索罗斯把米娜也剁了？” “天知道后面干嘛了，”哈尔杰德说，“下场比武帕索罗斯没去，再下一场还是没去。最后，第四场开始那会他总算回武斗场上接着比了，米娜就回那箱房里观赛了。她看着像是再也没啥病了。实际上她面带微笑，而且略带绯红呢。” “他们还接着做了？”肖玛拉高声惊呼。 “那些下流细节我怎么知道那么全。只是在比武之后，因为卢卡灰和他的汗黏在一起，十个扈从足足用十三个小时才把帕索罗斯的甲胄卸下来。” “我不太明白，你是说，他甚至用不着卸甲他们就——但这怎么做到的啊？” “正如我所说的那样，”哈尔杰德答，“这只是一个故事，讲一个在着甲时行动得比卸甲更灵动更迅敏的人而已。” “行吧，那确实是有本事。”加拉兹说。

哈尔杰德讲故事 塔维·德罗米奥 著

[pagebreak] [pagebreak]

“我觉得有史以来最伟大的战士一定是维鲁斯·诺梅努斯啦，”肖玛拉提名一人，“还有哪个战士夺取的疆域比他更多呢，提上名来吧。” “显然是泰伯·塞普汀么。”哈尔杰德说。 “他不是战士，他是个行政官……政治家啊。”加拉兹说，“再说，征服土地大小可不能作为选定最伟大战士的终极标准。你们看剑技如何呀？” “除了剑还有别的那么多武器啊，”肖玛拉反驳，“那为什么不说战斧或箭术咧？谁又是精通所有武器的大宗师呢？” “我可想不到谁是所有武器全通的大宗师，”哈尔杰德说，“黑沼泽埃吉亚·尼禄的巴拉克斯堪称枪中凶徒。灰地的厄恩斯·乐乌是我见过最强的棍棒手。若论打刀，那就该是某位咱们闻所未闻的阿卡维尔将军。至于箭术嘛——” “传说‘白轮’佩林纳尔只是单枪匹马就征服了泰姆瑞尔全境噢。”肖玛拉插嘴打断他。 “那都是第一纪元之前了，”加拉兹说，“有可能只是段神话而已。现代的伟大战士数不胜数。卡莫然篡位者又怎样呢？还有那个重组混沌之杖还击破了贾伽·萨恩的无名英雄嘛？” “咱把一位无名勇士称作至高战士明显有失偏颇吧。你们怎么看卡塔瑞亚女王的勇士南多尔·贝莱德？”肖玛拉再度提名，“据说他能驾驭任何已经被发明出来的武器啊。” “而他的下场呢？”加拉兹笑了，“他因为无法卸甲而溺死在幽灵海。你们大可说我吹毛求疵，但我觉得，世界上最伟大的战士最起码该知道如何脱下盔甲吧。” “要是把着甲视为一种技能来判定一位战士的高低，那可太难了吧，”肖玛拉说，“盔甲只有有用没用之分么。” “话别说死哦，”哈尔杰德说，“也有精于此道的大师，同样是穿着盔甲，人家做事情就比咱们有效率。你们有没有听说过国王的祖先，哈拉鲁·帕索罗斯啊？” 肖玛拉和加拉兹都坦言并不认识。 “那都是几百年前的事了，帕索罗斯是一大块封邑的统治者，这所庄园就是他作为这片土地最强战士赢来的。据说啊，而且也是事实吧，整个家族当今的权势呀，都是建立在帕索罗斯的战士生涯所积累资源的基础上喔。帕索罗斯每周都在他城堡里头搞比赛，跟周围领地的勇士们比试，不过他每回都能赢走些什么。 他最厉害的地方可不是耍武器，虽然他剑法斧法也还说得过去。但他全身给重甲包个严严实实的时侯，一举一动照样灵动敏捷得很，甚至有些传言说他着甲的时候比不穿盔甲行动更快。 “这故事开始前的几个月呢，他把附近某个邻邦的女儿给赢到了手。那叫米娜的女孩长得标致得很，帕索罗斯顺势娶她当了老婆。他非常疼爱妻子，却又十分猜忌她。米娜对丈夫的'技能'不怎么满意，而她还没出轨的唯一原因就是帕索罗斯老把她盯个死死的。说好听点，她身处这种被宠爱的地位，难免恃宠而骄，变得多情而不满。帕索罗斯不管上哪去都带着米娜。比武时，他就把她放在一间特制的箱子里头，这样即使时比赛时也能看到他。 “不过帕索罗斯可不知道，他最大的竞争威胁来自一个帅气军械师，这位小白脸也是他在比武里赢到的。米娜早留心他了，而那位叫泰伦的军械师自然也留心着她咯。” “哈尔杰德啊，这不就是个荤段子嘛。”肖玛拉笑道。 “我发誓确有其事，”哈尔杰德说，“情人们面临的问题嘛，毋庸置疑了，是他们永远没法私会。也许正因如此，这俩人反而有了一种强烈的痴迷。泰伦觉着他们交流爱情的良机呀，那就是在比武期间。米娜诈病就用不着给关在那箱房里头，但帕索罗斯在比武的时候隔个几分钟就要去探探病，叫这泰伦和米娜一直没法二人世界。不过，帕索罗斯每次探病上楼时甲叶叮当的声音让泰伦心生一计。 “他好好地给主子新打了一身强韧、光洁而且饰纹瑰丽的甲胄。出于他那目的，泰伦用卢卡灰擦抹护腿甲关节榫位，所以帕索罗斯运动越多冒汗越多，这地方就黏得越紧。过了一阵子，泰伦估计帕索罗斯肯定没法快走了，在比武两次战斗之间的空当也不够他去找老婆去了。但为了以防万一嘛，泰伦还在护腿甲上加了些铃铛，只要一动铃铛就铛铛作响。有了这个警报，奸夫淫妇就能在帕索罗斯过来之前有足够的时间提前听到他的到来。 “比武开始的第二周，米娜又装起病来。泰伦则给他主子献上新盔甲。如他所愿，帕索罗斯一见就爱不释手，还在第一战就穿上它了。泰伦随即摸上楼去了米娜的房间。 “外头静悄悄的，两人马上开始翻云覆雨。突然，米娜注意到泰伦表情异样，不等她开口问，奸夫就已经身首异处了。帕索罗斯拎着手斧正站在他后头呢。” “他怎么这么快就上楼了，护腿甲关节不是已经粘住了吗？难不成他们没听到铃声？”加拉兹问道。 “嗯，因为，当帕索罗斯发现他没法靠腿快走的时候，他就改用手走路了。” “我可不信。”肖玛拉大笑起来。 “然后呢？”加拉兹问，“帕索罗斯把米娜也剁了？” “天知道后面干嘛了，”哈尔杰德说，“下场比武帕索罗斯没去，再下一场还是没去。最后，第四场开始那会他总算回武斗场上接着比了，米娜就回那箱房里观赛了。她看着像是再也没啥病了。实际上她面带微笑，而且略带绯红呢。” “他们还接着做了？”肖玛拉高声惊呼。 “那些下流细节我怎么知道那么全。只是在比武之后，因为卢卡灰和他的汗黏在一起，十个扈从足足用十三个小时才把帕索罗斯的甲胄卸下来。” “我不太明白，你是说，他甚至用不着卸甲他们就——但这怎么做到的啊？” “正如我所说的那样，”哈尔杰德答，“这只是一个故事，讲一个在着甲时行动得比卸甲更灵动更迅敏的人而已。” “行吧，那确实是有本事。”加拉兹说。

HALLGERD'S TALE by Tavi Dromio

[pagebreak] [pagebreak]

think the greatest warrior who ever lived had to be Vilus Nommenus," offered Xiomara. "Name one other warrior who conquered more territory." "Tiber Septim, obviously," said Hallgerd. "He wasn't a warrior. He was an administrator... a politician," said Garaz. "And besides, acreage conquered can't be final means of determining the best warrior. How about skill with a blade?" "There are other weapons than blades," objected Xiomara. "Why not skill with an axe or a bow? Who was the greatest master of all weaponry?" "I can't think of one greatest master of all weaponry," said Hallgerd. "Balaxes of Agia Nero in Black Marsh was the greatest wielder of a lance. Ernse Llervu of the Ashlands is the greatest master of the club I've ever seen. The greatest master of the katana is probably an Akaviri warlord we've never heard of. As far as archery goes --" "Pelinal Whitestrake supposedly conquered all of Tamriel by himself," interrupted Xiomara. "That was before the First Era," said Garaz. "It's probably mostly myth. But there are all sorts of great warriors of the modern eras. The Camoran Usurper? The unknown hero who brought together the Staff of Chaos and defeated Jagar Tharn?" "We can't declare an unknown champion as the greatest warrior. What about Nandor Beraid, the Empress Katariah's champion?" suggested Xiomara. "They said he could use any weapon ever invented." "But what happened to him?" smiled Garaz. "He was drowned in the Sea of Ghosts because he couldn't get his armor off. Call me overly particular, but I think the greatest warrior in the world should know how to take armor off." "It's kinda hard to judge ability to wear armor as a skill," said Xiomara. "Either you have basic functionality in a suit of armor or you don't." "That's not true," said Hallgerd. "There are masters in that as well, people who can do things while wearing armor better than we can out of armor. Have you ever heard of Hlaalu Pasoroth, the King's great grandfather?" Xiomara and Garaz admitted that they had not. "This was hundreds and hundreds of years ago, and Pasoroth was the ruler of a great estate which he had won by right of being the greatest warrior in the land. It's been said, and truly, that much of the House's current power is based on Pasoroth's earnings as a warrior. Every week he held games at his castle, pitting his skill against the champions of the neighboring estates, and every week, he won something. His great skill wasn't in the use of weaponry, though he was decent enough with an axe and a long sword, but in his ability to move quickly and with great agility wearing a full suit of heavy mail. There were some who said that he moved faster while wearing armor than he did out of it. "Some months before this story begins, he had won the daughter of one of his neighbors, a beautiful creature named Mena who he had made his wife. He loved her very much, but he was intensely jealous, and with good reason. She wasn't very pleased with his husbandly skills, and the only reason Mena never strayed was because Pasoroth kept a close eye on her. She was, to put it kindly, naturally amorous and resentful of her position as a prize. Wherever he went, he always brought her with him. At the games, she was placed in a special box so that he could see her even while he competed. "But his real competition, though he didn't know it, was from a handsome young armorer he also had won at one of his competitions. Mena had noticed him, and the armorer, whose name was Taren, had certainly noticed her." "This has all the makings of a dirty joke, Hallgerd," said Xiomara, with a smile. "I swear that it's entirely true," said Hallgerd. "The problem facing the lovers was, of course, that they could never be alone. Perhaps because of this, it became a burning obsession to both of them. Taren decided that the best time for them to consummate their love was during the games. Mena feigned illness, so she didn't have to stay in the box, but Pasoroth visited the sickroom every few minutes between fights, so Taren and Mena could never get together. The sound of Pasoroth's armor clunking up the stairs to visit his sick wife gave Taren the idea. "He crafted his lord a new suit of armor, strong, and bright, and beautifully decorated. For his purposes, Taren rubbed the leg joints with luca dust so the more he sweated and the more he moved them, the more they'd stick together. After a little while, Taren figured, Pasoroth wouldn't be able to walk very quickly, and wouldn't have enough time in between fights to visit his wife. But just in case, Taren also added bells to the legs which rung loudly when they moved, so the couple would be able to hear him coming in plenty of time. "When the games commenced the following week, Mena feigned illness again and Taren presented his lord with the new armor. Pasoroth was delighted with it, as Taren hoped he would be, and donned it for his first fight. Taren then stole upstairs to Mena's bedchamber. "All was silent outside as the two began to make love. Suddenly, Mena noticed a peculiar expression on Taren's face and before she had a chance to ask him about it, his head fell off at the neck. Pasoroth was standing behind him with his axe in hand." "How did he get upstairs so quickly, with his leg joints gummed up? And didn't they hear the bells ringing?" asked Garaz. "Well, you see, when Pasoroth realized he couldn't walk on his legs very quickly, he walked on his hands." "I don't believe it," laughed Xiomara. "What happened next?" asked Garaz. "Did Pasoroth kill Mena also?" "No one knows exactly what happened next," said Hallgerd. "Pasoroth didn't return for the next game, nor for the next. Finally, at the fourth game, he returned to fight, and Mena appeared in the box to watch. She didn't appear to be sick anymore. In fact, she was smiling and had a light flush to her face." "They did it?" cried Xiomara. "I don't have all the salacious details, except that after the battle, it took ten squires thirteen hours to get Pasoroth's armor off because of all the luca dust mixed with sweat." "I don't understand, you mean, he didn't take his armor off when they -- but how?" "Like I said," replied Hallgerd. "This is a story about someone who was more agile and accomplished in his armor than out of it." "Now, that's skill," said Garaz. Chinese: [pagebreak]

english: Tired of bread and stew? Had enough of fish and fowl? Treat yourself and your kin to a choice cut of steak or a tender rack of ribs!
chinese: 吃腻了面包和炖菜还有鱼肉鸡肉了吗？买块精选的肉排或者鲜嫩的肋排犒劳一下一家人吧！

Song of the Askelde Men (Nord/Traditonal) Translation 3E213 Atheneum Monks at Old Anthel

[pagebreak]

Fifty Nights from home I last awoke upon a sky-flung cliff in Hjaalmarch Hold Though my flesh had died and gone to ground My Vision went on, from body unbound Winking there in the vale whence I came This dead man's eyes saw pale flame Where men the same who took life away Sung high their battle-glory and praise Wafting went I, a shade or a wight Through stoic pines, pitched ink of night Ere I came upon the pyre-burning throng I heard carried on wind's wing their song "Sing high and clear, bandsmen born of sky Let Sovngarde hear and join our cry" "These honored dead shed blood upon the fen Ending Orc and Elf and traitor men." "Your spirit went unto and filled their heart You sped them to glory, Hail Spirit Wulfharth" Then oil from urns fed greedy flames burning what few my legion and I slayed Wordlessy they chanted then until dawn Every flake of ash gathered ere they marched on Swept along unseen, so too went I Meekly haunting these Children of the Sky Tireless they went, over hearth and hill Exhaustion seemed only to spur them still Unflagging they went, a whorl of rage Soon finding our camp, bloated with prey My dead heart ached for I knew men within Doomed, never knowing how close was their end Again the Nord chests swelled up in refrain I screamed unheard. I wept with horror plain "Hear us, our ancestor, Ash King, Ysmir Honor this warband as we to glory repair" "Those dead to whom you spoke and heard We bear them upon us, Your valor conferred" And so it was, to the man each was smeared With ash of a Brother's bone, blood and beard These ashen brutes, the Askelde Men Set to a gruesome task, each bowstring bent I bellowed then, a cry of desperate rage A futile howl among those men, an empty page Yet one elder turned and unblinking, stared into the vapor-soul of me, his nostrils flared He bellowed ancient words, his beard aflame And my vision fell away, Peace at last came
chinese:
[pagebreak]

阿斯凯尔德人之歌 （诺德/传统） 第三纪元213年 古安泰尔文艺院僧侣译本

[pagebreak]

离家五十夜后我最后一次回神醒还 在希雅陲领一处高耸的悬崖上 虽我的肉身已经消亡，在地底埋藏 然我愿景仍继续前航，挣脱躯壳缚绑 于我所去往的那幽谷之中眼波流转 这亡者的双眸目见苍白的焰火煌煌 那些夺取同一条生命的人们在彼方 放声高歌着他们的战斗光耀与颂扬 飘忽的我啊，是死灵一头或暗影一张 穿过坚忍的松木林场，泼洒黑夜的墨浪 在我融入围拢焚燃柴堆的人群之前 我闻轻风有翼将彼等曲调托载飞扬 “且清声高歌吧，诞自天空的后裔 教松加德也聆听加入吾等咆哮豪气” “诸此荣耀亡者于沼泽挥洒鲜血淋漓 将那兽人、精灵与人类叛徒性命断尽。” “汝之英灵已深植而又充实彼等心灵 汝带彼等走向荣耀，万岁，英烈武夫哈斯” 瓮中的滚油激起了贪婪的火焰 烧起数个我与自己军团所杀的人员 他们默默无言地反复吟咏直至晨曦 他们行军前每一片灰烬皆聚拢为一 彼以无形之姿席卷而离，我遂也一起 逆来顺受萦绕着这些天空儿女的心际 他们似无惫疲，向前翻山越岭 而力竭好似只会刺激他们接续前行 他们前行势无懈怠，难遏怒火焚心怀 不久寻获我们营地，尽是待宰的羔羊 我已死之心痛哉，只因我知人类注定 皆有一死，却永不知命数终了何其近 诺德人们的胸膛又一次猛然地鼓起 我尖叫却无人能听。我因惊恐而泣鸣 “聆听吾等，吾等先祖，灰烬之王，伊斯米尔 荣耀此战团，一如吾等荣耀修复那般” “诸位与汝相言谈与聆听之亡者们啊 吾等将彼等背负在身，汝之英勇赐福” 便如是，对此人而言，每一个人都沾染 那一位弟兄骨灰、碧血和胡须的燃灰 这些灰白的蛮兽们，阿斯凯尔德人们 着手一桩阴森谋划，每缕弓弦皆折弯 我高吼一声，一声绝望而狂怒的暴喝 他们中有一声徒劳嚎叫，如一页空白 却有一位老者转身定睛，深深地凝望 刺入我如雾的魂魄，他的鼻孔翕动扩张 他吐喝古老文辞，他的虬髯昂然燃烧 而我的视野滑入深暗，安息终究到临
englishNormalized:
[pagebreak]

离家五十夜后我最后一次回神醒还 在希雅陲领一处高耸的悬崖上 虽我的肉身已经消亡，在地底埋藏 然我愿景仍继续前航，挣脱躯壳缚绑 于我所去往的那幽谷之中眼波流转 这亡者的双眸目见苍白的焰火煌煌 那些夺取同一条生命的人们在彼方 放声高歌着他们的战斗光耀与颂扬 飘忽的我啊，是死灵一头或暗影一张 穿过坚忍的松木林场，泼洒黑夜的墨浪 在我融入围拢焚燃柴堆的人群之前 我闻轻风有翼将彼等曲调托载飞扬 “且清声高歌吧，诞自天空的后裔 教松加德也聆听加入吾等咆哮豪气” “诸此荣耀亡者于沼泽挥洒鲜血淋漓 将那兽人、精灵与人类叛徒性命断尽。” “汝之英灵已深植而又充实彼等心灵 汝带彼等走向荣耀，万岁，英烈武夫哈斯” 瓮中的滚油激起了贪婪的火焰 烧起数个我与自己军团所杀的人员 他们默默无言地反复吟咏直至晨曦 他们行军前每一片灰烬皆聚拢为一 彼以无形之姿席卷而离，我遂也一起 逆来顺受萦绕着这些天空儿女的心际 他们似无惫疲，向前翻山越岭 而力竭好似只会刺激他们接续前行 他们前行势无懈怠，难遏怒火焚心怀 不久寻获我们营地，尽是待宰的羔羊 我已死之心痛哉，只因我知人类注定 皆有一死，却永不知命数终了何其近 诺德人们的胸膛又一次猛然地鼓起 我尖叫却无人能听。我因惊恐而泣鸣 “聆听吾等，吾等先祖，灰烬之王，伊斯米尔 荣耀此战团，一如吾等荣耀修复那般” “诸位与汝相言谈与聆听之亡者们啊 吾等将彼等背负在身，汝之英勇赐福” 便如是，对此人而言，每一个人都沾染 那一位弟兄骨灰、碧血和胡须的燃灰 这些灰白的蛮兽们，阿斯凯尔德人们 着手一桩阴森谋划，每缕弓弦皆折弯 我高吼一声，一声绝望而狂怒的暴喝 他们中有一声徒劳嚎叫，如一页空白 却有一位老者转身定睛，深深地凝望 刺入我如雾的魂魄，他的鼻孔翕动扩张 他吐喝古老文辞，他的虬髯昂然燃烧 而我的视野滑入深暗，安息终究到临

english: 8th of Evening Star, 4E 200 Jaree-Ra is up to something big. He wouldn't tell me exactly, all he said was, "It'll be just like the good old days, before the cave-in sealed your boats in there." We can't send our ships out of here anymore. What is he hinting at? 14th of Evening Star, 4E 200 He wants to know what loot we have saved up. I don't care what he says, I'm not telling him anything about my stash! It stays down there where it's safe. I can't imagine he'd ever jump in this murky water, let alone swim down to the sunken boat. 21st of Evening Star, 4E 200 It's on! Jaree-Ra and his sister, Deeja, have something cooked up. All they need is a scapegoat. I wonder where they'll find someone dumb enough to do what they say. There's bound to be someone out there who will.
chinese: 4E200年，夜星月 8日 加雷-拉有个大计划。但他不愿意细说，只是说：“像过去的美好时光一样，找回你的船被塌方封在洞里之前的日子。”我们已经没法把船开出去了。他在暗示什么？ 4E200年，夜星月 14日 他想知道我们还剩多少钱。不管他怎么说，我不能告诉他我藏的东西！它待在下面最安全的地方。他肯定不能跳进这浑浊的水里，更别提游到沉船去了。 4E200年，夜星月 21日 开始了！加雷-拉和他姐妹蒂亚要动手了。但他们还需要一个替罪羊。上哪儿去找傻到他们说什么就干什么的人？肯定有人会知道的。

english: Frostmere Crypt, Week 12 What a disaster. We haven't seen a caravan in a week. We actually lost ground in the tunnels- Thal and his team were crushed when their section collapsed. I've heard word of a mutiny. If next week isn't any better... [pagebreak] Frostmere Crypt, Week 13 Two new recruits- Eisa and Ra'jirr. Girl said she did time in Cidhna Mine, down in Markarth, and it shows- she's got more experience than all of us put together. I've put her in charge of the dig. Cat's good with a blade, so I've sent him topside with the raiders. [pagebreak] Frostmere Crypt, Week 14 Tunnel's going well. Eisa made our first big find just yesterday- buried chest with a couple hundred gold. Topside, Ra'jirr and his team have hit five merchants this week, in and out faster than the guard could respond. Looks like things are finally going my way. [pagebreak] Frostmere Crypt, Week 15 The tunnel broke out into some kind of giant cavern. There's whole forest down there- found a nice sword all laid out on some kind of monument and everything. But this is just strange. Eerie. What is this place? Not like any tomb I've ever seen. [pagebreak] Frostmere Crypt, Week 16 I think the cat's gone crazy. All of a sudden, he can't focus, keeps muttering to himself. He's gone from our best raider to our worst- botched two jobs just today. Eisa's trying to cover for him, but she's not fooling anyone. Lately, he keeps asking to see my sword, begging to borrow it. Like I'll let him get his paws on it- it's beautiful, perfectly balanced, better than any I've ever held. Sure, he can have it- right in the gut.
chinese: 霜池地穴第12周 真是一场灾难，我们已经一周没有看到车辆经过了。隧道里出了事故，塔尔那队人所在的区域塌了，所有人都被压碎了。我听说有暴动的消息。如果下周还不好转的话…… [pagebreak] 霜池地穴第13周 来了两个新人，一个叫埃莎一个叫拉’吉尔。那姑娘说自己在马卡斯的希讷矿场蹲过大狱，她肯定比我们所有人加起来都有经验。我让她负责挖掘工作。那个卡吉特的刀法不错，所以我让他到上层去突击队报道了。 [pagebreak] 霜池地穴第14周 隧道挖掘很顺利。就在昨天，埃莎带来了我们到这里来的第一个大发现——一口埋在地下装着几百枚金币的箱子。拉’吉尔和他的团队这周已经抢劫了五个商人，进进出出的速度比卫兵的反应快多了。看来事情终于朝着有利于我的方向发展了。 [pagebreak] 霜池地穴第15周 隧道的尽头有个巨大的洞穴。下面有一整片森林，我发现有把漂亮的剑放在一个类似纪念碑之类的东西上。很奇怪。太怪了。这是个什么地方？和我见过的坟墓都不一样。 [pagebreak] 霜池地穴第16周 我觉得那只猫疯了。他突然就没法集中注意力，还一直自言自语。就在今天，他这个最好的突击队员搞砸了两场买卖。埃莎还想替他掩饰，但她骗不了任何人。 最近他一直想看我的剑，求我借给他。说的好像我会让他把爪子碰一下似的。这把剑真漂亮，而且平衡性非常好，我就没用过这么好的剑。当然，他也可以拥有它，用自己的肚子。

english: <font face="$HandwrittenFont" size="18"> Lord Ulfric, Though we drove the Emperor's dogs from Fort Neugrad, they still nip at our heels. The chaos in Helgen is bad enough, but now I have word of a new Imperial force assembling in the south, ready to advance on our position as Pale Pass is clear. Send reinforcements, or all our gains will be for naught. </font>
chinese: <font face="$HandwrittenFont" size="18"> 乌弗瑞克大人： 虽然我们把皇帝的走狗赶出了纽格拉德堡，但它们还是穷追不舍。海尔根的混乱已经够糟了，但我现在得到消息，说一支新的帝国军正在南部集结，打算确保苍白隘口之后向我们推进。请速派援军，否则我们的战果就要白费了。 </font>
englishNormalized: <font face="$handwrittenfont" size="18"> lord ulfric, though we drove the emperor's dogs from fort neugrad, they still nip at our heels. the chaos in helgen is bad enough, but now i have word of a new imperial force assembling in the south, ready to advance on our position as pale pass is clear. send reinforcements, or all our gains will be for naught. </font>

Send reinforcements, or all our gains will be for naught. </font>

[pagebreak]

THE MIRROR by Berdier Wreans

[pagebreak] [pagebreak]

he wind blew over the open plain, jostling the few trees within to move back and forth with the irritation of it. A young man in bright green turban approached the army and gave his chieftain's terms for peace to the commander. He was refused. It was to be battle, the battle of Ain-Kolur. So the chief Iymbez had decreed his open defiance and his horsemen were at war once again. Many times the tribe had moved into territory that was not theirs to occupy, and many times the diplomatic approach had failed. It had come to this, at long last. It was just as well with Mindothrax. His allies may win or lose, but he would always survive. Though he had occasionally been on the losing side of a war, never once in all his thirty-four years had he lost in hand-to-hand combat. The two armies poured like dual frothing streams through the dust, and when they met a clamor rang out, echoing into the hills. Blood, the first liquor the clay had tasted in many a month, danced like powder. The high and low battle cries of the rival tribes met in harmony as the armies dug into one another's flesh. Mindothrax was in the element he loved. After ten hours of fighting with no ground given, both commanders called a mutual and honorable withdrawal from the field. The camp was positioned in a high-walled garden of an old burial ground, adorned by springtide blossoms. As Mindothrax toured the grounds, he was reminded of his childhood home. It was a happy and a sad recollection, the purity of childhood ambition, all of his schooling in the ways of battle, but tinged with memories of his poor mother. A beautiful woman looking down at her son with both pride and unspoken sorrow. She never talked about what troubled her, but it came as no surprise to any when she took the walk across the moors and was found days later, her throat slit open by her own hand. The army itself was like a colony of ants, newly shaken. Within a half hour's time after the end of the battle, they had reorganized as if by instinct. As the medics looked to the wounded, someone remarked, with a measure of admiration and astonishment, "Look at Mindothrax. His hair isn't even out of place." "He is a mighty swordsman," said the attending physician. "The sword is a greatly overvalued article," said Mindothrax, nevertheless pleased with the attention. "Warriors pay too much attention to striking and not enough in defending strikes. The proper way to go into battle is to defend yourself, and to hit your opponent only when the ideal moment arises." "I prefer a more straight-forward approach," smiled one of the wounded. "It is the way of the horse men." "If it is the way of the Bjoulsae tribes to fail, then I renounce my heritage," said Mindothrax, making a quick sign to the spirits that he was being expressive not blasphemous. "Remember what the great blademaster Gaiden Shinji said, 'The best techniques are passed on by the survivors.' I have been in thirty-six battles, and I haven't a scar to show for them. That is because I rely on my shield, and then my blade, in that order." "What is your secret?" "Think of melee as a mirror. I look to my opponent's left arm when I am striking with my right. If he is prepared to block my blow, I blow not. Why exert undue force?" Mindothrax cocked an eyebrow, "But when I see his right arm tense, my left arm goes to my shield. You see, it takes twice as much power to send force than it does to deflect it. When your eye can recognize whether your opponent is striking from above, or at angle, or in an uppercut from below, you learn to pivot and place your shield just so to protect yourself. I could block for hours if need be, but it only takes a few minutes, or even seconds, for your opponent, used to battering, to leave a space open for your own strike." "What was the longest you've ever had to defend yourself?" asked the wounded man. "I fought a man once for an hour's time," said Mindothrax. "He was tireless with his bludgeoning, never giving me a moment to do aught but block his strikes. But finally, he took a moment too long in raising his cudgel and I found my mark in his chest. He struck my shield a thousand times, and I struck his heart but once. But that was enough." "So he was your greatest opponent?" asked the medico. "Oh, indeed not," said Mindothrax, turning his great shield so the silvery metal reflected his own face. "There is he." The next day, the battle recommenced. Chief Iymbez had brought in reinforcements from the islands to the south. To the horror and disgrace of the tribe, mercenaries, renegade horsemen and even some Reachmen witches were included in the war. As Mindothrax stared across the field at the armies assembling, putting on his helmet and readying his shield and blade, he thought again of his poor mother. What had tortured her so? Why had she never been able to look at her son without grief? Between sunrise and sundown, the battle raged. A bright blue-sky overhead burned down on the combatants as they rushed against one another over and over again. In every melee, Mindothrax prevailed. A foe with an ax rained a series of strokes against his shield, but every one was deflected until at last Mindothrax could best the warrior. A spear maiden nearly pierced the shield with her first strike, but Mindothrax knew how to give with the blow, throwing her off balance and leaving her open for his counterstrike. Finally, he met a mercenary on the field, armed with shield and sword and a helm of golden bronze. For an hour and a half they battled. Mindothrax tried every trick he knew. When the mercenary tensed his left arm, he held back his strike. When his opponent rose his sword, his shield rose too and expertly blocked. For the first time in his life, he was battling another defensive fighter. Stationary, reflective, with energy to battle for days if need be. Occasionally, another warrior would enter into the fray, sometimes from Mindothrax's army, sometimes from his opponent's. These distractions were swiftly dispatched, and the champions returned to their fight. As they fought, circling one another, matching block for blow and blow for block, it dawned on Mindothrax that here at last he was fighting the perfect mirror. It became more a game, almost a dance, than a battle of blood. It was not until Mindothrax missed his own step, striking too soon, throwing himself off balance, that the promenade was ended. He saw, rather than felt, the mercenary's blade rip across him from throat to chest. A good strike. The sort he himself might have delivered. Mindothrax fell to the ground, feeling his life passing. The mercenary stood over him, prepared to give his worthy adversary the killing blow. It was a strange, honorable deed for an outsider to do, and Mindothrax was greatly moved. Across the battlefield, he heard someone call a name, similar to his own. "Jurrifax!" The mercenary removed his helmet to answer the call. As he did so, Mindothrax saw through the slits of his helmet his own reflection in the man. It was his own close-set eyes, red and brown hair, thin and wide mouth, and blunt chin. For a moment he marveled at the mirror, before the stranger turned back to him and delivered the death stroke. Jurrifax returned to his commander and was well paid for his part in the day's victory. They retired for a hot meal under the stars in a garden by an old cairn that had previously been occupied by their foes. The mercenary was strangely quiet as he observed the land. "Have you been here before, Jurrifax?" asked one of the tribesmen who had hired him. "I was born a horseman just like you. My mother sold me when I was just a babe. I have always wondered how my life might have been different had I not been bartered away. I might never have been a mercenary." "There are many things that decide our fate," said the witch. "It is madness to try to see how you might have taken this turn or that in the world. There are none exactly like yourself, so it is foolish to compare." "But there is one," said Jurrifax, looking to the stars. "My master, before he set me free, said that my mother had twin sons when I was born. She could only afford to raise but one child, but somewhere out there, there is a man just like me. My brother. I hope to meet him." The witch saw the spirits before her and knew the truth that the twins had met already. She remained silent and stared into the fire, banishing the thoughts from her head, too wise to tell all.
chinese:
[pagebreak]

镜 柏迪尔·温林斯 著

[pagebreak] [pagebreak]

开阔的平原上狂风怒号，暴戾地把仅有的几棵树推搡得前仰后合。一名裹着鲜绿色头巾的青年抵达军营，把他的酋长的议和条件递交给此间的指挥官。然而他被拒绝了，这即将引发一场战斗，即是艾恩-科勒尔之战。 于是恩贝茨酋长大张旗鼓地公开挑衅，而他的骑手将再度进入交战状态。这部落曾无数次强行迁徙到并不属于他们的领土内，而对他们无数次的交涉尝试均告失败。最终，这一矛盾已经发展到如此再难斡旋的地步。这于明铎扎克斯而言挺不错。他的盟友或胜或败，但他总能幸存。尽管他偶有成为战争败方的时候，但三十四年中，近身肉搏中他未尝半次败绩。 在扬天沙土中，两军有如两股白沫滚涌的怒流般狠狠撞散了彼此，狂呼长啸的战斗雄叫在群山间反复回荡。鲜血，是此间泥土数月以来品味到的第一种饮品，铁锈般的浓郁腥气纷乱飘舞。双方的士卒以各种武器撕掘着敌军的血肉，高高低低的战嚎相互应和，有如和谐的乐曲。明铎扎克斯十分欣喜这种场面。 在10小时的疯狂搏杀后，两军指挥官下令让军队有序体面地撤离战场。 营寨驻扎于某处拥有高耸围墙，墙内是春潮烂漫的装饰花海的老墓地花园中。当明铎扎克斯巡游此地时，他回忆起童年在故乡的生活。彼时的回忆快乐而悲伤，一尘不染的童年抱负、还有战争中受到的训练，但也有对他可怜母亲的追忆。在他心中，一位美丽的夫人眼波中带着骄傲和无以言表的哀伤，俯身凝望她的儿子。她从未谈及究竟是什么困扰着她，但她徒步穿越了旷野，数天后才被发现时，她已用手割开了自己的喉咙，但这没有出乎任何人意料之外。 军中混乱状况有如刚刚遇袭的纷乱蚁群。不过在休战后半小时内，他们像发自本能般重新组织了起来。在军医们照料伤员时，有些人聚在一块聊起天来，语带惊奇和钦佩：“瞧瞧明铎扎克斯，他甚至连发型都一丝不乱。” “他可是个非凡的剑客。”主治医师说。 “剑这种武器被高估太多了。” 明铎扎克斯说。其实他内心对于备受关注很高兴，“战士们总是过于注重进攻，而不怎么关注防守。战斗的正确技巧是保护好你自己，直到理想的时机到来，一击制敌。” “我更喜欢直接干，”伤员之一笑道，“这才像个骑兵的样子。” “若这便是比约尔萨部落战败的要因，那我就得改改我的思路了。”明铎扎克斯说。他用这样迂回的说法暗示，他上述言论并非表达着瞧不起勇猛者的含义：“牢记剑圣盖登·辛吉的名言吧：‘最好的技术都是由幸存者们代代传流的。’我已经历三十六役，但身无一创。皆因我优先依靠我的盾，其次才是我的剑。” “可你一定有什么秘诀吧？” “把搏杀想象成照镜子。当我的右手进攻，我就留心着对手的左臂。若他看起来已经准备好格挡我的攻势，那我就停下。何必做无用功进行明知失败的攻击？”明铎扎克斯挑起一边眉毛，“但一旦我看到他右臂绷紧，我就用左臂架起盾。你要知道，格挡偏转所需的力道不过是进攻一方的一半。等你的眼睛能辨别敌手或是上劈、或从随便哪个角度扫击、或是下撩时，你就渐渐明白如何以你的盾为中心，旋转、架置它来更好地保护自己。如有必要，我能数小时不断地格挡；但只需坚持几分钟，甚至可能几秒，你的疲于进攻的对手就会出现让你有机可乘的空当。” “那你最久的一次防御打了多长时间？”那伤员追问。 “这得数我和一个家伙打上足足一小时那回了，”明铎扎克斯说，“他抡起短棒就是一顿不知疲倦地捶，不给我丝毫喘息机会，我只好一直招架。但最终他累得半死，花很久才能举高短棍，于是我顺势在他胸口找到了空门。他捶了我的盾上千次，而我只刺中他的心脏一次。但这也够了。” “这么看来，他就是你遇过最大的对手了？”一直聆听着的医师问。 “噢，当然，”明铎扎克斯说着翻转他的巨盾，让鎏银那面倒映出他自己的脸，“是他。” 次日，战火重燃。恩贝茨酋长从南方岛屿请了不少增援，雇佣兵、变节骑士、甚至有些瑞驰女巫都参战了，这让部落人感到惊惧和耻辱。明铎扎克斯凝视着穿越战场集合的军列，戴上他的头盔，并准备好他的剑盾的时候，他再度回忆起他可怜的母亲。是什么让她饱受煎熬？为何她每次看到自己的儿子都那么悲伤？ 血战由日出开始，到日落结束。笼围在他们身上的明亮青空现在几如炙燃。两军一次又一次互相碰撞。而每波混战，明铎扎克斯均占上风。敌军一名斧兵抡起斧刃对他的盾连挥几下，但每一击都被偏转开来，最后以明铎扎克斯胜利告终。一位持矛女战士第一击就几近刺透他的盾，但明铎扎克斯知道如何还击，他持盾猛击，让她失衡狠摔在地空门大开，无力抵御他的反击。最终，他在战场上遇到一名雇佣兵。此人头戴镀金青铜头盔，手持剑盾。两人足足拼斗了一个半小时。 明铎扎克斯使遍生平所学。每当佣兵紧绷左臂，他便停止进攻。每当他的对手高举剑刃，他也高举起盾并做出完美格挡。在漫长的军旅生涯中，他首度与另一位防反型战士对战。他们同样架势岿然，同样反应敏捷，且必要时都有连斗数日之能。偶有外人前来助阵，既有明铎扎克斯一方的也有对方的。这些闲杂人等很快就会被解决掉，而那双方的冠军便重回他们的决斗。 他们彼此周旋，盾来剑往，明铎扎克斯意识到，他终于遇上了完美的镜像。 这一场酣畅淋漓的血战变得越来越像一场游戏，一段舞蹈。他们彼此起舞着，直到明铎扎克斯终于自乱节奏，过早地攻击而失衡，这场双人舞即刻告终。时间好像减缓了。他凝视着而远非感受着，那佣兵的利刃从他的咽喉开始撕裂，贯穿到胸腔。一记优美的终结技。他也可以做到。 明铎扎克斯摔落在地，感受着生命的流逝。佣兵立于其侧，准备好为这令人敬佩的对手奉上他值得的终止痛苦的一击。于一个陌生人而言，这是一种奇怪却很荣耀的举动，明铎扎克斯被深深感动了。他最后听见的是穿越战场原而来的某人呼唤着的名字，一个和他很相似的名字。 “尤利费克斯！” 佣兵除去头盔，高声应答那呼唤。在他这么做的时候，明铎扎克斯从其头盔的接缝中看到了他自己在此人身上的倒影。那是如同他一般极近的眼距，红综的头发，宽而薄的双唇和平钝的下巴。在生命的最后一瞬，他对这镜像的相似程度大感惊奇，随后这位熟悉的陌生人旋身送上了终结一击。 尤利费克斯回到他的指挥官处，因当日大捷而得到丰厚的酬金。他们在先前敌军驻扎的那花园处安营休息，在明亮的星光下享用着热腾腾的佳肴。佣兵带着异常的沉默凝望此地。 “以前你来过这儿啊，尤利费克斯？”一个曾雇过他的部落人问。 “我生来就是骑手，像你一样。母亲在我还是婴儿时就把我卖给了别人。我总想，要是我没被卖掉，我的人生会有什么不同。我可能压根不会成为佣兵。” “左右我们命运的事物太多了。”一边的女巫说，“若你老是想着无数的如果和选择的变化，最后肯定发疯。没有人会和你一模一样，比较这些很愚蠢。” “但还真的有一个，”尤利费克斯眺望天星，“我前主人在放我自由前，告诉我其实我母亲生的是双胞胎兄弟，然而她只能养大一人……但什么地方，的确有一个极其像我的人。我的兄弟。真希望能见见他。” 女术士看见了眼前的鬼魂，随即明白了这对双胞胎兄弟已曾相见的真相。她保持沉默，静静凝望着跳跃的篝火，驱散着脑海中的思绪。她很明智，没有把这一切吐露出来。

his opponent's. these distractions were swiftly dispatched, and the champions returned to their fight. as they fought, circling one another, matching block for blow and blow for block, it dawned on mindothrax that here at last he was fighting the perfect mirror. it became more a game, almost a dance, than a battle of blood. it was not until mindothrax missed his own step, striking too soon, throwing himself off balance, that the promenade was ended. he saw, rather than felt, the mercenary's blade rip across him from throat to chest. a good strike. the sort he himself might have delivered. mindothrax fell to the ground, feeling his life passing. the mercenary stood over him, prepared to give his worthy adversary the killing blow. it was a strange, honorable deed for an outsider to do, and mindothrax was greatly moved. across the battlefield, he heard someone call a name, similar to his own. "jurrifax!" the mercenary removed his helmet to answer the call. as he did so, mindothrax saw through the slits of his helmet his own reflection in the man. it was his own close-set eyes, red and brown hair, thin and wide mouth, and blunt chin. for a moment he marveled at the mirror, before the stranger turned back to him and delivered the death stroke. jurrifax returned to his commander and was well paid for his part in the day's victory. they retired for a hot meal under the stars in a garden by an old cairn that had previously been occupied by their foes. the mercenary was strangely quiet as he observed the land. "have you been here before, jurrifax?" asked one of the tribesmen who had hired him. "i was born a horseman just like you. my mother sold me when i was just a babe. i have always wondered how my life might have been different had i not been bartered away. i might never have been a mercenary." "there are many things that decide our fate," said the witch. "it is madness to try to see how you might have taken this turn or that in the world. there are none exactly like yourself, so it is foolish to compare." "but there is one," said jurrifax, looking to the stars. "my master, before he set me free, said that my mother had twin sons when i was born. she could only afford to raise but one child, but somewhere out there, there is a man just like me. my brother. i hope to meet him." the witch saw the spirits before her and knew the truth that the twins had met already. she remained silent and stared into the fire, banishing the thoughts from her head, too wise to tell all.

[pagebreak]

镜 柏迪尔·温林斯 著

[pagebreak] [pagebreak]

开阔的平原上狂风怒号，暴戾地把仅有的几棵树推搡得前仰后合。一名裹着鲜绿色头巾的青年抵达军营，把他的酋长的议和条件递交给此间的指挥官。然而他被拒绝了，这即将引发一场战斗，即是艾恩-科勒尔之战。 于是恩贝茨酋长大张旗鼓地公开挑衅，而他的骑手将再度进入交战状态。这部落曾无数次强行迁徙到并不属于他们的领土内，而对他们无数次的交涉尝试均告失败。最终，这一矛盾已经发展到如此再难斡旋的地步。这于明铎扎克斯而言挺不错。他的盟友或胜或败，但他总能幸存。尽管他偶有成为战争败方的时候，但三十四年中，近身肉搏中他未尝半次败绩。 在扬天沙土中，两军有如两股白沫滚涌的怒流般狠狠撞散了彼此，狂呼长啸的战斗雄叫在群山间反复回荡。鲜血，是此间泥土数月以来品味到的第一种饮品，铁锈般的浓郁腥气纷乱飘舞。双方的士卒以各种武器撕掘着敌军的血肉，高高低低的战嚎相互应和，有如和谐的乐曲。明铎扎克斯十分欣喜这种场面。 在10小时的疯狂搏杀后，两军指挥官下令让军队有序体面地撤离战场。 营寨驻扎于某处拥有高耸围墙，墙内是春潮烂漫的装饰花海的老墓地花园中。当明铎扎克斯巡游此地时，他回忆起童年在故乡的生活。彼时的回忆快乐而悲伤，一尘不染的童年抱负、还有战争中受到的训练，但也有对他可怜母亲的追忆。在他心中，一位美丽的夫人眼波中带着骄傲和无以言表的哀伤，俯身凝望她的儿子。她从未谈及究竟是什么困扰着她，但她徒步穿越了旷野，数天后才被发现时，她已用手割开了自己的喉咙，但这没有出乎任何人意料之外。 军中混乱状况有如刚刚遇袭的纷乱蚁群。不过在休战后半小时内，他们像发自本能般重新组织了起来。在军医们照料伤员时，有些人聚在一块聊起天来，语带惊奇和钦佩：“瞧瞧明铎扎克斯，他甚至连发型都一丝不乱。” “他可是个非凡的剑客。”主治医师说。 “剑这种武器被高估太多了。” 明铎扎克斯说。其实他内心对于备受关注很高兴，“战士们总是过于注重进攻，而不怎么关注防守。战斗的正确技巧是保护好你自己，直到理想的时机到来，一击制敌。” “我更喜欢直接干，”伤员之一笑道，“这才像个骑兵的样子。” “若这便是比约尔萨部落战败的要因，那我就得改改我的思路了。”明铎扎克斯说。他用这样迂回的说法暗示，他上述言论并非表达着瞧不起勇猛者的含义：“牢记剑圣盖登·辛吉的名言吧：‘最好的技术都是由幸存者们代代传流的。’我已经历三十六役，但身无一创。皆因我优先依靠我的盾，其次才是我的剑。” “可你一定有什么秘诀吧？” “把搏杀想象成照镜子。当我的右手进攻，我就留心着对手的左臂。若他看起来已经准备好格挡我的攻势，那我就停下。何必做无用功进行明知失败的攻击？”明铎扎克斯挑起一边眉毛，“但一旦我看到他右臂绷紧，我就用左臂架起盾。你要知道，格挡偏转所需的力道不过是进攻一方的一半。等你的眼睛能辨别敌手或是上劈、或从随便哪个角度扫击、或是下撩时，你就渐渐明白如何以你的盾为中心，旋转、架置它来更好地保护自己。如有必要，我能数小时不断地格挡；但只需坚持几分钟，甚至可能几秒，你的疲于进攻的对手就会出现让你有机可乘的空当。” “那你最久的一次防御打了多长时间？”那伤员追问。 “这得数我和一个家伙打上足足一小时那回了，”明铎扎克斯说，“他抡起短棒就是一顿不知疲倦地捶，不给我丝毫喘息机会，我只好一直招架。但最终他累得半死，花很久才能举高短棍，于是我顺势在他胸口找到了空门。他捶了我的盾上千次，而我只刺中他的心脏一次。但这也够了。” “这么看来，他就是你遇过最大的对手了？”一直聆听着的医师问。 “噢，当然，”明铎扎克斯说着翻转他的巨盾，让鎏银那面倒映出他自己的脸，“是他。” 次日，战火重燃。恩贝茨酋长从南方岛屿请了不少增援，雇佣兵、变节骑士、甚至有些瑞驰女巫都参战了，这让部落人感到惊惧和耻辱。明铎扎克斯凝视着穿越战场集合的军列，戴上他的头盔，并准备好他的剑盾的时候，他再度回忆起他可怜的母亲。是什么让她饱受煎熬？为何她每次看到自己的儿子都那么悲伤？ 血战由日出开始，到日落结束。笼围在他们身上的明亮青空现在几如炙燃。两军一次又一次互相碰撞。而每波混战，明铎扎克斯均占上风。敌军一名斧兵抡起斧刃对他的盾连挥几下，但每一击都被偏转开来，最后以明铎扎克斯胜利告终。一位持矛女战士第一击就几近刺透他的盾，但明铎扎克斯知道如何还击，他持盾猛击，让她失衡狠摔在地空门大开，无力抵御他的反击。最终，他在战场上遇到一名雇佣兵。此人头戴镀金青铜头盔，手持剑盾。两人足足拼斗了一个半小时。 明铎扎克斯使遍生平所学。每当佣兵紧绷左臂，他便停止进攻。每当他的对手高举剑刃，他也高举起盾并做出完美格挡。在漫长的军旅生涯中，他首度与另一位防反型战士对战。他们同样架势岿然，同样反应敏捷，且必要时都有连斗数日之能。偶有外人前来助阵，既有明铎扎克斯一方的也有对方的。这些闲杂人等很快就会被解决掉，而那双方的冠军便重回他们的决斗。 他们彼此周旋，盾来剑往，明铎扎克斯意识到，他终于遇上了完美的镜像。 这一场酣畅淋漓的血战变得越来越像一场游戏，一段舞蹈。他们彼此起舞着，直到明铎扎克斯终于自乱节奏，过早地攻击而失衡，这场双人舞即刻告终。时间好像减缓了。他凝视着而远非感受着，那佣兵的利刃从他的咽喉开始撕裂，贯穿到胸腔。一记优美的终结技。他也可以做到。 明铎扎克斯摔落在地，感受着生命的流逝。佣兵立于其侧，准备好为这令人敬佩的对手奉上他值得的终止痛苦的一击。于一个陌生人而言，这是一种奇怪却很荣耀的举动，明铎扎克斯被深深感动了。他最后听见的是穿越战场原而来的某人呼唤着的名字，一个和他很相似的名字。 “尤利费克斯！” 佣兵除去头盔，高声应答那呼唤。在他这么做的时候，明铎扎克斯从其头盔的接缝中看到了他自己在此人身上的倒影。那是如同他一般极近的眼距，红综的头发，宽而薄的双唇和平钝的下巴。在生命的最后一瞬，他对这镜像的相似程度大感惊奇，随后这位熟悉的陌生人旋身送上了终结一击。 尤利费克斯回到他的指挥官处，因当日大捷而得到丰厚的酬金。他们在先前敌军驻扎的那花园处安营休息，在明亮的星光下享用着热腾腾的佳肴。佣兵带着异常的沉默凝望此地。 “以前你来过这儿啊，尤利费克斯？”一个曾雇过他的部落人问。 “我生来就是骑手，像你一样。母亲在我还是婴儿时就把我卖给了别人。我总想，要是我没被卖掉，我的人生会有什么不同。我可能压根不会成为佣兵。” “左右我们命运的事物太多了。”一边的女巫说，“若你老是想着无数的如果和选择的变化，最后肯定发疯。没有人会和你一模一样，比较这些很愚蠢。” “但还真的有一个，”尤利费克斯眺望天星，“我前主人在放我自由前，告诉我其实我母亲生的是双胞胎兄弟，然而她只能养大一人……但什么地方，的确有一个极其像我的人。我的兄弟。真希望能见见他。” 女术士看见了眼前的鬼魂，随即明白了这对双胞胎兄弟已曾相见的真相。她保持沉默，静静凝望着跳跃的篝火，驱散着脑海中的思绪。她很明智，没有把这一切吐露出来。

THE MIRROR by Berdier Wreans

[pagebreak] [pagebreak]

he wind blew over the open plain, jostling the few trees within to move back and forth with the irritation of it. A young man in bright green turban approached the army and gave his chieftain's terms for peace to the commander. He was refused. It was to be battle, the battle of Ain-Kolur. So the chief Iymbez had decreed his open defiance and his horsemen were at war once again. Many times the tribe had moved into territory that was not theirs to occupy, and many times the diplomatic approach had failed. It had come to this, at long last. It was just as well with Mindothrax. His allies may win or lose, but he would always survive. Though he had occasionally been on the losing side of a war, never once in all his thirty-four years had he lost in hand-to-hand combat. The two armies poured like dual frothing streams through the dust, and when they met a clamor rang out, echoing into the hills. Blood, the first liquor the clay had tasted in many a month, danced like powder. The high and low battle cries of the rival tribes met in harmony as the armies dug into one another's flesh. Mindothrax was in the element he loved. After ten hours of fighting with no ground given, both commanders called a mutual and honorable withdrawal from the field. The camp was positioned in a high-walled garden of an old burial ground, adorned by springtide blossoms. As Mindothrax toured the grounds, he was reminded of his childhood home. It was a happy and a sad recollection, the purity of childhood ambition, all of his schooling in the ways of battle, but tinged with memories of his poor mother. A beautiful woman looking down at her son with both pride and unspoken sorrow. She never talked about what troubled her, but it came as no surprise to any when she took the walk across the moors and was found days later, her throat slit open by her own hand. The army itself was like a colony of ants, newly shaken. Within a half hour's time after the end of the battle, they had reorganized as if by instinct. As the medics looked to the wounded, someone remarked, with a measure of admiration and astonishment, "Look at Mindothrax. His hair isn't even out of place." "He is a mighty swordsman," said the attending physician. "The sword is a greatly overvalued article," said Mindothrax, nevertheless pleased with the attention. "Warriors pay too much attention to striking and not enough in defending strikes. The proper way to go into battle is to defend yourself, and to hit your opponent only when the ideal moment arises." "I prefer a more straight-forward approach," smiled one of the wounded. "It is the way of the horse men." "If it is the way of the Bjoulsae tribes to fail, then I renounce my heritage," said Mindothrax, making a quick sign to the spirits that he was being expressive not blasphemous. "Remember what the great blademaster Gaiden Shinji said, 'The best techniques are passed on by the survivors.' I have been in thirty-six battles, and I haven't a scar to show for them. That is because I rely on my shield, and then my blade, in that order." "What is your secret?" "Think of melee as a mirror. I look to my opponent's left arm when I am striking with my right. If he is prepared to block my blow, I blow not. Why exert undue force?" Mindothrax cocked an eyebrow, "But when I see his right arm tense, my left arm goes to my shield. You see, it takes twice as much power to send force than it does to deflect it. When your eye can recognize whether your opponent is striking from above, or at angle, or in an uppercut from below, you learn to pivot and place your shield just so to protect yourself. I could block for hours if need be, but it only takes a few minutes, or even seconds, for your opponent, used to battering, to leave a space open for your own strike." "What was the longest you've ever had to defend yourself?" asked the wounded man. "I fought a man once for an hour's time," said Mindothrax. "He was tireless with his bludgeoning, never giving me a moment to do aught but block his strikes. But finally, he took a moment too long in raising his cudgel and I found my mark in his chest. He struck my shield a thousand times, and I struck his heart but once. But that was enough." "So he was your greatest opponent?" asked the medico. "Oh, indeed not," said Mindothrax, turning his great shield so the silvery metal reflected his own face. "There is he." The next day, the battle recommenced. Chief Iymbez had brought in reinforcements from the islands to the south. To the horror and disgrace of the tribe, mercenaries, renegade horsemen and even some Reachmen witches were included in the war. As Mindothrax stared across the field at the armies assembling, putting on his helmet and readying his shield and blade, he thought again of his poor mother. What had tortured her so? Why had she never been able to look at her son without grief? Between sunrise and sundown, the battle raged. A bright blue-sky overhead burned down on the combatants as they rushed against one another over and over again. In every melee, Mindothrax prevailed. A foe with an ax rained a series of strokes against his shield, but every one was deflected until at last Mindothrax could best the warrior. A spear maiden nearly pierced the shield with her first strike, but Mindothrax knew how to give with the blow, throwing her off balance and leaving her open for his counterstrike. Finally, he met a mercenary on the field, armed with shield and sword and a helm of golden bronze. For an hour and a half they battled. Mindothrax tried every trick he knew. When the mercenary tensed his left arm, he held back his strike. When his opponent rose his sword, his shield rose too and expertly blocked. For the first time in his life, he was battling another defensive fighter. Stationary, reflective, with energy to battle for days if need be. Occasionally, another warrior would enter into the fray, sometimes from Mindothrax's army, sometimes from his opponent's. These distractions were swiftly dispatched, and the champions returned to their fight. As they fought, circling one another, matching block for blow and blow for block, it dawned on Mindothrax that here at last he was fighting the perfect mirror. It became more a game, almost a dance, than a battle of blood. It was not until Mindothrax missed his own step, striking too soon, throwing himself off balance, that the promenade was ended. He saw, rather than felt, the mercenary's blade rip across him from throat to chest. A good strike. The sort he himself might have delivered. Mindothrax fell to the ground, feeling his life passing. The mercenary stood over him, prepared to give his worthy adversary the killing blow. It was a strange, honorable deed for an outsider to do, and Mindothrax was greatly moved. Across the battlefield, he heard someone call a name, similar to his own. "Jurrifax!" The mercenary removed his helmet to answer the call. As he did so, Mindothrax saw through the slits of his helmet his own reflection in the man. It was his own close-set eyes, red and brown hair, thin and wide mouth, and blunt chin. For a moment he marveled at the mirror, before the stranger turned back to him and delivered the death stroke. Jurrifax returned to his commander and was well paid for his part in the day's victory. They retired for a hot meal under the stars in a garden by an old cairn that had previously been occupied by their foes. The mercenary was strangely quiet as he observed the land. "Have you been here before, Jurrifax?" asked one of the tribesmen who had hired him. "I was born a horseman just like you. My mother sold me when I was just a babe. I have always wondered how my life might have been different had I not been bartered away. I might never have been a mercenary." "There are many things that decide our fate," said the witch. "It is madness to try to see how you might have taken this turn or that in the world. There are none exactly like yourself, so it is foolish to compare." "But there is one," said Jurrifax, looking to the stars. "My master, before he set me free, said that my mother had twin sons when I was born. She could only afford to raise but one child, but somewhere out there, there is a man just like me. My brother. I hope to meet him." The witch saw the spirits before her and knew the truth that the twins had met already. She remained silent and stared into the fire, banishing the thoughts from her head, too wise to tell all. 营寨驻扎于某处拥有高耸围墙，墙内是春潮烂漫的装饰花海的老墓地花园中。当明铎扎克斯巡游此地时，他回忆起童年在故乡的生活。彼时的回忆快乐而悲伤，一尘不染的童年抱负、还有战争中受到的训练，但也有对他可怜母亲的追忆。在他心中，一位美丽的夫人眼波中带着骄傲和无以言表的哀伤，俯身凝望她的儿子。她从未谈及究竟是什么困扰着她，但她徒步穿越了旷野，数天后才被发现时，她已用手割开了自己的喉咙，但这没有出乎任何人意料之外。 军中混乱状况有如刚刚遇袭的纷乱蚁群。不过在休战后半小时内，他们像发自本能般重新组织了起来。在军医们照料伤员时，有些人聚在一块聊起天来，语带惊奇和钦佩：“瞧瞧明铎扎克斯，他甚至连发型都一丝不乱。” “他可是个非凡的剑客。”主治医师说。 “剑这种武器被高估太多了。” 明铎扎克斯说。其实他内心对于备受关注很高兴，“战士们总是过于注重进攻，而不怎么关注防守。战斗的正确技巧是保护好你自己，直到理想的时机到来，一击制敌。” “我更喜欢直接干，”伤员之一笑道，“这才像个骑兵的样子。” “若这便是比约尔萨部落战败的要因，那我就得改改我的思路了。”明铎扎克斯说。他用这样迂回的说法暗示，他上述言论并非表达着瞧不起勇猛者的含义：“牢记剑圣盖登·辛吉的名言吧：‘最好的技术都是由幸存者们代代传流的。’我已经历三十六役，但身无一创。皆因我优先依靠我的盾，其次才是我的剑。” “可你一定有什么秘诀吧？” “把搏杀想象成照镜子。当我的右手进攻，我就留心着对手的左臂。若他看起来已经准备好格挡我的攻势，那我就停下。何必做无用功进行明知失败的攻击？”明铎扎克斯挑起一边眉毛，“但一旦我看到他右臂绷紧，我就用左臂架起盾。你要知道，格挡偏转所需的力道不过是进攻一方的一半。等你的眼睛能辨别敌手或是上劈、或从随便哪个角度扫击、或是下撩时，你就渐渐明白如何以你的盾为中心，旋转、架置它来更好地保护自己。如有必要，我能数小时不断地格挡；但只需坚持几分钟，甚至可能几秒，你的疲于进攻的对手就会出现让你有机可乘的空当。” “那你最久的一次防御打了多长时间？”那伤员追问。 “这得数我和一个家伙打上足足一小时那回了，”明铎扎克斯说，“他抡起短棒就是一顿不知疲倦地捶，不给我丝毫喘息机会，我只好一直招架。但最终他累得半死，花很久才能举高短棍，于是我顺势在他胸口找到了空门。他捶了我的盾上千次，而我只刺中他的心脏一次。但这也够了。” “这么看来，他就是你遇过最大的对手了？”一直聆听着的医师问。 “噢，当然，”明铎扎克斯说着翻转他的巨盾，让鎏银那面倒映出他自己的脸，“是他。” 次日，战火重燃。恩贝茨酋长从南方岛屿请了不少增援，雇佣兵、变节骑士、甚至有些瑞驰女巫都参战了，这让部落人感到惊惧和耻辱。明铎扎克斯凝视着穿越战场集合的军列，戴上他的头盔，并准备好他的剑盾的时候，他再度回忆起他可怜的母亲。是什么让她饱受煎熬？为何她每次看到自己的儿子都那么悲伤？ 血战由日出开始，到日落结束。笼围在他们身上的明亮青空现在几如炙燃。两军一次又一次互相碰撞。而每波混战，明铎扎克斯均占上风。敌军一名斧兵抡起斧刃对他的盾连挥几下，但每一击都被偏转开来，最后以明铎扎克斯胜利告终。一位持矛女战士第一击就几近刺透他的盾，但明铎扎克斯知道如何还击，他持盾猛击，让她失衡狠摔在地空门大开，无力抵御他的反击。最终，他在战场上遇到一名雇佣兵。此人头戴镀金青铜头盔，手持剑盾。两人足足拼斗了一个半小时。 明铎扎克斯使遍生平所学。每当佣兵紧绷左臂，他便停止进攻。每当他的对手高举剑刃，他也高举起盾并做出完美格挡。在漫长的军旅生涯中，他首度与另一位防反型战士对战。他们同样架势岿然，同样反应敏捷，且必要时都有连斗数日之能。偶有外人前来助阵，既有明铎扎克斯一方的也有对方的。这些闲杂人等很快就会被解决掉，而那双方的冠军便重回他们的决斗。 他们彼此周旋，盾来剑往，明铎扎克斯意识到，他终于遇上了完美的镜像。 这一场酣畅淋漓的血战变得越来越像一场游戏，一段舞蹈。他们彼此起舞着，直到明铎扎克斯终于自乱节奏，过早地攻击而失衡，这场双人舞即刻告终。时间好像减缓了。他凝视着而远非感受着，那佣兵的利刃从他的咽喉开始撕裂，贯穿到胸腔。一记优美的终结技。他也可以做到。 明铎扎克斯摔落在地，感受着生命的流逝。佣兵立于其侧，准备好为这令人敬佩的对手奉上他值得的终止痛苦的一击。于一个陌生人而言，这是一种奇怪却很荣耀的举动，明铎扎克斯被深深感动了。他最后听见的是穿越战场原而来的某人呼唤着的名字，一个和他很相似的名字。 “尤利费克斯！” 佣兵除去头盔，高声应答那呼唤。在他这么做的时候，明铎扎克斯从其头盔的接缝中看到了他自己在此人身上的倒影。那是如同他一般极近的眼距，红综的头发，宽而薄的双唇和平钝的下巴。在生命的最后一瞬，他对这镜像的相似程度大感惊奇，随后这位熟悉的陌生人旋身送上了终结一击。 尤利费克斯回到他的指挥官处，因当日大捷而得到丰厚的酬金。他们在先前敌军驻扎的那花园处安营休息，在明亮的星光下享用着热腾腾的佳肴。佣兵带着异常的沉默凝望此地。 “以前你来过这儿啊，尤利费克斯？”一个曾雇过他的部落人问。 “我生来就是骑手，像你一样。母亲在我还是婴儿时就把我卖给了别人。我总想，要是我没被卖掉，我的人生会有什么不同。我可能压根不会成为佣兵。” “左右我们命运的事物太多了。”一边的女巫说，“若你老是想着无数的如果和选择的变化，最后肯定发疯。没有人会和你一模一样，比较这些很愚蠢。” “但还真的有一个，”尤利费克斯眺望天星，“我前主人在放我自由前，告诉我其实我母亲生的是双胞胎兄弟，然而她只能养大一人……但什么地方，的确有一个极其像我的人。我的兄弟。真希望能见见他。” 女术士看见了眼前的鬼魂，随即明白了这对双胞胎兄弟已曾相见的真相。她保持沉默，静静凝望着跳跃的篝火，驱散着脑海中的思绪。她很明智，没有把这一切吐露出来。

english: <font face='$HandwrittenFont'> Description of target: Bosmer male, goes by Malborn. Believed to be working for the Blades, so approach with caution. He has inside knowledge of our procedures, so he will be warier than usual. Malborn is not his real name - he's now been identified as a survivor of a family of traitors who were all believed to have died in a fire in Falinesti. Do not risk him evading us. He's likely trying to leave Skyrim. Make sure of your kill, and do not implicate us. </font>
chinese: <font face='$HandwrittenFont'> 目标描述： 波斯莫男性，名马伯恩。应为刀锋会协助者，所以小心行事。 该目标知道我们的内部情况，所以会格外小心。马伯恩并非本名，现在已确认为叛徒家族的幸存者，该家族本已认定在法林纳斯提的火灾中身亡。 别冒险惊动，导致目标躲避我们。他可能试图离开天际。确保杀死目标，不要牵扯到我们。 </font>

english:
Daughter-
I may seem like a doting old fool, but I still don't like this. The world is a more dangerous place than you realize, and hunting for treasure is a fool's life. Tread carefully, and keep half an eye on Beem-Ja. He should be able to protect you, but do not trust him. Come home as soon as you've tired of this charade.
chinese:
女儿：
我可能看着确实像个宠溺过度的老傻瓜，但我还是不喜欢你这样。这个世界比你想的要危险得多，寻宝真的就是傻子才会选择的生活。要小心，也要留心毕姆-加，虽然他奉命保护你，但也不要相信他。等玩够了就回家吧。

english: 4th of Evening Star, 4E 200 It never ceases to amaze me how many fall for my simple trap. I may have to consider tweaking it a bit though. Many of them, in recent months, have been dying the second they hit the cage floor. Putting a foot or two of water in didn't help either. In fact, it may actually be making things worse. The last one to fall in broke both his arms and legs! He proceeded to float there and eventually drown. If it weren't for the water I would have had a great time twisting his mind until I decided to end him. 8th of Evening Star, 4E 200 Captured another one...finally. It's definitely true that whatever these Orcs gain in size and strength they lose in mental capacity. I'm amazed they can even speak. He kept saying that when he gets out he'll break me in half like a twig...persuasive isn't he? Anyway, I told him that if he holds his head under the water for as long as this candle wick lasts I'd let him go free. Stupid thing drowned within minutes! I never tire of this. 13th of Evening Star, 4E 200 I haven't captured a soul for days, and the last victim died on impact. Of course I was still able to reclaim his soul, but without the thrill of killing, or any kind of torture, the process lacks that special something. The reclaimed souls I've summoned make decent targets, but taking a mortal's life is so much more...sweet.
chinese: 4E200年，夜星月4日 我总是惊讶于这简单的陷阱是怎么抓到这么多人。不过我可能要稍微调整一下。近来几个月许多人一碰到笼子底部就被摔死了，灌进去一两尺的水也无济于事。事实上水可能让事变得更糟了。上一个掉进去的摔断了胳膊腿！结果就那么漂在那里淹死了。要是没有水，我就能很开心地扭曲他的思维，直到我决定了结了他。 4E200年，夜星月8日 终于又抓到一个。毫无疑问，这些兽人的体型和力量与心智是成反比的。他们居然会说话。他一直在喊，说等他出来，就会把我像树枝一样劈成两半……挺有说服力啊？总之，我告诉他，如果他把头扎水里，坚持到蜡烛烧完，我就放他自由。几分钟就淹死了！我从来都是乐此不疲。 4E200年，夜星月13日 好几天没抓到灵魂了。最后一个受害者是摔死的。当然，我还是能收回他的灵魂，但没有了杀戮的刺激和任何形式的折磨，这个过程就缺乏了那种特殊的东西。召唤被再生灵魂倒是不错的目标，但剥夺凡人的生命会更……甜蜜。

english:
[pagebreak]

The Song of Pelinal Volume 5: On His Love of Morihaus [pagebreak]

[Editor's Note: Volumes 1-6 are taken from the so-called Reman Manuscript located in the Imperial Library. It is a transcription of older fragments collected by an unknown scholar of the early Second Era. Beyond this, little is known of the original sources of these fragments, some of which appear to be from the same period (perhaps even from the same manuscript). But, as no scholarly consensus yet exists on dating these six fragments, no opinions will be offered here.] [pagebreak] t is a solid truth that Morihaus was the son of Kyne, but whether or not Pelinal was indeed the Shezarrine is best left unsaid (for once Plontinu, who favored the short sword, said it, and that night he was smothered by moths). It is famous, though, that the two talked of each other as family, with Morihaus as the lesser, and that Pelinal loved him and called him nephew, but these could be merely the fancies of immortals. Never did Pelinal counsel Morihaus in time of war, for the man-bull fought magnificently, and led men well, and never resorted to Madness, but the Whitestrake did warn against the growing love with Perrif. "We are ada, Mor, and change things through love. We must take care lest we beget more monsters on this earth. If you do not desist, she will take to you, and you will transform all Cyrod if you do this." And to this the bull became shy, for he was a bull, and he felt his form too ugly for the Parvania at all times, especially when she disrobed for him. He snorted, though, and shook his nose-hoop into the light of the Secunda moon and said, "She is like this shine on my nose-hoop here: an accident sometimes, but whenever I move my head at night, she is there. And so you know what you ask is impossible."
chinese:
[pagebreak]

佩林纳尔之歌 卷五：英雄之爱 [pagebreak]

[编者按：卷一至卷六取自帝国图书馆藏所谓《雷曼手稿》。这是由第二纪元早期一位不知名学者收集的古早残篇抄本。此外，这些残篇的原始来源鲜为人知，其中一些似乎来自同一时期（甚至来自同一手稿）。但因学界并未就六份残篇的确切年代达成共识，这里也就不会提供任何参考意见。] [pagebreak] 莫里豪斯是凯娜之子，这毋庸置疑。但佩林纳尔是否确定为舍扎因还是不谈为妙（曾有一次，那位喜欢用短剑的普隆提努谈过这个话题，结果他晚上就被飞蛾闷死了）。众所周知，两人视彼此为家人，莫里豪斯辈分低一些，佩林纳尔钟爱于他，呼为侄子。但这可能仅是人们对不朽者的幻想。战争期间佩林纳尔从不给莫里豪斯提建议，因为人牛作战英勇，领导出色，且从不陷入疯狂，但佩林纳尔确实警告了他和佩里芙之间日益增长的爱慕。“我们是阿达啊，莫尔，我们被爱引导着去改变一切。我们必须小心，以免在这片土地上造出更多的怪物。如果你不断了这念想，她会爱上你的。你这么做会改变希罗的一切。”公牛对此感到害羞，作为一头公牛他总是认为自己丑陋的配不上帕拉万妮亚，尤其是她为自己宽衣解带的时候。他哼了一声，对着瑟坤达的月光摇了摇鼻环，说道：“她就像我鼻环上的这道光：虽然偶有意外，但每次我在夜里转过头去，她便在我眼前。你所说的我办不到。”
englishNormalized:
[pagebreak]

the song of pelinal volume 5: on his love of morihaus [pagebreak]

[editor's note: volumes 1-6 are taken from the so-called reman manuscript located in the imperial library. it is a transcription of older fragments collected by an unknown scholar of the early second era. beyond this, little is known of the original sources of these fragments, some of which appear to be from the same period (perhaps even from the same manuscript). but, as no scholarly consensus yet exists on dating these six fragments, no opinions will be offered here.] [pagebreak] t is a solid truth that morihaus was the son of kyne, but whether or not pelinal was indeed the shezarrine is best left unsaid (for once plontinu, who favored the short sword, said it, and that night he was smothered by moths). it is famous, though, that the two talked of each other as family, with morihaus as the lesser, and that pelinal loved him and called him nephew, but these could be merely the fancies of immortals. never did pelinal counsel morihaus in time of war, for the man-bull fought magnificently, and led men well, and never resorted to madness, but the whitestrake did warn against the growing love with perrif. "we are ada, mor, and change things through love. we must take care lest we beget more monsters on this earth. if you do not desist, she will take to you, and you will transform all cyrod if you do this." and to this the bull became shy, for he was a bull, and he felt his form too ugly for the parvania at all times, especially when she disrobed for him. he snorted, though, and shook his nose-hoop into the light of the secunda moon and said, "she is like this shine on my nose-hoop here: an accident sometimes, but whenever i move my head at night, she is there. and so you know what you ask is impossible."

It is a transcription of older fragments collected by an unknown scholar of the early Second Era. Beyond this, little is known of the original sources of these fragments, some of which appear to be from the same period (perhaps even from the same manuscript). But, as no scholarly consensus yet exists on dating these six fragments, no opinions will be offered here.] [pagebreak] t is a solid truth that Morihaus was the son of Kyne, but whether or not Pelinal was indeed the Shezarrine is best left unsaid (for once Plontinu, who favored the short sword, said it, and that night he was smothered by moths). It is famous, though, that the two talked of each other as family, with Morihaus as the lesser, and that Pelinal loved him and called him nephew, but these could be merely the fancies of immortals. Never did Pelinal counsel Morihaus in time of war, for the man-bull fought magnificently, and led men well, and never resorted to Madness, but the Whitestrake did warn against the growing love with Perrif. "We are ada, Mor, and change things through love. We must take care lest we beget more monsters on this earth. If you do not desist, she will take to you, and you will transform all Cyrod if you do this." And to this the bull became shy, for he was a bull, and he felt his form too ugly for the Parvania at all times, especially when she disrobed for him. He snorted, though, and shook his nose-hoop into the light of the Secunda moon and said, "She is like this shine on my nose-hoop here: an accident sometimes, but whenever I move my head at night, she is there. And so you know what you ask is impossible." Chinese: [pagebreak]

english:
[pagebreak]

Wabbajack

[pagebreak]

ittle boys shouldn't summon up the forces of eternal darkness unless they have an adult supervising, I know, I know. But on that sunny night on the 5th of First Seed, I didn't want an adult. I wanted Hermaeus Mora, the daedra of knowledge, learning, gums, and varnishes. You see, I was told by a beautiful, large breasted man who lived under the library in my home town that the 5th of First Seed was Hermaeus Mora's night. And if I wanted the Oghma Infinium, the book of knowledge, I had to summon him. When you're the new king of Solitude, every bit of knowledge helps. Normally, you need a witches coven, or a mages guild, or at least matching pillow case and sheets to invoke a prince of Oblivion. The Man Under the Library showed me how to do it myself. He told me to wait until the storm was at its height before shaving the cat. I've forgotten the rest of the ceremony. It doesn't matter. Someone appeared who I thought was Hermaeus Mora. The only thing that made me somewhat suspicious was Hermaeus Mora, from what I read, was a big blobby multi-eyed clawed monstrosity, and this guy looked like a waistcoated banker. Also, he kept calling himself Sheogorath, not Hermaeus Mora. Still, I was so happy to have successfully summoned Hermaeus Mora, these inconsistencies did not bother me. He had me do some things that didn't make any sense to me (beyond the mortal scope, breadth, and ken, I suppose), and then his servant happily gave me something he called the Wabbajack. Wabbajack. Wabbajack. Wabbajack. Wabbajack. Wabbajack. Wabbajack. Wabbajack. Wabbajack. Wabbajack. Maybe the Wabbajack is the Book of Knowledge. Maybe I'm smarter because I know cats can be bats can be rats can be hats can be gnats can be thats can be thises. And that doors can be boars can be snores can be floors can be roars can be spores can be yours can be mine. I must be smart, for the interconnective system is very clear to me. Then why, or wherefore do people keep calling me mad? Wabbajack. Wabbajack. Wabbajack.
chinese:
[pagebreak]

瓦巴杰克

[pagebreak]

小屁孩儿就不该召唤永恒黑暗的力量，除非他们有个大人给看着，我懂，我懂。不过在初种月五日那个阳光灿烂的晚上，我可不想要什么大人。我想要的是知识、学习、树胶与清漆之迪德拉，赫麦尤斯·莫拉。你看嘛，有个住我乡下那片儿的图书馆底下的大胸美男跟我说，初种月五日是赫麦尤斯·莫拉之夜。我要是想拿知识圣书《无限智典》，就非得召唤他不可。等你做了独孤城新王，这些知识都可有用了。 通常来讲，需要找女巫团或者说法师公会，又或者最起码一些配套的枕套和床单来召唤湮灭君王。而图书馆底下那人却教了我怎样自个儿搞。他跟我讲，等到风暴上了最高潮那会就把一只猫的毛儿给剃刮个干净。这仪式后头剩下要干嘛来着我给忘了。不过这没啥关系。 有个我觉着是赫麦尤斯·莫拉的家伙冒出来了。唯一叫我有点儿怀疑的是，我之前读到的赫麦尤斯·莫拉是个长着一堆眼睛和爪子的大怪物，可这家伙看着就像个裹马甲的银行佬。另外，他一直管自己叫“谢尔格拉”，而非“赫麦尤斯·莫拉”。虽说是这样，能成功召唤了赫麦尤斯·莫拉我还是很高兴的，这点矛盾可没法叫我伤脑筋。他叫我干了些对我来说一点意义都没有的事（它们超出了凡人的范围、宽度和认知范围），之后他的仆人高高兴兴地给了我一件东西，他管它叫瓦巴杰克。瓦巴杰克。瓦巴杰克。 瓦巴杰克。 瓦巴杰克、瓦巴杰克、瓦巴杰克。 瓦巴杰克、瓦巴杰克、瓦巴杰克。 说不定瓦巴杰克就是那本知识之书。说不定我变聪明了，因为我晓得猫可以是蝙蝠可以是老鼠可以是帽子可以是蚊虫可以是那啥可以是这啥。还有大门可以是野猪可以是呼噜可以是地板可以是咆哮可以是孢子可以是你的可以是我的。我肯定聪明得紧了，因为万物间联系的系统我已经再清楚不过了。可为什么，到底为什么，人们总叫我疯子呢？ 瓦巴杰克、瓦巴杰克、瓦巴杰克。
