瓦巴杰克

[pagebreak]

小屁孩儿就不该召唤永恒黑暗的力量，除非他们有个大人给看着，我懂，我懂。不过在初种月五日那个阳光灿烂的晚上，我可不想要什么大人。我想要的是知识、学习、树胶与清漆之迪德拉，赫麦尤斯·莫拉。你看嘛，有个住我乡下那片儿的图书馆底下的大胸美男跟我说，初种月五日是赫麦尤斯·莫拉之夜。我要是想拿知识圣书《无限智典》，就非得召唤他不可。等你做了独孤城新王，这些知识都可有用了。 通常来讲，需要找女巫团或者说法师公会，又或者最起码一些配套的枕套和床单来召唤湮灭君王。而图书馆底下那人却教了我怎样自个儿搞。他跟我讲，等到风暴上了最高潮那会就把一只猫的毛儿给剃刮个干净。这仪式后头剩下要干嘛来着我给忘了。不过这没啥关系。 有个我觉着是赫麦尤斯·莫拉的家伙冒出来了。唯一叫我有点儿怀疑的是，我之前读到的赫麦尤斯·莫拉是个长着一堆眼睛和爪子的大怪物，可这家伙看着就像个裹马甲的银行佬。另外，他一直管自己叫“谢尔格拉”，而非“赫麦尤斯·莫拉”。虽说是这样，能成功召唤了赫麦尤斯·莫拉我还是很高兴的，这点矛盾可没法叫我伤脑筋。他叫我干了些对我来说一点意义都没有的事（它们超出了凡人的范围、宽度和认知范围），之后他的仆人高高兴兴地给了我一件东西，他管它叫瓦巴杰克。瓦巴杰克。瓦巴杰克。 瓦巴杰克。 瓦巴杰克、瓦巴杰克、瓦巴杰克。 瓦巴杰克、瓦巴杰克、瓦巴杰克。 说不定瓦巴杰克就是那本知识之书。说不定我变聪明了，因为我晓得猫可以是蝙蝠可以是老鼠可以是帽子可以是蚊虫可以是那啥可以是这啥。还有大门可以是野猪可以是呼噜可以是地板可以是咆哮可以是孢子可以是你的可以是我的。我肯定聪明得紧了，因为万物间联系的系统我已经再清楚不过了。可为什么，到底为什么，人们总叫我疯子呢？ 瓦巴杰克、瓦巴杰克、瓦巴杰克。
agentText:
Addon: Skyrim EDID: Book2CommonWabbajack Record: BOOK:DESC SID: 004A69 English: [pagebreak]

Wabbajack

[pagebreak]

ittle boys shouldn't summon up the forces of eternal darkness unless they have an adult supervising, I know, I know. But on that sunny night on the 5th of First Seed, I didn't want an adult. I wanted Hermaeus Mora, the daedra of knowledge, learning, gums, and varnishes. You see, I was told by a beautiful, large breasted man who lived under the library in my home town that the 5th of First Seed was Hermaeus Mora's night. And if I wanted the Oghma Infinium, the book of knowledge, I had to summon him. When you're the new king of Solitude, every bit of knowledge helps. Normally, you need a witches coven, or a mages guild, or at least matching pillow case and sheets to invoke a prince of Oblivion. The Man Under the Library showed me how to do it myself. He told me to wait until the storm was at its height before shaving the cat. I've forgotten the rest of the ceremony. It doesn't matter. Someone appeared who I thought was Hermaeus Mora. The only thing that made me somewhat suspicious was Hermaeus Mora, from what I read, was a big blobby multi-eyed clawed monstrosity, and this guy looked like a waistcoated banker. Also, he kept calling himself Sheogorath, not Hermaeus Mora. Still, I was so happy to have successfully summoned Hermaeus Mora, these inconsistencies did not bother me. He had me do some things that didn't make any sense to me (beyond the mortal scope, breadth, and ken, I suppose), and then his servant happily gave me something he called the Wabbajack. Wabbajack. Wabbajack. Wabbajack. Wabbajack. Wabbajack. Wabbajack. Wabbajack. Wabbajack. Wabbajack. Maybe the Wabbajack is the Book of Knowledge. Maybe I'm smarter because I know cats can be bats can be rats can be hats can be gnats can be thats can be thises. And that doors can be boars can be snores can be floors can be roars can be spores can be yours can be mine. I must be smart, for the interconnective system is very clear to me. Then why, or wherefore do people keep calling me mad? Wabbajack. Wabbajack. Wabbajack. Chinese: [pagebreak]

瓦巴杰克

[pagebreak]

小屁孩儿就不该召唤永恒黑暗的力量，除非他们有个大人给看着，我懂，我懂。不过在初种月五日那个阳光灿烂的晚上，我可不想要什么大人。我想要的是知识、学习、树胶与清漆之迪德拉，赫麦尤斯·莫拉。你看嘛，有个住我乡下那片儿的图书馆底下的大胸美男跟我说，初种月五日是赫麦尤斯·莫拉之夜。我要是想拿知识圣书《无限智典》，就非得召唤他不可。等你做了独孤城新王，这些知识都可有用了。 通常来讲，需要找女巫团或者说法师公会，又或者最起码一些配套的枕套和床单来召唤湮灭君王。而图书馆底下那人却教了我怎样自个儿搞。他跟我讲，等到风暴上了最高潮那会就把一只猫的毛儿给剃刮个干净。这仪式后头剩下要干嘛来着我给忘了。不过这没啥关系。 有个我觉着是赫麦尤斯·莫拉的家伙冒出来了。唯一叫我有点儿怀疑的是，我之前读到的赫麦尤斯·莫拉是个长着一堆眼睛和爪子的大怪物，可这家伙看着就像个裹马甲的银行佬。另外，他一直管自己叫“谢尔格拉”，而非“赫麦尤斯·莫拉”。虽说是这样，能成功召唤了赫麦尤斯·莫拉我还是很高兴的，这点矛盾可没法叫我伤脑筋。他叫我干了些对我来说一点意义都没有的事（它们超出了凡人的范围、宽度和认知范围），之后他的仆人高高兴兴地给了我一件东西，他管它叫瓦巴杰克。瓦巴杰克。瓦巴杰克。 瓦巴杰克。 瓦巴杰克、瓦巴杰克、瓦巴杰克。 瓦巴杰克、瓦巴杰克、瓦巴杰克。 说不定瓦巴杰克就是那本知识之书。说不定我变聪明了，因为我晓得猫可以是蝙蝠可以是老鼠可以是帽子可以是蚊虫可以是那啥可以是这啥。还有大门可以是野猪可以是呼噜可以是地板可以是咆哮可以是孢子可以是你的可以是我的。我肯定聪明得紧了，因为万物间联系的系统我已经再清楚不过了。可为什么，到底为什么，人们总叫我疯子呢？ 瓦巴杰克、瓦巴杰克、瓦巴杰克。

english:
[pagebreak]

The Dragon Break Reexamined by Fal Droon

[pagebreak]

he late 3rd era was a period of remarkable religious ferment and creativity. The upheavals of the reign of Uriel VII were only the outward signs of the historical forces that would eventually lead to the fall of the Septim Dynasty. The so called "Dragon Break" was first proposed at this time, by a wide variety of cults and fringe sects across the Empire, connected only by a common obsession with the events surrounding Tiber Septim's rise to power -- the "founding myth," if you will, of the Septim Dynasty. The basis of the Dragon Break doctrine is now known to be a rather prosaic error in the timeline printed in the otherwise authoritative "Encyclopedia Tamrielica," first published in 3E 12, during the early years of Tiber Septim's reign. At that time, the archives of Alinor were still inaccessible to human scholars, and the extant records from the Alessian period were extremely fragmentary. The Alessians had systematically burned all the libraries they could find, and their own records were largely destroyed during the War of Righteousness. The author of the Encyclopedia Tamrielica was apparently unfamiliar with the Alessian "year," which their priesthood used to record all dates. We now know this refers to the length of the long vision-trances undertaken by the High Priestess, which might last anywhere from a few weeks to several months. Based on analysis of the surviving trance scrolls, as well as murals and friezes from Alessian temples, I estimate that the Alessian Order actually lasted only about 150 years, rather than the famous "one thousand and eight years" given by the Encyclopedia Tamrielica. The "mystery" of the millennial-plus rule of the Alessians was accepted but unexplained until the spread of the Lorkhan cults in the late 3rd era, when the doctrine of the Dragon Break took hold. Because this dating (and explanation) was so widely held at the time, and then repeated by historians down through today, it has come to have the force of tradition. Recall, however, that the 3rd era historians were already separated from the Alessians by a gulf of more than 2,000 years. And history was still in its infancy, relying on the few archives from those early days. Today, modern archaeology and paleonumerology have confirmed what my own research in Alessian dating first suggested: that the Dragon Break was invented in the late 3rd era, based on a scholarly error, fueled by obsession with eschatology and Numidiumism, and perpetuated by scholarly inertia.
chinese:
[pagebreak]

龙破再调查 法尔·杜恩 著

[pagebreak]

第三纪元末期显然是信仰动荡与创新的时期，尤瑞尔七世统治时期的剧变只是最终导致塞普汀王朝崩塌的历史性力量的外在迹象。帝国内各种异教、边缘教派最早在此时提出了所谓的“龙破”，与之相关的是围绕泰伯·塞普汀崛起掌权的一系列事件——你愿意的话可称之为塞普汀王朝的“开国神话”。 如今在权威的《泰姆瑞尔百科全书》（首次出版于3E 12，即泰伯·塞普汀的统治前期）时间轴里，龙破学说的要点已被认为是再乏味不过的谬误了。那时艾琳诺的档案仍不对人类学者们开放，而现存阿莱西亚时期的记录极为零碎。阿莱西亚教团有组织地烧毁了能找到的所有图书馆，而他们自身的档案也在正义之战中大量被毁。 《泰姆瑞尔百科全书》的作者看来是不熟悉阿莱西亚教团的“年”的概念，即他们的祭司用以记录所有日期的单位。现在我们清楚这是指代高级女祭司进行长时幻视冥想的时段，持续时间从几周到几个月。根据对现存冥想卷轴及阿莱西亚教神殿壁画、条幅的分析，我估计阿莱西亚教团实际只维持了约150年，而不是由《泰姆瑞尔百科全书》给出的知名度极高的“1008年”。虽然被接受了，但直至第三纪元末期，随着洛克汗教派的传播与龙破学说的建立，阿莱西亚教团千年统治之谜才得以解释。因为这个纪年法（与解释）在当时广为接受，又有历史学者复述，到了今天它早已有其承袭而来的力量。然而请回想一下，第三纪元的历史学者与阿莱西亚教团之间隔着一条两千多年的鸿沟。当时的历史研究仍处于起步阶段，只有早期数目极少的档案资料。 如今，现代考古学与古生物学已经证实了我在阿莱西亚纪年研究所表明的：龙破是一个首次于3E末期提出的，由对来世论及纳米迪安的痴迷推动的学术谬误，并被学术界惰性地长久延续下来。

english:
[pagebreak]

The Book of Daedra [pagebreak]

[pagebreak] zura, whose sphere is dusk and dawn, the magic in-between realms of twilight, known as Moonshadow, Mother of the Rose, and Queen of the Night Sky. [pagebreak] oethiah, whose sphere is deceit and conspiracy, and the secret plots of murder, assassination, treason, and unlawful overthrow of authority. [pagebreak] lavicus Vile, whose sphere is the granting of power and wishes through ritual invocations and pact. [pagebreak] ermaeus Mora, whose sphere is scrying of the tides of Fate, of the past and future as read in the stars and heavens, and in whose dominion are the treasures of knowledge and memory. [pagebreak] ircine, whose sphere is the Hunt, the Sport of Daedra, the Great Game, the Chase, known as the Huntsman and the Father of Manbeasts. [pagebreak] alacath, whose sphere is the patronage of the spurned and ostracized, the keeper of the Sworn Oath, and the Bloody Curse. [pagebreak] ehrunes Dagon, whose sphere is Destruction, Change, Revolution, Energy, and Ambition. [pagebreak] ephala, whose sphere is obscured to mortals; known by the names Webspinner, Spinner, and Spider; whose only consistent theme seems to be interference in the affairs of mortals for her amusement. [pagebreak] eridia, whose sphere is obscured to mortals; who is associated with the energies of living things. [pagebreak] olag Bal, whose sphere is the domination and enslavement of mortals; whose desire is to harvest the souls of mortals and to bring mortal souls within his sway by spreading seeds of strife and discord in the mortal realms. [pagebreak] amira, whose sphere is the ancient Darkness; known as the Spirit Daedra, ruler of sundry dark and shadowy spirits; associated with spiders, insects, slugs, and other repulsive creatures which inspire mortals with an instinctive revulsion. [pagebreak] octurnal, whose sphere is the night and darkness; who is known as the Night Mistress. [pagebreak] eryite, whose sphere is the ordering of the lowest orders of Oblivion, known as the Taskmaster. [pagebreak] anguine, whose sphere is hedonistic revelry and debauchery, and passionate indulgences of darker natures. [pagebreak] heogorath, whose sphere is Madness, and whose motives are unknowable. [pagebreak] aernima, whose sphere is the realm of dreams and nightmares, and from whose realm issues forth evil omens. [pagebreak] [Especially marked for special interest under the heading "Malacath" you find a reference to SCOURGE, blessed by Malacath, and dedicated to the use of mortals. In short, the reference suggests that any Daedra attempting to invoke the weapon's powers will be expelled into the voidstreams of Oblivion.] "Of the legendary artifacts of the Daedra, many are well known, like Azura's Star, and Sheogorath's Wabbajack. Others are less well known, like Scourge, Mackkan's Hammer, Bane of Daedra...." "...yet though Malacath blessed Scourge to be potent against his Daedra kin, he thought not that it should fall into Daedric hands, then to serve as a tool for private war among caitiff and forsaken. Thus did Malacath curse the device such that, should any dark kin seek to invoke its powers, that a void should open and swallow that Daedra, and purge him into Oblivion's voidstreams, from thence to pathfind back to the Real and Unreal Worlds in the full order of time."
chinese:
[pagebreak]

迪德拉之书 [pagebreak]

[pagebreak] 阿祖拉，被世人奉为“月影”、“玫瑰之母”、“夜空女王”。其领域是介于黄昏与黎明间的奇妙暮光之域。 [pagebreak] 波耶西亚，活跃于诡计、串通还有那些关于谋害、暗杀、叛变、政变的密谋。 [pagebreak] 卡拉威库斯·维尔，执掌那些经由仪式的祈祷、契约所带来的力量、愿望的授予、实现。 [pagebreak] 赫麦尤斯·莫拉，职司命运潮汐——从群星、诸天和位于其领域内的知识记忆宝库中，解读过去与未来的占卜。 [pagebreak] 海尔辛，被称为“狩猎者”、“人兽之父”。其钟情于狩猎、迪德拉的野外运动、致命游戏、追猎。 [pagebreak] 玛拉凯斯，专注于对被唾弃者与被放逐者的庇护，是誓言与血咒的监管者。 [pagebreak] 梅鲁涅斯·大衮，其倾向于毁灭、更替、变革、活力与野心。 [pagebreak] 梅法拉，以“织网者”、“纺织者”、“蜘蛛”之名而被熟知。但其感兴趣的事物却让凡人们费解，仅知晓其一贯的主题似乎就只是通过干涉凡人们的事务取乐。 [pagebreak] 美瑞蒂亚，其偏爱的事物也是凡人们不甚了解的，仅明白其与活物的活力有关联。 [pagebreak] 莫拉格·巴尔，其热衷于对凡人们的统治与奴役，渴望收集凡人们的灵魂。因此，其通过在凡间散播纷争与不和的种子，将凡人们的灵魂带进他的支配之中。 [pagebreak] 娜米拉，被人们认为是“灵体迪德拉”，是各式各样的黑暗与幽暗之灵的统治者。其掌管着远古的黑暗，与蜘蛛、虫豸、蛞蝓等其他能引起凡人们本能厌恶的恶心生物有关联。 [pagebreak] 诺克图娜尔，其主宰黑夜与黑暗，亦被他人称作黑夜的女主人。 [pagebreak] 魄伊特，被大伙称作“工头”，其掌控着湮灭最底层秩序的安排。 [pagebreak] 桑吉恩，其痴迷于享乐的狂欢、淫逸与深暗本性下的狂热放纵。 [pagebreak] 谢尔格拉，其表现为无止尽的疯狂，因此其行为动机永远是不可知的。 [pagebreak] 瓦尔迷纳，其管辖着美梦与噩梦之域，以及从其领域流出的邪恶预兆。 [pagebreak] 【在“玛拉凯斯”标题下特意标记为特殊兴趣的地方，你可以找到提及灾祸的文献，这是一把受玛拉凯斯祝福、献给凡人使用的武器。简而言之，该文献暗示任何试图唤起此武器力量的迪德拉都将被放逐到湮灭的虚空流之中。】 “在众多的迪德拉传奇器物之中，有许多是众所周知的——像阿祖拉之星、谢尔格拉的瓦巴杰克。但其他的就不太知名了——像灾祸、玛克肯之锤、迪德拉之灾……” “……纵然受玛拉凯斯祝福的灾祸对他的迪德拉亲族有着极大的威胁，但他还是认为它不应该落入迪德拉的手中，然后成为劣民与弃民之间私斗的工具。因此玛拉凯斯便对这把武器降下诅咒——一旦有任何黑暗亲族想要唤起它的力量，虚空就会打开并将那迪德拉吞下、放逐到湮灭的虚空流之中，且从此刻起，其将在时间的完整秩序里，从真实与虚构的世界中寻找归路。”

[pagebreak] [Especially marked for special interest under the heading "Malacath" you find a reference to SCOURGE, blessed by Malacath, and dedicated to the use of mortals. In short, the reference suggests that any Daedra attempting to invoke the weapon's powers will be expelled into the voidstreams of Oblivion.] "Of the legendary artifacts of the Daedra, many are well known, like Azura's Star, and Sheogorath's Wabbajack. Others are less well known, like Scourge, Mackkan's Hammer, Bane of Daedra...." "...yet though Malacath blessed Scourge to be potent against his Daedra kin, he thought not that it should fall into Daedric hands, then to serve as a tool for private war among caitiff and forsaken. Thus did Malacath curse the device such that, should any dark kin seek to invoke its powers, that a void should open and swallow that Daedra, and purge him into Oblivion's voidstreams, from thence to pathfind back to the Real and Unreal Worlds in the full order of time." Chinese: [pagebreak]

迪德拉之书 [pagebreak]

[pagebreak] 阿祖拉，被世人奉为“月影”、“玫瑰之母”、“夜空女王”。其领域是介于黄昏与黎明间的奇妙暮光之域。 [pagebreak] 波耶西亚，活跃于诡计、串通还有那些关于谋害、暗杀、叛变、政变的密谋。 [pagebreak] 卡拉威库斯·维尔，执掌那些经由仪式的祈祷、契约所带来的力量、愿望的授予、实现。 [pagebreak] 赫麦尤斯·莫拉，职司命运潮汐——从群星、诸天和位于其领域内的知识记忆宝库中，解读过去与未来的占卜。 [pagebreak] 海尔辛，被称为“狩猎者”、“人兽之父”。其钟情于狩猎、迪德拉的野外运动、致命游戏、追猎。 [pagebreak] 玛拉凯斯，专注于对被唾弃者与被放逐者的庇护，是誓言与血咒的监管者。 [pagebreak] 梅鲁涅斯·大衮，其倾向于毁灭、更替、变革、活力与野心。 [pagebreak] 梅法拉，以“织网者”、“纺织者”、“蜘蛛”之名而被熟知。但其感兴趣的事物却让凡人们费解，仅知晓其一贯的主题似乎就只是通过干涉凡人们的事务取乐。 [pagebreak] 美瑞蒂亚，其偏爱的事物也是凡人们不甚了解的，仅明白其与活物的活力有关联。 [pagebreak] 莫拉格·巴尔，其热衷于对凡人们的统治与奴役，渴望收集凡人们的灵魂。因此，其通过在凡间散播纷争与不和的种子，将凡人们的灵魂带进他的支配之中。 [pagebreak] 娜米拉，被人们认为是“灵体迪德拉”，是各式各样的黑暗与幽暗之灵的统治者。其掌管着远古的黑暗，与蜘蛛、虫豸、蛞蝓等其他能引起凡人们本能厌恶的恶心生物有关联。 [pagebreak] 诺克图娜尔，其主宰黑夜与黑暗，亦被他人称作黑夜的女主人。 [pagebreak] 魄伊特，被大伙称作“工头”，其掌控着湮灭最底层秩序的安排。 [pagebreak] 桑吉恩，其痴迷于享乐的狂欢、淫逸与深暗本性下的狂热放纵。 [pagebreak] 谢尔格拉，其表现为无止尽的疯狂，因此其行为动机永远是不可知的。 [pagebreak] 瓦尔迷纳，其管辖着美梦与噩梦之域，以及从其领域流出的邪恶预兆。 [pagebreak] 【在“玛拉凯斯”标题下特意标记为特殊兴趣的地方，你可以找到提及灾祸的文献，这是一把受玛拉凯斯祝福、献给凡人使用的武器。简而言之，该文献暗示任何试图唤起此武器力量的迪德拉都将被放逐到湮灭的虚空流之中。】 “在众多的迪德拉传奇器物之中，有许多是众所周知的——像阿祖拉之星、谢尔格拉的瓦巴杰克。但其他的就不太知名了——像灾祸、玛克肯之锤、迪德拉之灾……” “……纵然受玛拉凯斯祝福的灾祸对他的迪德拉亲族有着极大的威胁，但他还是认为它不应该落入迪德拉的手中，然后成为劣民与弃民之间私斗的工具。因此玛拉凯斯便对这把武器降下诅咒——一旦有任何黑暗亲族想要唤起它的力量，虚空就会打开并将那迪德拉吞下、放逐到湮灭的虚空流之中，且从此刻起，其将在时间的完整秩序里，从真实与虚构的世界中寻找归路。”

english: [pagebreak]<font face='$HandwrittenFont'> I grow guilt-ridden in my old age. So many of the young sent to their deaths. All in the name of the Forsworn. All in the name of Madanach. My king. Who watches us from behind the iron bars of Cidhna Mine. How long have I served you? Since the uprising against the Nords? Was there ever a time when all that violence hasn't over-shadowed our destinies? What choice do I have but to do as I am instructed? </font>

Our only hope is Enthir, one of Karliah and Gallus's former acquaintances who is a wizard at the College of Winterhold.

english: <font face="$HandwrittenFont" size="20"> There's something unsettling about these caravan attacks. I expected bandits, but the remains we saw were literally torn apart, rent limb from limb. Yet no mere animal could coordinate an attack on a full-sized merchant train. And wolves don't take prisoners. We've found a trail leading up into the hills. We'll make camp here for the night, then set out at dawn. Whoever- whatever- murdered those people, we'll make sure it never happens again.
chinese: <font face="$HandwrittenFont" size="20"> 商队被袭击让人有些不安。我以为是强盗，但我们看到的尸体都被撕成碎片肢解了。然而，仅仅动物是无法协调攻击一队正常规模的商队的。而且狼也不会抓人。 我们发现了一条通往山上的小路。我们在这里扎营过夜，然后等清晨再出发。不管是谁或者什么东西杀了那些人，我们都必须保证这种事不再发生。
englishNormalized: <font face="$handwrittenfont" size="20"> there's something unsettling about these caravan attacks. i expected bandits, but the remains we saw were literally torn apart, rent limb from limb. yet no mere animal could coordinate an attack on a full-sized merchant train. and wolves don't take prisoners. we've found a trail leading up into the hills. we'll make camp here for the night, then set out at dawn. whoever- whatever- murdered those people, we'll make sure it never happens again.
chineseNormalized: <font face="$handwrittenfont" size="20"> 商队被袭击让人有些不安。我以为是强盗，但我们看到的尸体都被撕成碎片肢解了。然而，仅仅动物是无法协调攻击一队正常规模的商队的。而且狼也不会抓人。 我们发现了一条通往山上的小路。我们在这里扎营过夜，然后等清晨再出发。不管是谁或者什么东西杀了那些人，我们都必须保证这种事不再发生。
agentText:
Addon: Skyrim
EDID: dunPOIChillwindJournal1
Record: BOOK:DESC
SID: 004E81
English: <font face="$HandwrittenFont" size="20"> There's something unsettling about these caravan attacks. I expected bandits, but the remains we saw were literally torn apart, rent limb from limb. Yet no mere animal could coordinate an attack on a full-sized merchant train. And wolves don't take prisoners. We've found a trail leading up into the hills. We'll make camp here for the night, then set out at dawn. Whoever- whatever- murdered those people, we'll make sure it never happens again.
Chinese: <font face="$HandwrittenFont" size="20"> 商队被袭击让人有些不安。我以为是强盗，但我们看到的尸体都被撕成碎片肢解了。然而，仅仅动物是无法协调攻击一队正常规模的商队的。而且狼也不会抓人。 我们发现了一条通往山上的小路。我们在这里扎营过夜，然后等清晨再出发。不管是谁或者什么东西杀了那些人，我们都必须保证这种事不再发生。

english:
[pagebreak]

The Buying Game By Ababael Timsar-Dadisun

[pagebreak] [pagebreak]

So many people simply buy the items they need at the price they are given. It's a very sad state of affairs, when the game is really open to all, you don't need an invitation. And it is a game, the game of bargaining, to be played seriously and, I hasten to add, politely. In Elsweyr, it is common for the shop-owner to offer the prospective buyer tea or sweetmeats and engage in polite conversation before commencing the business. This eminently civilized tradition has a practical purpose, allowing the buyer to observe the wares for sale. It is considered impolite not to accept, though it does not imply obligation on the part of the buyer. Whether this particular custom is part of the culture or not, it's wise for the buyer and seller to greet one another with smiles and warm salutations, like gladiators honoring one another before the battle. Bargaining is expected all over Tamriel, but the game can be broken if one's offer is so preposterously low that it insults the shop-keeper. If you are offered something for ten gold pieces, try offering six and see where that takes you. Do not look like you're very interested, but do not mock the quality of the goods, even if they deserve it. Much better to admire the quality of workmanship, but comment that, regretfully, you simply cannot afford such a price. When the shop-keeper compliments your taste, smile, but try to resist the flattery. A lot of the game depends on recognizing the types of shop-keepers and not automatically assuming that the rural merchant is ignorant and easily fooled, or the rapacious city merchant is selling shoddy merchandise. Caravans, it should be mentioned, are always good places to go to buy or trade. Knowing what you're buying and from whom is a talent bought only after years of practice. Know the specialties of certain regions and merchants before you even step foot in a shop. Recognize too the prejudices of the region. In Morrowind where I hail from, for example, Argonians are viewed with a certain amount of suspicion. Don't be surprised or insulted if the shopkeepers follow you around the shop, assuming you're going to steal something. Similarly, Nords, Bretons, and Cyrodiils are sometimes treated coolly by merchants in the Summurset Isles. Of course, I don't know any shopkeepers anywhere, no matter their open- mindedness, who aren't alerted when a Khajiit or a Bosmer enters their shop. Even Khajiiti and Bosmeri shopkeepers. If you see something you really like or need, buy it then and there at the best price you can get. I cannot tell you how many times I passed up a rare and interesting relic, assuming that I could find it elsewhere in the region, perhaps at a larger town at a better price. Too late, I discovered I was wrong, and when I returned to the shop weeks later, the item I wanted was gone. Better to get a great purchase at a decent price and discover it again at a worse price than to miss out on your opportunities for ownership. Occasionally impulsiveness is the best buying strategy. Sense the moves of the game, and everyone can win.
chinese:
[pagebreak]

买卖博弈 阿达巴贝尔·蒂姆萨尔-达狄孙 著

[pagebreak] [pagebreak]

许多人们仅是按定价购买他们所需的物品。太可悲了，当这场博弈真正向所有人开放时，您就不需要获得邀请了。这是一场博弈，一场讨价还价的博弈，应该要严肃认真地参与其中，我得赶紧补充一点，还要有礼貌。在艾斯维尔，店主通常会在开始做生意前为潜在买家提供茶饮或糖果，并进行礼貌交谈。这种极其文明的传统具备一项实际目的，即让买家观察待售商品。不接受赠品是不礼貌的行为，但接受也并非是买家的义务。 无论这种特定习俗是否是文化的一部分，买卖双方报以笑容热情寒暄都是明智之举，好比角斗士在搏斗前彼此致敬。 讨价还价在泰姆瑞尔随处可见，但若一方报出特别荒谬的低价以至侮辱了店主，则博弈就此打破。若有人向您出价10金币，可尝试以6金币的价格还价，看看结果如何。 切勿让您看上去对商品深感兴趣，但也莫要嘲弄货物质量，即使它们本就如此。最好的做法是表达对商品做工的欣赏，但也遗憾的是您负担不起这样的价格。当店主称颂您的品味时，微笑，但尽量抵御他的阿谀奉承。 这类博弈很大程度上取决于能否识别店家的类型，而非自然而然地假设乡野货郎就真的无知蠢笨易于愚弄，或是贪婪的城镇商贾出售的一定是劣质产品。值得一提，大篷车商队，向来是购置或交易的好地方。 明白自己买的是什么、从谁那里买的才能，仅有历经多年实践方能具备。在您踏入商铺前，先了解地区和商店的特色。也要认识到种种地域偏见。举例，在我故乡晨风，人们对阿尔贡抱有一定程度的怀疑。倘若店主跟随身为阿尔贡的您一起逛商店，警惕您要偷盗，不要感到惊讶或羞辱。与之相似，诺德人、布莱顿人和希罗帝尔人有时也会遭到夏暮岛商人冷淡相待。诚然，我并非深知任何地方的任何店主，但不论他们思想有多开放，都不会在一名卡吉特或是波斯莫进入其店铺时放松警惕。甚至换作卡吉特和波斯莫商家亦如是。 倘若您看见您真正喜好或需求之物，一定要以最惠价当场买下。我都不知道自己错过多少次稀有而有趣的文物，就因为我猜想还能在该地区内其他地方寻得它，或许在更大的城镇里有更优的价格。等发现自己错了也为时晚矣，当我在数周后返回那商铺时，我想要的商品已经不见了。以合理价码购入心仪之物，然后以更贵的价码再次发现它，这种感觉总比错过机会好得多。偶发冲动的购买欲是最佳的购物策略。 感知博弈诸般动向，人人皆可得胜而归。

许多人们仅是按定价购买他们所需的物品。太可悲了，当这场博弈真正向所有人开放时，您就不需要获得邀请了。这是一场博弈，一场讨价还价的博弈，应该要严肃认真地参与其中，我得赶紧补充一点，还要有礼貌。在艾斯维尔，店主通常会在开始做生意前为潜在买家提供茶饮或糖果，并进行礼貌交谈。这种极其文明的传统具备一项实际目的，即让买家观察待售商品。不接受赠品是不礼貌的行为，但接受也并非是买家的义务。 无论这种特定习俗是否是文化的一部分，买卖双方报以笑容热情寒暄都是明智之举，好比角斗士在搏斗前彼此致敬。 讨价还价在泰姆瑞尔随处可见，但若一方报出特别荒谬的低价以至侮辱了店主，则博弈就此打破。若有人向您出价10金币，可尝试以6金币的价格还价，看看结果如何。 切勿让您看上去对商品深感兴趣，但也莫要嘲弄货物质量，即使它们本就如此。最好的做法是表达对商品做工的欣赏，但也遗憾的是您负担不起这样的价格。当店主称颂您的品味时，微笑，但尽量抵御他的阿谀奉承。 这类博弈很大程度上取决于能否识别店家的类型，而非自然而然地假设乡野货郎就真的无知蠢笨易于愚弄，或是贪婪的城镇商贾出售的一定是劣质产品。值得一提，大篷车商队，向来是购置或交易的好地方。 明白自己买的是什么、从谁那里买的才能，仅有历经多年实践方能具备。在您踏入商铺前，先了解地区和商店的特色。也要认识到种种地域偏见。举例，在我故乡晨风，人们对阿尔贡抱有一定程度的怀疑。倘若店主跟随身为阿尔贡的您一起逛商店，警惕您要偷盗，不要感到惊讶或羞辱。与之相似，诺德人、布莱顿人和希罗帝尔人有时也会遭到夏暮岛商人冷淡相待。诚然，我并非深知任何地方的任何店主，但不论他们思想有多开放，都不会在一名卡吉特或是波斯莫进入其店铺时放松警惕。甚至换作卡吉特和波斯莫商家亦如是。 倘若您看见您真正喜好或需求之物，一定要以最惠价当场买下。我都不知道自己错过多少次稀有而有趣的文物，就因为我猜想还能在该地区内其他地方寻得它，或许在更大的城镇里有更优的价格。等发现自己错了也为时晚矣，当我在数周后返回那商铺时，我想要的商品已经不见了。以合理价码购入心仪之物，然后以更贵的价码再次发现它，这种感觉总比错过机会好得多。偶发冲动的购买欲是最佳的购物策略。 感知博弈诸般动向，人人皆可得胜而归。
agentText:
Addon: Skyrim EDID: SkillSpeechcraft4 Record: BOOK:DESC SID: 004EEB English: [pagebreak]

The Buying Game By Ababael Timsar-Dadisun

[pagebreak] [pagebreak]

So many people simply buy the items they need at the price they are given. It's a very sad state of affairs, when the game is really open to all, you don't need an invitation. And it is a game, the game of bargaining, to be played seriously and, I hasten to add, politely. In Elsweyr, it is common for the shop-owner to offer the prospective buyer tea or sweetmeats and engage in polite conversation before commencing the business. This eminently civilized tradition has a practical purpose, allowing the buyer to observe the wares for sale. It is considered impolite not to accept, though it does not imply obligation on the part of the buyer. Whether this particular custom is part of the culture or not, it's wise for the buyer and seller to greet one another with smiles and warm salutations, like gladiators honoring one another before the battle. Bargaining is expected all over Tamriel, but the game can be broken if one's offer is so preposterously low that it insults the shop-keeper. If you are offered something for ten gold pieces, try offering six and see where that takes you. Do not look like you're very interested, but do not mock the quality of the goods, even if they deserve it. Much better to admire the quality of workmanship, but comment that, regretfully, you simply cannot afford such a price. When the shop-keeper compliments your taste, smile, but try to resist the flattery. A lot of the game depends on recognizing the types of shop-keepers and not automatically assuming that the rural merchant is ignorant and easily fooled, or the rapacious city merchant is selling shoddy merchandise. Caravans, it should be mentioned, are always good places to go to buy or trade. Knowing what you're buying and from whom is a talent bought only after years of practice. Know the specialties of certain regions and merchants before you even step foot in a shop. Recognize too the prejudices of the region. In Morrowind where I hail from, for example, Argonians are viewed with a certain amount of suspicion. Don't be surprised or insulted if the shopkeepers follow you around the shop, assuming you're going to steal something. Similarly, Nords, Bretons, and Cyrodiils are sometimes treated coolly by merchants in the Summurset Isles. Of course, I don't know any shopkeepers anywhere, no matter their open- mindedness, who aren't alerted when a Khajiit or a Bosmer enters their shop. Even Khajiiti and Bosmeri shopkeepers. If you see something you really like or need, buy it then and there at the best price you can get. I cannot tell you how many times I passed up a rare and interesting relic, assuming that I could find it elsewhere in the region, perhaps at a larger town at a better price. Too late, I discovered I was wrong, and when I returned to the shop weeks later, the item I wanted was gone. Better to get a great purchase at a decent price and discover it again at a worse price than to miss out on your opportunities for ownership. Occasionally impulsiveness is the best buying strategy. Sense the moves of the game, and everyone can win. Chinese: [pagebreak]

english: <font face="$HandwrittenFont"> PRIVATE PROPERTY OF SUVARIS ATHERON PLEASE RETURN TO HIM IN WINDHELM IF FOUND [pagebreak] Journal for month of Last Seed, year 201 of the 4th Era 1st of Last Seed Spoke with Master Torbjorn in the morning. Pleased with projects to increase competitiveness of shipping office. Spent afternoon overseeing offices and various paperworks. Moderate lunch and heavy dinner. 2nd of Last Seed Punished one of the dockworkers for laziness. Two days rations -- empty stomach means less good work for a few days, but he'll work hard from here on out. Moderate lunch and dinner. 3rd of Last Seed Correspondence with Uncle Mithorpa in Morrowind. Sadness continues to abound flowing west. Light lunch, no dinner. 4th of Last Seed Reports that our pirate friends are offering to let the Company ships through in exchange for gold. Will need to look into this. Heavy lunch and dinner. 5th of Last Seed Tried to get in to see Master Torbjorn about the potential issues, but he proved too busy tending to his wife and daughter in mourning. Did not wish to intrude. Skipped lunch (waiting at the Shatter-Shield house), moderate dinner. 6th of Last Seed Explained situation to Master Torbjorn. As usual he did not wish to know details, only how much money I needed to ensure smooth operations. I depart tomorrow for Dawnstar, hoping to catch the captain at the Windpeak for his crew's regular "festivities." Heavy lunch, skipped dinner in favor of packing. 7th of Last Seed Beginning journey. Plan to be back by the 12th. 15th of Last Seed Returned safely, though not unscathed. Bandit attacks delayed the initial travel, then Stig Salt-Plank proved to be more uncooperative than I had hoped. Chipped in some additional gold out of my own purse (some cleverness in the books will see it repaid), and the deal was done. Ate on the road, light meals all around. 16th of Last Seed Hoping word gets back to Stig's betters of our new arrangements before more Company ships can make it through. Found the Argonians had completely fouled up the operations in my absence. No surprise, there. Need to see to the old one's skooma supply, that should keep them motivated. Heavy lunch and dinner. </font>
chinese: <font face="$HandwrittenFont"> 苏瓦利斯·奥瑟伦的私人物品 如您捡到，请还回风舵城 [pagebreak] 第四纪元201年，末种月日记 末种月1日 早上与托布乔恩老板交谈。他对增加航运公司竞争力的项目感到高兴。 整个下午都在审查办公室和各种文件的工作。 午餐适量，晚餐丰盛。 末种月2日 惩罚了偷懒的码头工人。扣他两日份口粮，空腹意味着工作效率低下，但从现在起他会学会努力工作。 午餐、晚餐均适量。 末种月3日 晨风的米索帕叔叔来信。 悲伤继续向西蔓延。 午餐少量，未进晚餐。 末种月4日 有报告说我们的海盗朋友提出想让公司船只通过以换取金币。需要调查此事。 午餐、晚餐均丰盛。 末种月5日 想要和托布乔恩老板谈谈潜在的问题，但他却忙于照顾妻女，不想被打扰。 未进午餐（在碎盾宅等候），晚餐尚可。 末种月6日 向托布乔恩老板解释了情况。一如既往，他不想听细节，只想知道我需要多少资金来确保运作顺利。我明天就动身去晨星，希望能在风岳见到船长，参加他们例行的“庆祝活动”。 午餐丰盛，为了收拾行李，未进晚餐。 末种月7日 启程。计划12日返回。 末种月15日 虽然不算毫发无损，但好歹安全返回了。强盗的袭击推迟了最初的行程，“咸木板”斯蒂比我希望的还不配合。从我的腰包里掏出额外的金币（聪明人会在账本上看出回报）之后，交易就完成了。 路上吃的，都是便餐。 末种月16日 希望在更多的公司船只通过前，能从斯蒂那里获得新安排的消息。不出所料，我不在的时候，阿尔贡搞得一团糟。需要处理一下旧的斯库玛供应，这样才能让他们保持动力。 午餐、晚餐均丰盛。 </font>

Surfeit of Thieves by Aniis Noru

[pagebreak] [pagebreak]

"This looks interesting," said Indyk, his eyes narrowing to observe the black caravan making its way to the spires of the secluded castle. A gaudy, alien coat of arms marked each carriage, the lacquer glistening in the light of the moons. "Who do you suppose they are?" "They're obviously well-off," smiled his partner, Heriah. "Perhaps some new Imperial Cult dedicated to the acquisition of wealth?" "Go into town and find out what you can about the castle," said Indyk. "I'll see if I can learn anything about who these strangers are. We meet on this hill tomorrow night." Heriah had two great skills: picking locks and picking information. By dusk of the following day, she had returned to the hill. Indyk joined her an hour later. "The place is called Ald Olyra," she explained. "It dates back to the second era when a collection of nobles built it to protect themselves during one of the epidemics. They didn't want any of the diseased masses to get into their midst and spread the plague, so they built up quite a sophisticated security system for the time. Of course, it's mostly fallen into ruin, but I have a good idea about what kind of locks and traps might still be operational. What did you find out?" "I wasn't nearly so successful," frowned Indyk. "No one seemed to have any idea about the group, even that that there were here. I was about to give up, but at the charterhouse, I met a monk who said that his masters were a hermetic group called the Order of St. Eadnua. I talked to him for some time, this fellow name of Parathion, and it seems they're having some sort of ritual feast tonight." "Are they wealthy?" asked Heriah impatiently. "Embarrassingly so according to the fellow. But they're only at the castle for tonight." "I have my picks on me," winked Heriah. "Opportunity has smiled on us." She drew a diagram of the castle in the dirt: the main hall and kitchen were near the front gate, and the stables and secured armory were in the back. The thieves had a system that never failed. Heriah would find a way into the castle and collect as much loot as possible, while Indyk provided the distraction. He waited until his partner had scaled the wall before rapping on the gate. Perhaps this time he would be a bard, or a lost adventurer. The details were most fun to improvise. Heriah heard Indyk talking to the woman who came to the gate, but she was too far away to hear the words exchanged. He was evidently successful: a moment later, she heard the door shut. The man had charm, she would give him that. Only a few of the traps and locks to the armory had been set. Undoubtedly, many of the keys had been lost in time. Whatever servants had been in charge of securing the Order's treasures had brought a few new locks to affix. It took extra time to maneuver the intricate hasps and bolts of the new traps before proceeding to the old but still working systems, but Heriah found her heart beating with anticipation. Whatever lay beyond the door, she thought, must be of sufficient value to merit such protection. When at last the door swung quietly open, the thief found her avaricious dreams paled to reality. A mountain of golden treasure, ancient relics glimmering with untapped magicka, weaponry of matchless quality, gemstones the size of her fist, row after row of strange potions, and stacks of valuable documents and scrolls. She was so enthralled by the sight, she did not hear the man behind her approach. "You must be Lady Tressed," said the voice and she jumped. It was a monk in a black, hooded robe, intricately woven with silver and gold threads. For a moment, she could not speak. This was the sort of encounter that Indyk loved, but she could think to do nothing but nod her head with what she hoped looked like certainty. "I'm afraid I'm a little lost," she stammered. "I can see that," the man laughed. "That's the armory. I'll show you the way to the dining hall. We were afraid you weren't going to arrive. The feast is nearly over." Heriah followed the monk across the courtyard, to the double doors leading to the dining hall. A robe identical to the one he was wearing hung on a hook outside, and he handed it to her with a knowing smile. She slipped it on. She mimicked him as she lowered the hood over her head and entered the hall. Torches illuminated the figures within around the large table. Each wore the uniform black robe that covered all features, and from the look of things, the feast was over. Empty plates, platters, and glasses filled every inch of the wood with only the faintest spots and dribbles of the food remaining. It was a breaking of a fast it seemed. For a moment, Heriah stopped to think about poor, lost Lady Tressed who had missed her opportunity for gluttony. The only unusual item on the table was its centerpiece: a huge golden hourglass which was on its last minute's worth of sand. Though each person looked alike, some were sleeping, some were chatting merrily to one another, and one was playing a lute. Indyk's lute, she noticed, and then noticed Indyk's ring on the man's finger. Heriah was suddenly grateful for the anonymity of the hood. Perhaps Indyk would not realize that it was she, and that she had blundered. "Tressed," said the young man to the assembled, who turned as one to her and burst into applause. The conscious members of the Order arose to kiss her hand, and introduce themselves. "Nirdla." "Suelec." "Kyler." The names got stranger. "Toniop." "Htillyts." "Noihtarap." She could not help laughing: "I understand. It's all backwards. Your real names are Aldrin, Celeus, Relyk, Poinot, Styllith, Parathion." "Of course," said the young man. "Won't you have a seat?" "Sey," giggled Heriah, getting into the spirit of the masque and taking an empty chair. "I suppose that when the hourglass runs out, the backwards names go back to normal?" "That's correct, Tressed," said the woman next to her. "It's just one of our Order's little amusements. This castle seemed like the appropriately ironic venue for our feast, devised as it was to shun the plague victims who were, in their way, a walking dead." Heriah felt herself light-headed from the odor of the torches, and bumped into the sleeping man next to her. He fell face forward onto the table. "Poor Esruoc Tsrif," said a neighboring man, helping to prop the body up. "He's given us so much." Heriah stumbled to her feet and began walking uncertainly for the front gate. "Where are you going, Tressed?" asked one of the figures, his voice taking on an unpleasant mocking quality. "My name isn't Tressed," she mumbled, gripping Indyk's arm. "I'm sorry, partner. We need to go." The last crumb of sand fell in the hour glass as the man pulled back his hood. It was not Indyk. It was not even human, but a stretched grotesquerie of a man with hungry eyes and a wide mouth filled with tusk-like fangs. Heriah fell back into the chair of the figure they called Esruoc Tsrif. His hood fell open, revealing the pallid, bloodless face of Indyk. As she began to scream, they fell on her. In her last living moment, Heriah finally spelled "Tressed" backwards.

贪得无厌的盗贼 安尼斯·诺卢 著

[pagebreak] [pagebreak]

“有点意思，”英迪柯眯眼细细端详着那朝着隐僻城堡的尖顶缓缓行进的黑色大篷车。每厢都镶嵌着同样华丽繁复的异域纹章，它们的色漆闪烁着清冷的月光。“你猜猜看他们是谁？” “不管他们是谁，但肯定很有钱。” 他的搭档赫莱娅莞尔，“怕不是满脑子想着发财的帝国教会新成员吧？” “你进城去，尽可能打听打听这城堡的情况，”英迪柯说。“我来摸摸这群生面孔的底。明儿晚上咱在这山丘上碰头。” 赫莱娅身怀两大绝技：撬锁和收集情报。次日黄昏，她已返回小丘。英迪柯则在一小时后与她汇合。 “这地儿叫奥德·欧历拉，”她细细说着，“说远了能扯到第二纪元，一群贵族为了躲瘟疫建起来的。他们当然不想让哪个得了病的闯到他们中间传病，所以当时还造了很安保设施。不过嘛，现在基本上都废了，但我知道哪些锁和陷阱还能用。你有啥收获？” “我打听来的可没你那么详细，”英迪柯皱眉。“没人知道这伙是什么人，甚至压根没人知道他们来了这儿。我都想着算了吧，但我在修道院里见到个修道士，说他的头儿们是个秘密组织，叫做圣戴恩奥教团。我就跟那人聊了会，他叫帕拉西翁，好像他们今晚还有一场仪式盛宴。” “那这伙人有没有油水啊？”赫莱娅不耐烦地问。 “那家伙说有。但他们就今晚待在这城堡。” “我带了开锁器，”赫莱娅眨眨眼，“机遇正对着咱们微笑呢。” 她在泥地上画出城堡布局示意图：主厅和厨房在前门附近，而马厩和守备严密的军械库在其后方。这对大盗有着百试百灵的套路。赫莱娅探查出潜入城堡的通路，然后尽量搜带更多赃物，而在这期间英迪柯则帮她想方设法分散主人家的注意力。英迪柯等到搭档攀上围墙后叩敲前门。他这回要么扮作个吟游诗人，或是个迷路的冒险家。现编现造曲折的细节可有意思了。 赫莱娅模糊听见英迪柯和应门的女人交涉，但她相隔甚远，根本听不见交谈的内容。他看起来进展不错：没多久，赫莱娅听见关门声。这男人颇具魅力，那女人信了他的鬼话。 去军械库那的陷阱和锁具没多少还生效了。毋庸置疑，大部分的钥匙都成了历史的眼泪。不管是哪个仆人负责守护骑士团财富安全，都带来了数把新锁安上。她耗费了大量时间解除新装陷阱错综复杂的锁扣和制栓后，又要处理剩下那些老而弥坚的旧系统，但赫莱娅的心因期待而疯狂跳动。她想，门内的东西一定非常值钱，才值得这样保护。 当门安静敞开后，盗贼发现她穷尽想象力的期待幻想与眼前现实何止相形见绌。堆积如山的黄金财宝、闪烁着未知魔法的古代遗物、无与伦比的武器、拳头大小的宝石、成排的奇怪药水，还有珍贵的文件卷轴。她沉溺于眼前这一切，并未听到有人逼近她背后。 “想必您就是特莉丝德（Tressed）夫人吧。”一声话语惊得她跳了起来。 是个黑衣修士，穿着由繁复的金丝银线织成的连帽长袍。她一时哑口无言，这种情景英迪柯完全能放飞自我，但她压根想不出该讲什么，只好点头表现肯定。 “我恐怕是迷路了。”她结结巴巴。 “正如我所见。”他笑，“此处是军械库。我来领您去宴会厅。我们还担心您不来呢。宴会就要结束了。” 赫莱娅随修士经行庭院走到宴会厅的双开门前。与他同款的一件长袍挂于门外衣帽钩上，他会心一笑，把衣服递给她。她遂披上长袍，模仿修士拉低兜帽，步入宴会厅。 火把照亮了巨桌周遭众人。人人统一遮掩全身的黑袍制服，看起来宴席已终。空落落的大盘、浅碟和玻璃杯堆满木桌，几乎没剩下什么食物。宴会貌似是迅速结束的。有那么一会儿，赫莱娅停了下来想：可怜的、迷路的特莉丝德夫人错失了饱餐一顿的机会。 桌上唯一怪异之物是它的桌心饰品：一个剩余计量一分钟量、正往下渗漏着沙子的硕大黄金沙漏。 尽管每个人看着都很像，不过有些正酣睡，有些彼此聊得火热，还有个在奏鲁特琴。她注意到那是英迪柯的鲁特琴。她还留意到那人指上戴着英迪柯的戒指。赫莱娅突然很感激这隐藏每个人容貌的兜帽，英迪柯也许不会意识到这是她，而她竟犯了这么愚蠢的错误。 “特莉丝德，”那位年轻的黑衣修士对众人引荐，转身望向她鼓掌欢迎。 还清醒的骑士团成员们站起对她行吻手礼并自荐。 “尼尔德拉（Nirdla）。” “苏瓦雷克（Suelec）。” “凯尔（Kyler）。” 名号越发奇怪。 “托尼欧普（Toniop）。” “诃提尔特斯（Htillyts）。” “诺伊哈塔拉普（Noihtarap）。” 她没忍住大笑：“知道知道，都是倒着的。你们其实叫奥德印（Aldrin），西里尔斯（Celeus），瑞利克（Relyk），珀诺特（Poinot），斯泰林斯（Styllith）和帕拉西翁（Parathion）。” “没错，”年轻人说，“您不坐坐？” “嗯哼，”赫莱娅咯咯笑着打开脑中的假面剧开关，坐在某处空位上，“我猜猜，等沙漏漏干净了，那就该把这倒过来的名字正回去了吧？” “如您所言，特莉丝德，”她一侧的女人答，“这只是我们教团的一项小娱乐。这城堡貌似正适合作我们讽刺好笑的盛宴地点。它当初是设计为躲避那些瘟疫患者，用那种话来讲，那些行尸走肉。” 赫莱娅觉得火把的气味让她有点晕头转向，撞上旁边熟睡的男人，让他面朝前趴倒在桌上。 “可怜的埃斯鲁克·特利夫（Esruoc Tsrif），”邻座男人说着搀扶起他。“他馈赠给我们许多。” 赫莱娅踉踉跄跄地站起来，惶恐不安地向前门走去。 “想去哪呀，特莉丝德？”其中一人问，他的语调中令人不快的嘲笑意味毫无掩饰。 “我不叫特莉丝德，”她嘟囔着，握住英迪柯的手臂。“抱歉，伙计。我们该走了。” 最后点点沙粒漏入底部，而这男人拉下他的兜帽。他不是英迪柯。“他”甚至不是人。这怪人眼中写满饥饿，宽阔拉伸的巨嘴极其滑稽，口内犬牙交错。 赫莱娅跌跌撞撞坐回他们称作“埃斯鲁克·特利夫”那人的椅上。他的兜帽滑下，露出英迪柯苍白而毫无血色的脸。她惊声尖叫时，所有人都扑向她。 在她生命的最后一刹，赫莱娅总算把“特莉丝德”倒着拼了出来。

"that's correct, tressed," said the woman next to her. "it's just one of our order's little amusements. this castle seemed like the appropriately ironic venue for our feast, devised as it was to shun the plague victims who were, in their way, a walking dead." heriah felt herself light-headed from the odor of the torches, and bumped into the sleeping man next to her. he fell face forward onto the table. "poor esruoc tsrif," said a neighboring man, helping to prop the body up. "he's given us so much." heriah stumbled to her feet and began walking uncertainly for the front gate. "where are you going, tressed?" asked one of the figures, his voice taking on an unpleasant mocking quality. "my name isn't tressed," she mumbled, gripping indyk's arm. "i'm sorry, partner. we need to go." the last crumb of sand fell in the hour glass as the man pulled back his hood. it was not indyk. it was not even human, but a stretched grotesquerie of a man with hungry eyes and a wide mouth filled with tusk-like fangs. heriah fell back into the chair of the figure they called esruoc tsrif. his hood fell open, revealing the pallid, bloodless face of indyk. as she began to scream, they fell on her. in her last living moment, heriah finally spelled "tressed" backwards.

[pagebreak]

贪得无厌的盗贼 安尼斯·诺卢 著

[pagebreak] [pagebreak]

“有点意思，”英迪柯眯眼细细端详着那朝着隐僻城堡的尖顶缓缓行进的黑色大篷车。每厢都镶嵌着同样华丽繁复的异域纹章，它们的色漆闪烁着清冷的月光。“你猜猜看他们是谁？” “不管他们是谁，但肯定很有钱。” 他的搭档赫莱娅莞尔，“怕不是满脑子想着发财的帝国教会新成员吧？” “你进城去，尽可能打听打听这城堡的情况，”英迪柯说。“我来摸摸这群生面孔的底。明儿晚上咱在这山丘上碰头。” 赫莱娅身怀两大绝技：撬锁和收集情报。次日黄昏，她已返回小丘。英迪柯则在一小时后与她汇合。 “这地儿叫奥德·欧历拉，”她细细说着，“说远了能扯到第二纪元，一群贵族为了躲瘟疫建起来的。他们当然不想让哪个得了病的闯到他们中间传病，所以当时还造了很安保设施。不过嘛，现在基本上都废了，但我知道哪些锁和陷阱还能用。你有啥收获？” “我打听来的可没你那么详细，”英迪柯皱眉。“没人知道这伙是什么人，甚至压根没人知道他们来了这儿。我都想着算了吧，但我在修道院里见到个修道士，说他的头儿们是个秘密组织，叫做圣戴恩奥教团。我就跟那人聊了会，他叫帕拉西翁，好像他们今晚还有一场仪式盛宴。” “那这伙人有没有油水啊？”赫莱娅不耐烦地问。 “那家伙说有。但他们就今晚待在这城堡。” “我带了开锁器，”赫莱娅眨眨眼，“机遇正对着咱们微笑呢。” 她在泥地上画出城堡布局示意图：主厅和厨房在前门附近，而马厩和守备严密的军械库在其后方。这对大盗有着百试百灵的套路。赫莱娅探查出潜入城堡的通路，然后尽量搜带更多赃物，而在这期间英迪柯则帮她想方设法分散主人家的注意力。英迪柯等到搭档攀上围墙后叩敲前门。他这回要么扮作个吟游诗人，或是个迷路的冒险家。现编现造曲折的细节可有意思了。 赫莱娅模糊听见英迪柯和应门的女人交涉，但她相隔甚远，根本听不见交谈的内容。他看起来进展不错：没多久，赫莱娅听见关门声。这男人颇具魅力，那女人信了他的鬼话。 去军械库那的陷阱和锁具没多少还生效了。毋庸置疑，大部分的钥匙都成了历史的眼泪。不管是哪个仆人负责守护骑士团财富安全，都带来了数把新锁安上。她耗费了大量时间解除新装陷阱错综复杂的锁扣和制栓后，又要处理剩下那些老而弥坚的旧系统，但赫莱娅的心因期待而疯狂跳动。她想，门内的东西一定非常值钱，才值得这样保护。 当门安静敞开后，盗贼发现她穷尽想象力的期待幻想与眼前现实何止相形见绌。堆积如山的黄金财宝、闪烁着未知魔法的古代遗物、无与伦比的武器、拳头大小的宝石、成排的奇怪药水，还有珍贵的文件卷轴。她沉溺于眼前这一切，并未听到有人逼近她背后。 “想必您就是特莉丝德（tressed）夫人吧。”一声话语惊得她跳了起来。 是个黑衣修士，穿着由繁复的金丝银线织成的连帽长袍。她一时哑口无言，这种情景英迪柯完全能放飞自我，但她压根想不出该讲什么，只好点头表现肯定。 “我恐怕是迷路了。”她结结巴巴。 “正如我所见。”他笑，“此处是军械库。我来领您去宴会厅。我们还担心您不来呢。宴会就要结束了。” 赫莱娅随修士经行庭院走到宴会厅的双开门前。与他同款的一件长袍挂于门外衣帽钩上，他会心一笑，把衣服递给她。她遂披上长袍，模仿修士拉低兜帽，步入宴会厅。 火把照亮了巨桌周遭众人。人人统一遮掩全身的黑袍制服，看起来宴席已终。空落落的大盘、浅碟和玻璃杯堆满木桌，几乎没剩下什么食物。宴会貌似是迅速结束的。有那么一会儿，赫莱娅停了下来想：可怜的、迷路的特莉丝德夫人错失了饱餐一顿的机会。 桌上唯一怪异之物是它的桌心饰品：一个剩余计量一分钟量、正往下渗漏着沙子的硕大黄金沙漏。 尽管每个人看着都很像，不过有些正酣睡，有些彼此聊得火热，还有个在奏鲁特琴。她注意到那是英迪柯的鲁特琴。她还留意到那人指上戴着英迪柯的戒指。赫莱娅突然很感激这隐藏每个人容貌的兜帽，英迪柯也许不会意识到这是她，而她竟犯了这么愚蠢的错误。 “特莉丝德，”那位年轻的黑衣修士对众人引荐，转身望向她鼓掌欢迎。 还清醒的骑士团成员们站起对她行吻手礼并自荐。 “尼尔德拉（nirdla）。” “苏瓦雷克（suelec）。” “凯尔（kyler）。” 名号越发奇怪。 “托尼欧普（toniop）。” “诃提尔特斯（htillyts）。” “诺伊哈塔拉普（noihtarap）。” 她没忍住大笑：“知道知道，都是倒着的。你们其实叫奥德印（aldrin），西里尔斯（celeus），瑞利克（relyk），珀诺特（poinot），斯泰林斯（styllith）和帕拉西翁（parathion）。” “没错，”年轻人说，“您不坐坐？” “嗯哼，”赫莱娅咯咯笑着打开脑中的假面剧开关，坐在某处空位上，“我猜猜，等沙漏漏干净了，那就该把这倒过来的名字正回去了吧？” “如您所言，特莉丝德，”她一侧的女人答，“这只是我们教团的一项小娱乐。这城堡貌似正适合作我们讽刺好笑的盛宴地点。它当初是设计为躲避那些瘟疫患者，用那种话来讲，那些行尸走肉。” 赫莱娅觉得火把的气味让她有点晕头转向，撞上旁边熟睡的男人，让他面朝前趴倒在桌上。 “可怜的埃斯鲁克·特利夫（esruoc tsrif），”邻座男人说着搀扶起他。“他馈赠给我们许多。” 赫莱娅踉踉跄跄地站起来，惶恐不安地向前门走去。 “想去哪呀，特莉丝德？”其中一人问，他的语调中令人不快的嘲笑意味毫无掩饰。 “我不叫特莉丝德，”她嘟囔着，握住英迪柯的手臂。“抱歉，伙计。我们该走了。” 最后点点沙粒漏入底部，而这男人拉下他的兜帽。他不是英迪柯。“他”甚至不是人。这怪人眼中写满饥饿，宽阔拉伸的巨嘴极其滑稽，口内犬牙交错。 赫莱娅跌跌撞撞坐回他们称作“埃斯鲁克·特利夫”那人的椅上。他的兜帽滑下，露出英迪柯苍白而毫无血色的脸。她惊声尖叫时，所有人都扑向她。 在她生命的最后一刹，赫莱娅总算把“特莉丝德”倒着拼了出来。

Surfeit of Thieves by Aniis Noru

[pagebreak] [pagebreak]

"This looks interesting," said Indyk, his eyes narrowing to observe the black caravan making its way to the spires of the secluded castle. A gaudy, alien coat of arms marked each carriage, the lacquer glistening in the light of the moons. "Who do you suppose they are?" "They're obviously well-off," smiled his partner, Heriah. "Perhaps some new Imperial Cult dedicated to the acquisition of wealth?" "Go into town and find out what you can about the castle," said Indyk. "I'll see if I can learn anything about who these strangers are. We meet on this hill tomorrow night." Heriah had two great skills: picking locks and picking information. By dusk of the following day, she had returned to the hill. Indyk joined her an hour later. "The place is called Ald Olyra," she explained. "It dates back to the second era when a collection of nobles built it to protect themselves during one of the epidemics. They didn't want any of the diseased masses to get into their midst and spread the plague, so they built up quite a sophisticated security system for the time. Of course, it's mostly fallen into ruin, but I have a good idea about what kind of locks and traps might still be operational. What did you find out?" "I wasn't nearly so successful," frowned Indyk. "No one seemed to have any idea about the group, even that that there were here. I was about to give up, but at the charterhouse, I met a monk who said that his masters were a hermetic group called the Order of St. Eadnua. I talked to him for some time, this fellow name of Parathion, and it seems they're having some sort of ritual feast tonight." "Are they wealthy?" asked Heriah impatiently. "Embarrassingly so according to the fellow. But they're only at the castle for tonight." "I have my picks on me," winked Heriah. "Opportunity has smiled on us." She drew a diagram of the castle in the dirt: the main hall and kitchen were near the front gate, and the stables and secured armory were in the back. The thieves had a system that never failed. Heriah would find a way into the castle and collect as much loot as possible, while Indyk provided the distraction. He waited until his partner had scaled the wall before rapping on the gate. Perhaps this time he would be a bard, or a lost adventurer. The details were most fun to improvise. Heriah heard Indyk talking to the woman who came to the gate, but she was too far away to hear the words exchanged. He was evidently successful: a moment later, she heard the door shut. The man had charm, she would give him that. Only a few of the traps and locks to the armory had been set. Undoubtedly, many of the keys had been lost in time. Whatever servants had been in charge of securing the Order's treasures had brought a few new locks to affix. It took extra time to maneuver the intricate hasps and bolts of the new traps before proceeding to the old but still working systems, but Heriah found her heart beating with anticipation. Whatever lay beyond the door, she thought, must be of sufficient value to merit such protection. When at last the door swung quietly open, the thief found her avaricious dreams paled to reality. A mountain of golden treasure, ancient relics glimmering with untapped magicka, weaponry of matchless quality, gemstones the size of her fist, row after row of strange potions, and stacks of valuable documents and scrolls. She was so enthralled by the sight, she did not hear the man behind her approach. "You must be Lady Tressed," said the voice and she jumped. It was a monk in a black, hooded robe, intricately woven with silver and gold threads. For a moment, she could not speak. This was the sort of encounter that Indyk loved, but she could think to do nothing but nod her head with what she hoped looked like certainty. "I'm afraid I'm a little lost," she stammered. "I can see that," the man laughed. "That's the armory. I'll show you the way to the dining hall. We were afraid you weren't going to arrive. The feast is nearly over." Heriah followed the monk across the courtyard, to the double doors leading to the dining hall. A robe identical to the one he was wearing hung on a hook outside, and he handed it to her with a knowing smile. She slipped it on. She mimicked him as she lowered the hood over her head and entered the hall. Torches illuminated the figures within around the large table. Each wore the uniform black robe that covered all features, and from the look of things, the feast was over. Empty plates, platters, and glasses filled every inch of the wood with only the faintest spots and dribbles of the food remaining. It was a breaking of a fast it seemed. For a moment, Heriah stopped to think about poor, lost Lady Tressed who had missed her opportunity for gluttony. The only unusual item on the table was its centerpiece: a huge golden hourglass which was on its last minute's worth of sand. Though each person looked alike, some were sleeping, some were chatting merrily to one another, and one was playing a lute. Indyk's lute, she noticed, and then noticed Indyk's ring on the man's finger. Heriah was suddenly grateful for the anonymity of the hood. Perhaps Indyk would not realize that it was she, and that she had blundered. "Tressed," said the young man to the assembled, who turned as one to her and burst into applause. The conscious members of the Order arose to kiss her hand, and introduce themselves. "Nirdla." "Suelec." "Kyler." The names got stranger. "Toniop." "Htillyts." "Noihtarap." She could not help laughing: "I understand. It's all backwards. Your real names are Aldrin, Celeus, Relyk, Poinot, Styllith, Parathion." "Of course," said the young man. "Won't you have a seat?" "Sey," giggled Heriah, getting into the spirit of the masque and taking an empty chair. "I suppose that when the hourglass runs out, the backwards names go back to normal?" "That's correct, Tressed," said the woman next to her. "It's just one of our Order's little amusements. This castle seemed like the appropriately ironic venue for our feast, devised as it was to shun the plague victims who were, in their way, a walking dead." Heriah felt herself light-headed from the odor of the torches, and bumped into the sleeping man next to her. He fell face forward onto the table. "Poor Esruoc Tsrif," said a neighboring man, helping to prop the body up. "He's given us so much." Heriah stumbled to her feet and began walking uncertainly for the front gate. "Where are you going, Tressed?" asked one of the figures, his voice taking on an unpleasant mocking quality. "My name isn't Tressed," she mumbled, gripping Indyk's arm. "I'm sorry, partner. We need to go." The last crumb of sand fell in the hour glass as the man pulled back his hood. It was not Indyk. It was not even human, but a stretched grotesquerie of a man with hungry eyes and a wide mouth filled with tusk-like fangs. Heriah fell back into the chair of the figure they called Esruoc Tsrif. His hood fell open, revealing the pallid, bloodless face of Indyk. As she began to scream, they fell on her. In her last living moment, Heriah finally spelled "Tressed" backwards. 桌上唯一怪异之物是它的桌心饰品：一个剩余计量一分钟量、正往下渗漏着沙子的硕大黄金沙漏。 尽管每个人看着都很像，不过有些正酣睡，有些彼此聊得火热，还有个在奏鲁特琴。她注意到那是英迪柯的鲁特琴。她还留意到那人指上戴着英迪柯的戒指。赫莱娅突然很感激这隐藏每个人容貌的兜帽，英迪柯也许不会意识到这是她，而她竟犯了这么愚蠢的错误。 “特莉丝德，”那位年轻的黑衣修士对众人引荐，转身望向她鼓掌欢迎。 还清醒的骑士团成员们站起对她行吻手礼并自荐。 “尼尔德拉（Nirdla）。” “苏瓦雷克（Suelec）。” “凯尔（Kyler）。” 名号越发奇怪。 “托尼欧普（Toniop）。” “诃提尔特斯（Htillyts）。” “诺伊哈塔拉普（Noihtarap）。” 她没忍住大笑：“知道知道，都是倒着的。你们其实叫奥德印（Aldrin），西里尔斯（Celeus），瑞利克（Relyk），珀诺特（Poinot），斯泰林斯（Styllith）和帕拉西翁（Parathion）。” “没错，”年轻人说，“您不坐坐？” “嗯哼，”赫莱娅咯咯笑着打开脑中的假面剧开关，坐在某处空位上，“我猜猜，等沙漏漏干净了，那就该把这倒过来的名字正回去了吧？” “如您所言，特莉丝德，”她一侧的女人答，“这只是我们教团的一项小娱乐。这城堡貌似正适合作我们讽刺好笑的盛宴地点。它当初是设计为躲避那些瘟疫患者，用那种话来讲，那些行尸走肉。” 赫莱娅觉得火把的气味让她有点晕头转向，撞上旁边熟睡的男人，让他面朝前趴倒在桌上。 “可怜的埃斯鲁克·特利夫（Esruoc Tsrif），”邻座男人说着搀扶起他。“他馈赠给我们许多。” 赫莱娅踉踉跄跄地站起来，惶恐不安地向前门走去。 “想去哪呀，特莉丝德？”其中一人问，他的语调中令人不快的嘲笑意味毫无掩饰。 “我不叫特莉丝德，”她嘟囔着，握住英迪柯的手臂。“抱歉，伙计。我们该走了。” 最后点点沙粒漏入底部，而这男人拉下他的兜帽。他不是英迪柯。“他”甚至不是人。这怪人眼中写满饥饿，宽阔拉伸的巨嘴极其滑稽，口内犬牙交错。 赫莱娅跌跌撞撞坐回他们称作“埃斯鲁克·特利夫”那人的椅上。他的兜帽滑下，露出英迪柯苍白而毫无血色的脸。她惊声尖叫时，所有人都扑向她。 在她生命的最后一刹，赫莱娅总算把“特莉丝德”倒着拼了出来。

english:
[pagebreak]

Velehk Sain Pirate King of the Abecean

Poke out your eyes lad, pour lead in your ears Those sails portend madness, dark horror and fear Abandon your lasses, your ship and your gold Blood on the water, Velehk this way comes A noose from the rigging, a plank from boards Do yourself in, don't try at crossing swords Mercy's not a shipmate among that heartless horde Blood on the water, the Pirate King comes Stout Empire Galleon or Swift Elven Skiff They every one splinter and just as soon sink But only after crew and captain have their fun Blood on the water, your days are done He'll tear your gut and he'll eat your heart raw His eyes gleam red, his heart will never thaw Mark well these words, you quaking babes Blood on the water follows Captain Velehk Sain
chinese:
[pagebreak]

维勒克·赛因 阿贝希恩海盗王

戳穿你的眼睛小伙计，拿铅灌你耳朵里 那些风帆昭示癫狂、黑暗惧怖还有恐慌 抛下你的女孩、你的舰船还有你的钱财 水波粼粼血光所在，维勒克正朝这边来 索具的碎断绳套漂，甲板的断裂木板摇 你自个性命自个了，别妄想交战太看小 那帮家伙是狠心汉，怜悯不跟他们同船 水波粼粼血光所在，海盗之王正索命来 结实的帝国大帆船，灵活的精灵小快艇 哪个都裂成一片片，快快沉底消失不见 可这得等船员船长，玩个爽快过后实现 水波粼粼血光所在，你的时日从此不再 他会撕烂你的胃肠，他会生吞你的心脏 他的双眼闪烁红光，他的心肠永远硬邦 老实记着这些话吧，你等筛糠的小心肝 水波粼粼那血光翻着浪，尾随那维勒克·赛因船长

[pagebreak]

english:
[pagebreak]

Timeline Series - Vol 1 Before the Ages of Man By Aicantar of Shimerene

[pagebreak] [pagebreak]

efore man came to rule Tamriel, and before the chronicles of the historians recorded the affairs of the rulers of Tamriel, the events of our world are known only through myths and legends, and through the divinely inspired teachings of the Nine Divines. For convenience, historians divide the distant ages of prehistory into two broad periods of time -- the Dawn Era, and the Merethic Era. [pagebreak] * The Dawn Era * The Dawn Era is that period before the beginning of mortal time, when the feats of the gods take place. The Dawn Era ends with the exodus of the gods and magic from the World at the founding of the Adamantine Tower. The term 'Merethic' comes from the Nordic, literally, "Era of the Elves." The Merethic Era is the prehistoric time after the exodus of the gods and magic from the World at the founding of the Adamantine Tower and before the arrival of Ysgramor the Nord in Tamriel. The following are the most notable events of the Dawn Era, presented roughly in sequence as it must be understaoo by creatures of time such as ourselves. The Cosmos formed from the Aurbis [chaos, or totality] by Anu and Padomay. Akatosh (Auriel) formed and Time began. The Gods (et'Ada) formed. Lorkhan convinced -- or tricked -- the Gods into creating the mortal plane, Nirn. The mortal plane was at this point highly magical and dangerous. As the Gods walked, the physical make-up of the mortal plane and even the timeless continuity of existence itself became unstable. When Magic (Magnus), architect of the plans for the mortal world, decided to terminate the project, the Gods convened at the Adamantine Tower [Direnni Tower, the oldest known structure in Tamriel] and decided what to do. Most left when Magic did. Others sacrificed themselves into other forms so that they might Stay (the Ehlnofey). Lorkhan was condemned by the Gods to exile in the mortal realms, and his heart was torn out and cast from the Tower. Where it landed, a Volcano formed. With Magic (in the Mythic Sense) gone, the Cosmos stabilized. Elven history, finally linear, began (ME2500). [pagebreak] * The Merethic Era * The Merethic Era was figured by early Nord scholars as a series of years numbered in reverse order backward from the their 'beginning of time' -- the founding of the Camoran Dynasty, recorded as Year Zero of the First Era. The prehistoric events of the Merethic Era are listed here with their traditional Nordic Merethic dates. The earliest Merethic date cited by King Harald's scholars was ME2500 -- the Nordic reckoning of the first year of time. As such, the Merethic Era extends from ME2500 in the distant past to ME1 -- the year before the founding of the Camoran Dysnasty and the establishment of the White Gold Tower as an indepenent city-state. According to King Harald's bards, ME2500 was the date of construction of the Adamantine Tower on Balfiera Island in High Rock, the oldest known structure of Tamriel. (This corresponds roughly to the earliest historical dates given in various unpublished Elvish chronicles.) During the early Merethic Era, the aboriginal beastpeoples of Tamriel -- the ancestors of the Khajiit, Argonian, Orcish, and other beastfolk -- lived in preliterate communities throughout Tamriel. In the Middle Merethic Era, the Aldmeri (mortals of Elven origin) refugees left their doomed and now-lost continent of Aldmeris (also known as 'Old Ehlnofey') and settled in southwestern Tamriel. The first colonies were distributed at wide intervals on islands along the entire coast of Tamriel. Later inland settlements were founded primarily in fertile lowlands in southwest and central Tamriel. Wherever the beastfolk encountered the Elves, the sophisticated, literate, technologically advanced Aldmeri cultures displaced the primitive beastfolk into the jungles, marshes, mountains, and wastelands. The Adamantine Tower was rediscovered and captured by the Direnni, a prominent and powerful Aldmeri clan. The Crystal Tower was built on Summerset Isle and, later, White Gold Tower in Cyrodiil. During the Middle Merethic Era, Aldmeri explorers mapped the coasts of Vvardenfel, building the First Era High Elven wizard towers at Ald Redaynia, Bal Fell, Tel Aruhn, and Tel Mora in Morrowind. It was also during this period that Ayleid [Wild Elven] settlements flourished in the jungles surrounding White Gold Tower (present day Cyrodiil). Wild Elves, also known as the Heartland High Elves, preserved the Dawn Era magics and language of the Ehlnofey. Ostensibly a tribute-land to the High King of Alinor, the Heartland's long lines of communication from the Summerset Isles' sovereignty effectively isolated Cyrodill from the High Kings at Crystal Tower. The Late Middle Merethic Era is the period of the High Velothi Culture. The Chimer, ancestors of the modern Dunmer, or Dark Elves, were dynamic, ambitious, long-lived Elven clans devoted to fundamentalist ancestor worship. The Chimer clans followed the Prophet Veloth out of the ancestral Elven homelands in the southwest to settle in the lands now known as Morrowind. Despising the secular culture and profane practices of the Dwemer, the Chimer also coveted the lands and resources of the Dwemer, and for centuries provoked them with minor raids and territorial disputes. The Dwemer (Dwarves), free-thinking, reclusive Elven clans devoted to the secrets of science, engineering, and alchemy, established underground cities and communities in the mountain range (later the Velothi Mountains) separating modern Skyrim and Morrowind. The Late Merethic Era marks the precipitous decline of Velothi culture. Some Velothi settled in villages near declining and abandoned ancient Velothi towers. During this period, Velothi high culture disappeared on Vvardenfell Island. The earliest Dwemer Freehold colonies date from this period. Degenerate Velothi devolved into tribal cultures which, in time, evolved into the modern Great Houses of Morrowind, or persisted as the barbarian Ashlander tribes. The only surviving traces of this tribal culture are scattered Velothi towers and Ashlander nomads on Vvardenfell Island. The original First Era High Elven wizard towers along the coasts of Tamriel were also abandoned about this time. It was in the Late Merethic Era that the pre-literate humans, the so-called "Nedic Peoples", from the continent of Atmora (also 'Altmora' or 'the Elder Wood' in Aldmeris) migrated and settleed in northern Tamriel. The Nord culture hero Ysgramor, leader of a great colonizing fleet to Tamriel, is credited with developing a runic transcription of Nord speech based on Elvish principles, and so Ysgramor is considered the first human historian. Ysgramor's fleet landed at Hsaarik Head at the extreme northern tip of Skyrim's Broken Cape. The Nords built there the legendary city of Saarthal. The Elves drove the Men away during the Night of Tears, but Ysgramor soon returned with his Five Hundred Companions. Also during the Late Merethic Era the legendary immortal hero, warrior, sorceror, and king variously known as Pelinal Whitestrake, Harrald Hairy Breeks, Ysmir, Hans the Fox, etc., wandered Tamriel, gathering armies, conquering lands, ruling, then abandoning his kingdoms to wander again.

[pagebreak]

时间线系列–卷一 人类时代之前 微光城的爱坎塔 著

[pagebreak] [pagebreak]

在人类统治泰姆瑞尔前，在史家们的编年史记录泰姆瑞尔统治者们的事务前，我们世界上的各大事件只有通过神话和传奇，以及通过九圣灵的神圣启示教诲方可知晓。 为便利起见，史家们将遥远的史前时代断作两纪——黎明纪元、精灵纪元。 [pagebreak] * 黎明纪元 * 黎明纪元是在凡人时代发端前的时期，彼时充斥着众神的神迹壮举。黎明纪元止于众神与魔法从这世界脱离，和精金塔的落成。 术语“莫的”源出于诺德。其字面意义为“精灵们的纪元”。精灵纪元指众神与魔法从这世界脱离和精金塔落成后，诺德的伊斯格拉默抵达泰姆瑞尔前的史前时期。 接下来是在黎明纪元所发生最值得注意的大事件，大体依照能被如我们这样的时间性生命所理解的先后次序来呈现。 在阿努和帕多梅作用下，奥比斯[混沌，或整体的一]中开辟形成了宇宙。阿卡托什（奥瑞艾尔）先凝形，则时光始流，诸神（艾'阿达）陆续凝形。洛克汗说服（或欺瞒）诸神投身于凡世位面奈恩中创世。彼时的凡世位面充斥着高度魔法而又危机四伏。当众神行走其间时，人间的一切物质构成，甚至连存在的无终连续性本身都变得岌岌可危。 凡世计划的建筑师魔法（玛格努斯），决定终止这项宏图时，众神群聚于精金塔[又称迪伦尼塔，泰姆瑞尔已知最古老的建筑]商讨该如何是好。当魔法逃脱时，绝大多数神灵亦随之而去。其余者牺牲自己化为其他形态，以便他们能在凡世强而有力地守留（伊诺菲）。洛克汗被诸神定罪，放逐到凡世各领域，心脏被掏出，自塔上抛射出去。其落地处轰出了一座火山。待（神话意义上的）魔法逝去后，宇宙趋于稳定。精灵的历史就此开始，最终实现了线性化（精灵纪元2500年）。 [pagebreak] * 精灵纪元 * 早期诺德学者们将精灵纪元定义为一系列从他们的“时间的起始”节点倒序地排列的年代：卡莫然王朝的创立被载为第一纪元零年。列于下文的精灵纪元史前事件均以他们的传统诺德精灵纪元日期表示。哈拉尔德王的学者所援引的最早精灵纪元日期是精灵纪元2500年——这便是诺德人对线性时间元年的估算。因此，精灵纪元自上古遥远的精灵纪元2500年起延伸到精灵纪元元年，即卡莫然王朝建立、白金塔作为独立城邦建立的前一年。 根据哈拉尔德王的吟游诗人所述，精灵纪元2500年正是全泰姆瑞尔已知最古老的建筑精金塔在高岩的巴尔菲拉岛上建成的年份。（这一点和各种各样的未公开发表精灵编年史中所给出的最早历史日期大致吻合。） 精灵纪元早期，泰姆瑞尔的兽民土著——卡吉特、阿尔贡、兽人和一些其他兽族的祖先——生活在遍布泰姆瑞尔各地的无文字社群中。 精灵纪元中期，奥德莫难民们（精灵凡人的起源）离开了他们那命定毁灭、今已消失的奥德莫里斯大陆（或称“古伊诺菲”），定居泰姆瑞尔大陆西南陲。首批殖民地散落分布在泰姆瑞尔整片海岸线的各岛屿上。后来的内陆定居点主要建立在泰姆瑞尔的西南和中部的丰饶低地处。无论是何处的兽民遭遇精灵们，文明开化、知书达礼、技术先进的奥德莫文化就会取代原始粗鄙的兽民并将之赶到丛林、沼泽、山脉和荒原中去。显赫强大的奥德莫氏族迪伦尼重新发现了精金塔，并将之据为己有。此外，夏暮岛上树起水晶塔，而随后，希罗帝尔建成了白金塔。 精灵纪元中叶期间，奥德莫探险者们绘成瓦登费尔的海岸线，并在晨风建立了数座第一纪元的高精灵巫师塔：奥德·瑞戴尼亚，巴尔·费尔，泰尔·阿鲁恩以及泰尔·莫拉。同样在此期间，亚历德[野精灵]定居点在环绕白金塔的丛林中蓬勃发展（即如今的希罗帝尔）。野精灵，又称“腹地高精灵”，完整保留了黎明纪元使用的伊诺菲魔法和语言。只看其表，他们仍是艾琳诺至高王的朝贡分支，但实际上自夏暮岛王权导向腹地那漫长的通信线路，已将希罗帝尔和远在水晶塔中的至高王相隔开来。 精灵纪元中叶晚期，是高度维洛锡文化的时期。现代丹莫或暗精灵的祖先凯莫，是一群积极活跃、雄心勃勃且寿命悠长的精灵氏族，致力于原教旨主义祖先信仰。凯莫氏族追随先知维洛斯离开西南陲祖祖辈辈的精灵故乡，迁徙到今天的晨风定居。凯莫既鄙视着锻莫的世俗文化和渎神习俗，却又觊觎着他们的土地和资源，数世纪以来通过一些小规模掠夺和领土争端不断地挑衅后者。锻莫（矮人），是一群思想自由，深居简出的精灵氏族，致力于研究科学、工程学、炼金学的奥秘，在分隔天际和今晨风的山区（日后的维洛锡山脉）开凿出了众多的地下城邦和社区。 精灵纪元晚期标志着维洛锡文化的骤然衰落。一些维洛锡来到废弃的古维洛锡塔附近的村庄定居。在此期间，维洛锡高等文化在瓦登费尔岛上消隐无踪。最早的锻莫自由保有殖民地始建于此时。退化没落的维洛斯文化逐渐演变为部落文化，随着时间推移逐渐发展成现代晨风的大家族，或是持久化作原始野蛮的灰地民部落。该部落文化的仅存痕迹，为零落在维洛斯塔附近和瓦登费尔岛上的灰地诸游牧民族。而最初的那些海岸线上的第一纪元高精灵巫师塔也在此时被一同遗弃。 精灵纪元的末尾，被称作“尼德人”的原始社会人类自阿特莫拉大陆（奥德莫语称“奥尔特莫拉”或又称“上古森林”）迁往泰姆瑞尔北境定居。诺德文化的英雄伊斯格拉默，领导着前往泰姆瑞尔的庞大殖民舰队，被认为基于精灵语语法创造出诺德语的符文转录，因此伊斯格拉默又被视为首位人类史家。其舰队在天际碎裂海角极北端的萨黎柯岬登陆。诺德人在彼处建成了传奇之城萨瑟尔。在泪之夜期间，精灵将人类驱逐而去。但伊斯格拉默不久便率其五百勇士卷土重来。 同样在精灵纪元末期时，一位传奇不朽英雄、战士、术士和君王（被各种称呼为“白轮”佩林纳尔，“绒马裤”哈拉尔德，伊斯米尔和“狡狐”汉斯等等）浪迹于泰姆瑞尔。他征召大军，征服疆土，实行统治，随后又遗弃了他的无数王国，再度云游四方。

timeline series - vol 1 before the ages of man by aicantar of shimerene

[pagebreak] [pagebreak]

efore man came to rule tamriel, and before the chronicles of the historians recorded the affairs of the rulers of tamriel, the events of our world are known only through myths and legends, and through the divinely inspired teachings of the nine divines. for convenience, historians divide the distant ages of prehistory into two broad periods of time -- the dawn era, and the merethic era. [pagebreak] * the dawn era * the dawn era is that period before the beginning of mortal time, when the feats of the gods take place. the dawn era ends with the exodus of the gods and magic from the world at the founding of the adamantine tower. the term 'merethic' comes from the nordic, literally, "era of the elves." the merethic era is the prehistoric time after the exodus of the gods and magic from the world at the founding of the adamantine tower and before the arrival of ysgramor the nord in tamriel. the following are the most notable events of the dawn era, presented roughly in sequence as it must be understaoo by creatures of time such as ourselves. the cosmos formed from the aurbis [chaos, or totality] by anu and padomay. akatosh (auriel) formed and time began. the gods (et'ada) formed. lorkhan convinced -- or tricked -- the gods into creating the mortal plane, nirn. the mortal plane was at this point highly magical and dangerous. as the gods walked, the physical make-up of the mortal plane and even the timeless continuity of existence itself became unstable. when magic (magnus), architect of the plans for the mortal world, decided to terminate the project, the gods convened at the adamantine tower [direnni tower, the oldest known structure in tamriel] and decided what to do. most left when magic did. others sacrificed themselves into other forms so that they might stay (the ehlnofey). lorkhan was condemned by the gods to exile in the mortal realms, and his heart was torn out and cast from the tower. where it landed, a volcano formed. with magic (in the mythic sense) gone, the cosmos stabilized. elven history, finally linear, began (me2500). [pagebreak] * the merethic era * the merethic era was figured by early nord scholars as a series of years numbered in reverse order backward from the their 'beginning of time' -- the founding of the camoran dynasty, recorded as year zero of the first era. the prehistoric events of the merethic era are listed here with their traditional nordic merethic dates. the earliest merethic date cited by king harald's scholars was me2500 -- the nordic reckoning of the first year of time. as such, the merethic era extends from me2500 in the distant past to me1 -- the year before the founding of the camoran dysnasty and the establishment of the white gold tower as an indepenent city-state. according to king harald's bards, me2500 was the date of construction of the adamantine tower on balfiera island in high rock, the oldest known structure of tamriel. (this corresponds roughly to the earliest historical dates given in various unpublished elvish chronicles.) during the early merethic era, the aboriginal beastpeoples of tamriel -- the ancestors of the khajiit, argonian, orcish, and other beastfolk -- lived in preliterate communities throughout tamriel. in the middle merethic era, the aldmeri (mortals of elven origin) refugees left their doomed and now-lost continent of aldmeris (also known as 'old ehlnofey') and settled in southwestern tamriel. the first colonies were distributed at wide intervals on islands along the entire coast of tamriel. later inland settlements were founded primarily in fertile lowlands in southwest and central tamriel. wherever the beastfolk encountered the elves, the sophisticated, literate, technologically advanced aldmeri cultures displaced the primitive beastfolk into the jungles, marshes, mountains, and wastelands. the adamantine tower was rediscovered and captured by the direnni, a prominent and powerful aldmeri clan. the crystal tower was built on summerset isle and, later, white gold tower in cyrodiil. during the middle merethic era, aldmeri explorers mapped the coasts of vvardenfel, building the first era high elven wizard towers at ald redaynia, bal fell, tel aruhn, and tel mora in morrowind. it was also during this period that ayleid [wild elven] settlements flourished in the jungles surrounding white gold tower (present day cyrodiil). wild elves, also known as the heartland high elves, preserved the dawn era magics and language of the ehlnofey. ostensibly a tribute-land to the high king of alinor, the heartland's long lines of communication from the summerset isles' sovereignty effectively isolated cyrodill from the high kings at crystal tower. the late middle merethic era is the period of the high velothi culture. the chimer, ancestors of the modern dunmer, or dark elves, were dynamic, ambitious, long-lived elven clans devoted to fundamentalist ancestor worship. the chimer clans followed the prophet veloth out of the ancestral elven homelands in the southwest to settle in the lands now known as morrowind. despising the secular culture and profane practices of the dwemer, the chimer also coveted the lands and resources of the dwemer, and for centuries provoked them with minor raids and territorial disputes. the dwemer (dwarves), free-thinking, reclusive elven clans devoted to the secrets of science, engineering, and alchemy, established underground cities and communities in the mountain range (later the velothi mountains) separating modern skyrim and morrowind. the late merethic era marks the precipitous decline of velothi culture. some velothi settled in villages near declining and abandoned ancient velothi towers. during this period, velothi high culture disappeared on vvardenfell island. the earliest dwemer freehold colonies date from this period. degenerate velothi devolved into tribal cultures which, in time, evolved into the modern great houses of morrowind, or persisted as the barbarian ashlander tribes. the only surviving traces of this tribal culture are scattered velothi towers and ashlander nomads on vvardenfell island. the original first era high elven wizard towers along the coasts of tamriel were also abandoned about this time. it was in the late merethic era that the pre-literate humans, the so-called "nedic peoples", from the continent of atmora (also 'altmora' or 'the elder wood' in aldmeris) migrated and settleed in northern tamriel. the nord culture hero ysgramor, leader of a great colonizing fleet to tamriel, is credited with developing a runic transcription of nord speech based on elvish principles, and so ysgramor is considered the first human historian. ysgramor's fleet landed at hsaarik head at the extreme northern tip of skyrim's broken cape. the nords built there the legendary city of saarthal. the elves drove the men away during the night of tears, but ysgramor soon returned with his five hundred companions. also during the late merethic era the legendary immortal hero, warrior, sorceror, and king variously known as pelinal whitestrake, harrald hairy breeks, ysmir, hans the fox, etc., wandered tamriel, gathering armies, conquering lands, ruling, then abandoning his kingdoms to wander again.

精灵纪元中叶晚期，是高度维洛锡文化的时期。现代丹莫或暗精灵的祖先凯莫，是一群积极活跃、雄心勃勃且寿命悠长的精灵氏族，致力于原教旨主义祖先信仰。凯莫氏族追随先知维洛斯离开西南陲祖祖辈辈的精灵故乡，迁徙到今天的晨风定居。凯莫既鄙视着锻莫的世俗文化和渎神习俗，却又觊觎着他们的土地和资源，数世纪以来通过一些小规模掠夺和领土争端不断地挑衅后者。锻莫（矮人），是一群思想自由，深居简出的精灵氏族，致力于研究科学、工程学、炼金学的奥秘，在分隔天际和今晨风的山区（日后的维洛锡山脉）开凿出了众多的地下城邦和社区。 精灵纪元晚期标志着维洛锡文化的骤然衰落。一些维洛锡来到废弃的古维洛锡塔附近的村庄定居。在此期间，维洛锡高等文化在瓦登费尔岛上消隐无踪。最早的锻莫自由保有殖民地始建于此时。退化没落的维洛斯文化逐渐演变为部落文化，随着时间推移逐渐发展成现代晨风的大家族，或是持久化作原始野蛮的灰地民部落。该部落文化的仅存痕迹，为零落在维洛斯塔附近和瓦登费尔岛上的灰地诸游牧民族。而最初的那些海岸线上的第一纪元高精灵巫师塔也在此时被一同遗弃。 精灵纪元的末尾，被称作“尼德人”的原始社会人类自阿特莫拉大陆（奥德莫语称“奥尔特莫拉”或又称“上古森林”）迁往泰姆瑞尔北境定居。诺德文化的英雄伊斯格拉默，领导着前往泰姆瑞尔的庞大殖民舰队，被认为基于精灵语语法创造出诺德语的符文转录，因此伊斯格拉默又被视为首位人类史家。其舰队在天际碎裂海角极北端的萨黎柯岬登陆。诺德人在彼处建成了传奇之城萨瑟尔。在泪之夜期间，精灵将人类驱逐而去。但伊斯格拉默不久便率其五百勇士卷土重来。 同样在精灵纪元末期时，一位传奇不朽英雄、战士、术士和君王（被各种称呼为“白轮”佩林纳尔，“绒马裤”哈拉尔德，伊斯米尔和“狡狐”汉斯等等）浪迹于泰姆瑞尔。他征召大军，征服疆土，实行统治，随后又遗弃了他的无数王国，再度云游四方。

Addon: Skyrim EDID: SkillIllusion4 Record: BOOK:DESC SID: 0050C3 English: [pagebreak]

Timeline Series - Vol 1 Before the Ages of Man By Aicantar of Shimerene

[pagebreak] [pagebreak]

efore man came to rule Tamriel, and before the chronicles of the historians recorded the affairs of the rulers of Tamriel, the events of our world are known only through myths and legends, and through the divinely inspired teachings of the Nine Divines. For convenience, historians divide the distant ages of prehistory into two broad periods of time -- the Dawn Era, and the Merethic Era. [pagebreak] * The Dawn Era * The Dawn Era is that period before the beginning of mortal time, when the feats of the gods take place. The Dawn Era ends with the exodus of the gods and magic from the World at the founding of the Adamantine Tower. The term 'Merethic' comes from the Nordic, literally, "Era of the Elves." The Merethic Era is the prehistoric time after the exodus of the gods and magic from the World at the founding of the Adamantine Tower and before the arrival of Ysgramor the Nord in Tamriel. The following are the most notable events of the Dawn Era, presented roughly in sequence as it must be understaoo by creatures of time such as ourselves. The Cosmos formed from the Aurbis [chaos, or totality] by Anu and Padomay. Akatosh (Auriel) formed and Time began. The Gods (et'Ada) formed. Lorkhan convinced -- or tricked -- the Gods into creating the mortal plane, Nirn. The mortal plane was at this point highly magical and dangerous. As the Gods walked, the physical make-up of the mortal plane and even the timeless continuity of existence itself became unstable. When Magic (Magnus), architect of the plans for the mortal world, decided to terminate the project, the Gods convened at the Adamantine Tower [Direnni Tower, the oldest known structure in Tamriel] and decided what to do. Most left when Magic did. Others sacrificed themselves into other forms so that they might Stay (the Ehlnofey). Lorkhan was condemned by the Gods to exile in the mortal realms, and his heart was torn out and cast from the Tower. Where it landed, a Volcano formed. With Magic (in the Mythic Sense) gone, the Cosmos stabilized. Elven history, finally linear, began (ME2500). [pagebreak] * The Merethic Era * The Merethic Era was figured by early Nord scholars as a series of years numbered in reverse order backward from the their 'beginning of time' -- the founding of the Camoran Dynasty, recorded as Year Zero of the First Era. The prehistoric events of the Merethic Era are listed here with their traditional Nordic Merethic dates. The earliest Merethic date cited by King Harald's scholars was ME2500 -- the Nordic reckoning of the first year of time. As such, the Merethic Era extends from ME2500 in the distant past to ME1 -- the year before the founding of the Camoran Dysnasty and the establishment of the White Gold Tower as an indepenent city-state. According to King Harald's bards, ME2500 was the date of construction of the Adamantine Tower on Balfiera Island in High Rock, the oldest known structure of Tamriel. (This corresponds roughly to the earliest historical dates given in various unpublished Elvish chronicles.) During the early Merethic Era, the aboriginal beastpeoples of Tamriel -- the ancestors of the Khajiit, Argonian, Orcish, and other beastfolk -- lived in preliterate communities throughout Tamriel. In the Middle Merethic Era, the Aldmeri (mortals of Elven origin) refugees left their doomed and now-lost continent of Aldmeris (also known as 'Old Ehlnofey') and settled in southwestern Tamriel. The first colonies were distributed at wide intervals on islands along the entire coast of Tamriel. Later inland settlements were founded primarily in fertile lowlands in southwest and central Tamriel. Wherever the beastfolk encountered the Elves, the sophisticated, literate, technologically advanced Aldmeri cultures displaced the primitive beastfolk into the jungles, marshes, mountains, and wastelands. The Adamantine Tower was rediscovered and captured by the Direnni, a prominent and powerful Aldmeri clan. The Crystal Tower was built on Summerset Isle and, later, White Gold Tower in Cyrodiil. During the Middle Merethic Era, Aldmeri explorers mapped the coasts of Vvardenfel, building the First Era High Elven wizard towers at Ald Redaynia, Bal Fell, Tel Aruhn, and Tel Mora in Morrowind. It was also during this period that Ayleid [Wild Elven] settlements flourished in the jungles surrounding White Gold Tower (present day Cyrodiil). Wild Elves, also known as the Heartland High Elves, preserved the Dawn Era magics and language of the Ehlnofey. Ostensibly a tribute-land to the High King of Alinor, the Heartland's long lines of communication from the Summerset Isles' sovereignty effectively isolated Cyrodill from the High Kings at Crystal Tower. The Late Middle Merethic Era is the period of the High Velothi Culture. The Chimer, ancestors of the modern Dunmer, or Dark Elves, were dynamic, ambitious, long-lived Elven clans devoted to fundamentalist ancestor worship. The Chimer clans followed the Prophet Veloth out of the ancestral Elven homelands in the southwest to settle in the lands now known as Morrowind. Despising the secular culture and profane practices of the Dwemer, the Chimer also coveted the lands and resources of the Dwemer, and for centuries provoked them with minor raids and territorial disputes. The Dwemer (Dwarves), free-thinking, reclusive Elven clans devoted to the secrets of science, engineering, and alchemy, established underground cities and communities in the mountain range (later the Velothi Mountains) separating modern Skyrim and Morrowind. The Late Merethic Era marks the precipitous decline of Velothi culture. Some Velothi settled in villages near declining and abandoned ancient Velothi towers. During this period, Velothi high culture disappeared on Vvardenfell Island. The earliest Dwemer Freehold colonies date from this period. Degenerate Velothi devolved into tribal cultures which, in time, evolved into the modern Great Houses of Morrowind, or persisted as the barbarian Ashlander tribes. The only surviving traces of this tribal culture are scattered Velothi towers and Ashlander nomads on Vvardenfell Island. The original First Era High Elven wizard towers along the coasts of Tamriel were also abandoned about this time. It was in the Late Merethic Era that the pre-literate humans, the so-called "Nedic Peoples", from the continent of Atmora (also 'Altmora' or 'the Elder Wood' in Aldmeris) migrated and settleed in northern Tamriel. The Nord culture hero Ysgramor, leader of a great colonizing fleet to Tamriel, is credited with developing a runic transcription of Nord speech based on Elvish principles, and so Ysgramor is considered the first human historian. Ysgramor's fleet landed at Hsaarik Head at the extreme northern tip of Skyrim's Broken Cape. The Nords built there the legendary city of Saarthal. The Elves drove the Men away during the Night of Tears, but Ysgramor soon returned with his Five Hundred Companions. Also during the Late Merethic Era the legendary immortal hero, warrior, sorceror, and king variously known as Pelinal Whitestrake, Harrald Hairy Breeks, Ysmir, Hans the Fox, etc., wandered Tamriel, gathering armies, conquering lands, ruling, then abandoning his kingdoms to wander again. 时间线系列–卷一 人类时代之前 微光城的爱坎塔 著

[pagebreak] [pagebreak]

在人类统治泰姆瑞尔前，在史家们的编年史记录泰姆瑞尔统治者们的事务前，我们世界上的各大事件只有通过神话和传奇，以及通过九圣灵的神圣启示教诲方可知晓。 为便利起见，史家们将遥远的史前时代断作两纪——黎明纪元、精灵纪元。 [pagebreak] * 黎明纪元 * 黎明纪元是在凡人时代发端前的时期，彼时充斥着众神的神迹壮举。黎明纪元止于众神与魔法从这世界脱离，和精金塔的落成。 术语“莫的”源出于诺德。其字面意义为“精灵们的纪元”。精灵纪元指众神与魔法从这世界脱离和精金塔落成后，诺德的伊斯格拉默抵达泰姆瑞尔前的史前时期。 接下来是在黎明纪元所发生最值得注意的大事件，大体依照能被如我们这样的时间性生命所理解的先后次序来呈现。 在阿努和帕多梅作用下，奥比斯[混沌，或整体的一]中开辟形成了宇宙。阿卡托什（奥瑞艾尔）先凝形，则时光始流，诸神（艾'阿达）陆续凝形。洛克汗说服（或欺瞒）诸神投身于凡世位面奈恩中创世。彼时的凡世位面充斥着高度魔法而又危机四伏。当众神行走其间时，人间的一切物质构成，甚至连存在的无终连续性本身都变得岌岌可危。 凡世计划的建筑师魔法（玛格努斯），决定终止这项宏图时，众神群聚于精金塔[又称迪伦尼塔，泰姆瑞尔已知最古老的建筑]商讨该如何是好。当魔法逃脱时，绝大多数神灵亦随之而去。其余者牺牲自己化为其他形态，以便他们能在凡世强而有力地守留（伊诺菲）。洛克汗被诸神定罪，放逐到凡世各领域，心脏被掏出，自塔上抛射出去。其落地处轰出了一座火山。待（神话意义上的）魔法逝去后，宇宙趋于稳定。精灵的历史就此开始，最终实现了线性化（精灵纪元2500年）。 [pagebreak] * 精灵纪元 * 早期诺德学者们将精灵纪元定义为一系列从他们的“时间的起始”节点倒序地排列的年代：卡莫然王朝的创立被载为第一纪元零年。列于下文的精灵纪元史前事件均以他们的传统诺德精灵纪元日期表示。哈拉尔德王的学者所援引的最早精灵纪元日期是精灵纪元2500年——这便是诺德人对线性时间元年的估算。因此，精灵纪元自上古遥远的精灵纪元2500年起延伸到精灵纪元元年，即卡莫然王朝建立、白金塔作为独立城邦建立的前一年。 根据哈拉尔德王的吟游诗人所述，精灵纪元2500年正是全泰姆瑞尔已知最古老的建筑精金塔在高岩的巴尔菲拉岛上建成的年份。（这一点和各种各样的未公开发表精灵编年史中所给出的最早历史日期大致吻合。） 精灵纪元早期，泰姆瑞尔的兽民土著——卡吉特、阿尔贡、兽人和一些其他兽族的祖先——生活在遍布泰姆瑞尔各地的无文字社群中。 精灵纪元中期，奥德莫难民们（精灵凡人的起源）离开了他们那命定毁灭、今已消失的奥德莫里斯大陆（或称“古伊诺菲”），定居泰姆瑞尔大陆西南陲。首批殖民地散落分布在泰姆瑞尔整片海岸线的各岛屿上。后来的内陆定居点主要建立在泰姆瑞尔的西南和中部的丰饶低地处。无论是何处的兽民遭遇精灵们，文明开化、知书达礼、技术先进的奥德莫文化就会取代原始粗鄙的兽民并将之赶到丛林、沼泽、山脉和荒原中去。显赫强大的奥德莫氏族迪伦尼重新发现了精金塔，并将之据为己有。此外，夏暮岛上树起水晶塔，而随后，希罗帝尔建成了白金塔。 english: Two hero-hearts Two hidden keys One fallen Priest Who lies beneath Hail Sarek, Hail Torsten Raise them in your songs Who tricked mad-king Otar and rescued Ragnvald for all Otar! once our chieftain Glorious in battle Fair in judgment Those days ended in pain Dark voices whispered into Otar's mind nobility shouted down Once good now craven Priest Fair folk of Ragnvald Good Nords and True Broken by his will our city eaten alive Send Sarek! Send Torsten! Sure spirits swords that don't fail Otar was bent back but never destroyed Bound instead, contained Two Heroes, Two Keys
chinese: 两位英雄之心， 两枚隐藏之钥， 一位堕落的祭司安葬此处。 赞美萨瑞克，赞美托斯滕， 用尔等歌声颂扬吧， 他们是欺骗疯王奥塔， 拯救拉根沃德之人。 我们曾经的领袖奥塔啊！ 作战英勇， 赋均讼平， 那往昔已在痛苦中结束。 黑暗的低语， 侵袭了奥塔的思维， 高贵的声音亦被击垮， 曾经的贤师变成了今日的懦夫。 拉根沃德的大好族人， 真挚善良的诺德， 被他的意志摧毁， 我们的城市被他生吞活剥。 出发吧萨瑞克！ 出发吧托斯滕！ 坚定的精神， 不败的利剑。 奥塔屈服， 却未被消灭， 而是被封印在内， 两位英雄，两把钥匙。
englishNormalized: two hero-hearts two hidden keys one fallen priest who lies beneath hail sarek, hail torsten raise them in your songs who tricked mad-king otar and rescued ragnvald for all otar! once our chieftain glorious in battle fair in judgment those days ended in pain dark voices whispered into otar's mind nobility shouted down once good now craven priest fair folk of ragnvald good nords and true broken by his will our city eaten alive send sarek! send torsten! sure spirits swords that don't fail otar was bent back but never destroyed bound instead, contained two heroes, two keys
chineseNormalized: 两位英雄之心， 两枚隐藏之钥， 一位堕落的祭司安葬此处。 赞美萨瑞克，赞美托斯滕， 用尔等歌声颂扬吧， 他们是欺骗疯王奥塔， 拯救拉根沃德之人。 我们曾经的领袖奥塔啊！ 作战英勇， 赋均讼平， 那往昔已在痛苦中结束。 黑暗的低语， 侵袭了奥塔的思维， 高贵的声音亦被击垮， 曾经的贤师变成了今日的懦夫。 拉根沃德的大好族人， 真挚善良的诺德， 被他的意志摧毁， 我们的城市被他生吞活剥。 出发吧萨瑞克！ 出发吧托斯滕！ 坚定的精神， 不败的利剑。 奥塔屈服， 却未被消灭， 而是被封印在内， 两位英雄，两把钥匙。

English: Two hero-hearts Two hidden keys One fallen Priest Who lies beneath Hail Sarek, Hail Torsten Raise them in your songs Who tricked mad-king Otar and rescued Ragnvald for all Otar! once our chieftain Glorious in battle Fair in judgment Those days ended in pain Dark voices whispered into Otar's mind nobility shouted down Once good now craven Priest Fair folk of Ragnvald Good Nords and True Broken by his will our city eaten alive Send Sarek! Send Torsten! Sure spirits swords that don't fail Otar was bent back but never destroyed Bound instead, contained Two Heroes, Two Keys
Chinese: 两位英雄之心， 两枚隐藏之钥， 一位堕落的祭司安葬此处。 赞美萨瑞克，赞美托斯滕， 用尔等歌声颂扬吧， 他们是欺骗疯王奥塔， 拯救拉根沃德之人。 我们曾经的领袖奥塔啊！ 作战英勇， 赋均讼平， 那往昔已在痛苦中结束。 黑暗的低语， 侵袭了奥塔的思维， 高贵的声音亦被击垮， 曾经的贤师变成了今日的懦夫。 拉根沃德的大好族人， 真挚善良的诺德， 被他的意志摧毁， 我们的城市被他生吞活剥。 出发吧萨瑞克！ 出发吧托斯滕！ 坚定的精神， 不败的利剑。 奥塔屈服， 却未被消灭， 而是被封印在内， 两位英雄，两把钥匙。
